translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bar a wʊ pɔɔ, ka ala ba ɩ sɛ̃fʊ jũu tɩ kul pɔɔ yuo, wʊ sɔ̃ɔn a kul taa.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ a wiilfʊ nyã ka a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ a ba bɔ̃ɔ a par tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ waan na bibiir ɩka wʊ dɔɔl ba a wʊ nũu na wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba. Tɩchɛ a poturbo mɩɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na waan ba.

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: ) na fʊ mɩ lɛ nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ mãɛ kaar.” (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Ata

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Wʊ dɔɔl ban a wʊ nuur sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba, tɩ chen.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr, “Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ sʊrɔ ma bon 'lɔ na ɩ vɩla? Nɩbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ ɩ nɩbie. Ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka fʊ nyɛ nyɛvʊr turo a nɛbinsɩ.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Yɩ bar a bibiir sɔr ka ba wa a n sɛ̃, yɩ ta pii ba sɔr ɛ, a banyã taaba lɛ so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ na de a nie na wa ben puor, tɩchɛ ka a puor dẽme nɩ wa de a nie.

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Ka a daba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “N tu naa anyãna a jaa, bo lɛ chɛ ma?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka fʊ ɩ chɛchɛ, tɩ de a fʊ bʊ̃tarsɩ a jaa da, na fʊ de a libie kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme, fʊn nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ tɩ na fʊ wa turo ma.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: A daba na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ chiin naa pʊsɔ̃ɔ bojũu wʊ tara na bʊ̃tarsɩ.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ yɩ, an ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ tara sʊɔ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ go, an ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ ka nyɔɔma na kpɛ kparpĩi bɔɔ pɔ gar tara sʊɔ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: A poturbo na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, nɛɛ 'maa ba naa ka ba sʊrɔ, “An lɛ na pãa nyɛ faafʊ?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Ka Yesu kaa ba tɩ 'yɛr, “Ala na ɩ tuo nɩrɛ sɛ̃, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ yele jaa ba ɩ tuo ɛ.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
translation_verse: A Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Nyɛ sɩn bar bonsɩ a jaa tɩ wa turo fʊ? Bo sãyar lɛ sɩn nyɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a bonsɩ a jaa bʊ̃paalaa lɩɛbfʊ daar, a maa Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ naa a tɩɩr nãalʊ̃ʊ dakɔɔ ju. Ka a yɩn bala mɩ na turo ma mɩ jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ pie nɩ ayi ju, tɔɔrɔ a Isɩral bala pie nɩ ayi 'yɛr.

english_verse: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ nyã jaa na bar a wʊ yir bɩɩ yɛɛr bɩɩ yɛpɔbɔ, bɩɩ sãa, bɩɩ ma, bɩɩ bibiir, bɩɩ puo a maa jũu na nyɛn dɔɔltaa gbɛɛ kɔbaa tɩ tara nyɛvʊr na ba tara baar fʊ ɛ.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: “Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛntãan fɩka daba kɔ̃ɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ŋmaa ir bibio jaa pipi yi ɩka wʊ tɩ bɔ nɩbɛ ka ba chen tɩ kɔ wʊ puo pɔ.

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: Wʊ sɔɔ naa ɩka wʊn yab ba naa a lɛ ban na de a bɩbir gbul ka wʊ tara ba chiin a wʊ puo pɔ.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: “A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ lɛ wa do bãlãa wʊ yɩn tɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na ara a daa pɔ na baa maala bon jaa ɛ.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yɩ mɩ chen a n puo pɔ tɩ ɩn yab yɩn naa a lɛ jaa na sɛɛn.’

english_verse: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Ka ba sɔɔ chen. Tɩ wʊ lɛ yɩ go a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa vɩrʊ̃ gar ju sãlsɔɔ nɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa suro a tɩɩr pɔ na wʊ tɩ bɔ a nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: A jãanɔ'maar sɔ̃ɔ, wʊ lɛ yin tɩ nyɛ bamɩne mɩ na chɛnɛ jɛ̃ pɔrɔ jaa. Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, ‘Bo lɛ so a dɩna yɩn jãnɛ ba maala bon jaa ɛ?’

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: A ba 'yɛr, ‘Nɩrɛ jaa ba sʊɔr sɩ kɔb ɛ.’ Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yɩ wa a sɩ chen a n puo pɔ a yɩ tɩ kɔ.’

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: “Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, a puo sʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaara a wʊ puo, ‘Bʊɔl a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ kʊ ba a ba libie, de nie piel a bala na bɩɛr wa tɩ tãn a bala na dɔ̃ɔ nie wa.’

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: “A bala na wa bɩɛr wa ãsʊɔ jaa den a bɩbir gbul libir.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ban wa de a lɛ baar, ba vʊ̃n nɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a puo sʊɔ,

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: na ba gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a puo sʊɔ 'yɛrɛ, ‘A nɩbɛ banyãna wa bɩɛr wa tɩ fʊ yab sɩ jãtaa, nɩ sɩn na ŋmaa ir nɩ ɔɔr wa tʊ̃ lɛ̃ɛɛ ka mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ŋme sɩ tɩ mʊrʊ̃.’

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: “Ka wʊ lɩɛb sʊʊr a ba ãsʊɔ, ‘N badaba, n ba ɩ fʊ dɛɛr ɛ, a ba ɩ yɩn lɛ sɔɔ ɩka yɩn den a bɩbir gbul libir ɛ?

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: De a fʊ libir chiine. Maa lɛ nʊ̃ naa kʊ a puor dẽme nɩtãa a lɛ ɩn kʊ yɩ.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: N ba tara sɔr na de a n libie ɩn a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ fʊ tara nyuur nɩ a n maal vɩla?’

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: “A lɛ jũu, a puor dẽme na wa ben niem ka a nie dẽme mɩ wa be a puor.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
translation_verse: Yesu ara na tɩ bʊɔl ba sʊrɔ, “Bo lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal kʊ yɩ?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
translation_verse: Nɩbaal kpɛ̃n a Yesu, ka wʊ sɩɩr a ba nɩbie, ka ba nyɛrɛ na ba turo wʊ.

english_verse: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Ka a nɩyɔɔ 'yɛr ɩka ba bar gɔ̃mɔ̃ tɩchɛ ka ba chɛnɛ gboolo, “Sɩ Soro, David Bie wooo ɩ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal.”

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
translation_verse: Yesu na wa pãa duoro a Jerusalɛm, wʊ tara na a poturbo pie nɩ ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba,

english_verse: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
translation_verse: Ban de man kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba la ma, tʊrɔ ma, chɩrɛ nataar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma, na ba ge ma, tɩ kʊ ma, ka a ber ata daar ka n ir ɩ vʊr go.”

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka Jebedee pɔɔ tara a wʊ bibiir ayi waan a wʊ sɛ̃ wa lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem na wʊ sʊɔrɔ bon kɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, “Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ?” Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Vɛ̃ ka a n bibiir banyã ayi ka ãsʊɔ jãnɛ a fʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ ka ãsʊɔ mɩ jãnɛ a fʊ gʊbaa jie a fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Faa bɔ̃ɔ a bon 'lɔ fʊn sʊɔrɔ ɛ. Ban tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele 'lɔ ɩn na nyũn ɔ?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “Sɩn tʊ̃ɔ naa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a n ŋmele tɩchɛ maa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a buor sʊɔ na jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ bɩɩ a n gʊbaa jie ɛ, ka a bala a n Sãa na chɔɔr kʊ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: A poturbo pie bala na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, ba nyɛn naa a yɛɛr ayi bala suur.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu bʊɔl ba waan wa 'yɛr, “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn na ɩka a bala na kaara a nɩbɛ bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ lɛ kaara ba bon jaa pɔ, ka a ba nɩbɛrɛ tʊ̃ɔ ba.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
translation_verse: A kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ be a lɛ a yɩ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ nɩdiere, wʊ ɩ a yɩ gbãgbaa.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩtãa lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na ba wa ɩka ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ ma ɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ de a n nyɛvʊr faan nɩyɔɔ yelbier bar.”

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
translation_verse: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo na wa yire a Jeriko nɩyɔɔ turo wʊ naa.

english_verse: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Ka a jɔ̃n ayi jãnɛ a sɔr kõkoor. Ban wa wõ ɩka Yesu lɛ tala, ka ba gboolo, “Sɩ Soro, David Bie wooo ɩ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal.”

english_verse: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
translation_verse: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Sɩ Soro, sɩ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ nyɛrɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr? Daba lɛ tara bibiir ayi, na wʊ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ, ‘N Bie chen tɩ tʊ̃ a n puo tʊ̃mɔ̃ a dɩna.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ a pokaarba na wa nyɛ a bie, ba 'yɛr kʊ taa, ‘Nyã lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃dire, yɩ ɩ a sɩ kʊ wʊ tɩ de a bonsɩ.’

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ naa, ‘Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃ɔ?’ Yɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘A Sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ yaa.’ Wʊn bar sɔr ka yɩ foraa waan fɔɔ jaa.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: A yele anyã ɩ na ka a na ɩ yelmãɛ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr na wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

english_verse: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
translation_verse: “'Yɛr kʊ a Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, ‘Nyɛ, a yɩ nãa waara na a yɩ sɛ̃. Wʊ siir wʊ mãɛ na wʊ do bʊ̃ɔ. Bʊ̃pɔl bile lɛ wʊ do waara nɩ.’ ” (Jekariya

english_verse: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
translation_verse: A poturbo chen tɩ tʊ̃ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr ɩka ba ɩ.

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
translation_verse: Wʊn wa kpɛ a Jerusalɛm tẽe pɔ, a nɩbɛ ba jaa ɩrɛ na yulõbʊmm, na ba sʊrɔ, “An lɛ a nyã?”

english_verse: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe na nyɛ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ wʊn tʊ̃ nɩ a bibiir na ɩrɛ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ ɩka, “Yãan yooo! Nãa David Bie.” Ka a ba pʊʊr sɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
translation_verse: A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Faraseemɩne na wa wõ a wʊ sʊkpar anyãna, ba bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka bala lɛ wʊ lɔɔrɔ a sʊkpar gbɛɛrɛ nɩ.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
translation_verse: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ‘A n yir na ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie,’ (Isaiya

english_verse: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
translation_verse: Ba sʊɔr a Yesu, “Fʊ wõn a lɛ a bibiir banyãna na 'yɛrɛ ɔ?” Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Ɔ̃ɔ, yaa dɔ̃ɔ kar ɩka, ‘A bibiir nɩ bipɩlɛ nɛgãma pɔ lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ pɛɛfʊ ɛ?’ ” (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
translation_verse: Wʊ bar ba naa tɩ yi chen a Betanɩ tẽe pɔ tɩ gã a be.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N mɩ na sʊʊr yɩn sʊʊrfʊ bʊ̃'yen. Yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wiil ma a par, n mɩ na wiil yɩ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna.

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
translation_verse: Wʊn wa lɩɛb chen a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ a bibio, kɔ̃ kpɛ wʊ naa.

english_verse: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
translation_verse: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn tara sɔɔfʊ na yaa baal ɛ, a ba ɩ a lɛ na tʊ̃ kʊ a tɩɛ nyã tɛɛ lɛ yɩn tʊ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn gba 'yɛr kʊ a tɔ̃ɔ nyãna, ‘Ir suu a man pɔ,’ tɩ wʊ̃n ir naa.

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
translation_verse: Be lɛ a ba 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, “Saa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.” Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: “Yɩ lɛ chɛlɛ a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ. Daba lɛ kɔ wʊ puo na wʊ. Wʊ wɔɔn a puo wʊ jaa jilʊ̃, na wʊ tuu bɔɔ ka ban ma pɔr a wɔ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ na ba nɛŋmɛr ir a kʊ̃ɔ. Wʊ mɛn jie ãsʊɔ na do ka nɩrɛ na ma do jãnɛ a be kaara a puo. Wʊ den a puo kʊ nɩbɛ ɩka ba kaara tɩ na wʊ yɔ chen paal yuo pɔ.

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: A Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr bɩɩ nɩbɛ lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr? Yɩ 'yɛr n kaa?” Ka ba piel chɩrɛ taa, “Sɩn be 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ wʊn sʊʊr sɩ naa ka bo lɛ so ka saa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ?

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ sɩn mɩ 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ a ba ɩ ɛ, sɩ joro naa a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ bojũu nɩrɛ jaa sɔɔ naa ɩka a Jɔɔn ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘N kʊ̃ chen ɛ,’ tɩchɛ na wʊ ta sɔ̃ɔ lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr na wʊ chen.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: A sãa lɛ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a ayi sʊɔ a lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ, ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘N chen naa,’ tɩ na wʊ tɔr.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: “A bibiir banyãna ayi, buor sʊɔ lɛ tʊ̃ a wʊ sãa bɔfʊ?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “A dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ lɛ.” Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar na dɔ̃ɔ yɩn nie kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro wan wa wile yɩ a yelmãɛ sɔr ka yaa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar sɔɔn de wʊ, yɩn sɔɔ wa nyɛ gba yaa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: A pɔrfʊ sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ gbãgbaar ɩka ba chen a nɩbɛ bala sɛ̃ tɩ pɔr a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bala nyɔɔ a wʊ gbãgbaar ŋme a ãsʊɔ, na ba kʊ a ãsʊɔ tɩ na ba lɔb a ãsʊɔ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ kʊ.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ lɛ tʊ̃ a gbãgbaar bamɩne ka ba ge ba a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: A baaraa lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ mãɛ bie a ba sɛ̃ na wʊ 'yɛr, ‘Ban 'yɔ̃ɔ naa a n bie.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba nyɔɔ wʊ kʊ lɔb bar a puo kõkoor.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: “Pʊ̃pãanyã a puo sʊɔ wa wa, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn ɩ a pokaarba banyãna?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Wʊn wan wa kʊ a pokaarba banyã ba jaa tɩ de a puo kʊ nɩyor, a bala na ma sɔɔ ka ba põ a puo bonsɩ a ŋmafʊ sɔ̃ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yaa kar a jie nyã a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr ɩka a, ‘A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr bar lɛ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju. A sɩ Soro lɛ tʊ̃ a nɩtɔ̃ ka a sɔb sɩ a nɩbie.’ (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: “A lɛ jũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na faan a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ yin a yɩ sɛ̃ kʊ a nɩyor bala na sɔɔ wʊ.

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ nyã na lo a kʊsɩr nyã ju na pʊrpʊr a, tɩchɛ a wʊ mɩ na kʊ nɩrɛ nyã ju wʊn lo dɔɔl.”

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: Ka ba bʊɔrɔ na sɔr ɩka ba nyɔɔ wʊ, tɩ na ba joro a nɩyɔɔ dãbãɛ bojũu ba nyɛn ka wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: “Nɩyɔɔ lɛ ba bʊɔl, tɩchɛ bãlãa lɛ ban chɛl ir.”

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a Faraseemɩne guor taa na ba wa bɛɛr Yesu kaa na ba na nyɔɔ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛrbie pɔ.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Wiwile, Mosesɩ sɛb ɩka yɛbɛ wa kpi tɩ bar pɔɔ na ba tara bie ɛ, a wʊ yɛbɛ wʊ kul a pɔɔkʊɔr 'lɔ na wʊ dɔɔ bibiir kʊ a wʊ yɛbɛ 'lɔ na kpi.

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛntãan fɩka nãa kɔ̃ɔ na maal a wʊ bidaba na kulo pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: “Wʊ lɛ tʊ̃n nɩyor ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl, ‘Nyɛ, n maal a pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir baar, n kʊn a n nadar nɩ a napɔl ka yele jaa chɔɔr baar, yɩ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.’

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: A nãa suur ir naa ka wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ sojasɩ ɩka ba tɩ kʊ a nɩkʊrbɔ bala tɩ chʊm a ba tẽe.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: “Tɩ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, ‘Yele jaa chɔɔr baar, tɩchɛ a bala ɩn bʊɔl ba sɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Yɩ yi a tẽe pɔ tɩ bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa yɩn nyɛ ka wʊ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.’

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ a nãa na wa kpɛ ɩka wʊ kaa a nɩbɛ, wʊ nyɛn daba ãsʊɔ na ba su a pɔɔbɩɛl fuu ɛ,

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a lɛ fʊn tɩɛr. A tara na sɔr ɩka sɩ yab a lõpoo kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bɩɩ sɩ ta yab ɛ?”

english_verse: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
translation_verse: Ka a ayi sʊɔ mɩ de a pɔɔkʊɔr kul baa mɩ dɔɔ bie, tɩchɛ ka wʊ mɩ kpi. A lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ ata tɩ tãn a ayopõi sʊɔ.

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba tʊ̃ a ba poturbo nɩ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Nãa Hɛrɔd sɛ̃, na ba 'yɛr, “Wiwile, sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka fʊ ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ, na fʊ wile Nãaŋmɩn sɔr nɩ yelmãɛ, bojũu faa kaara lɛ nɩbɛ na 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ oor ɛ.

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Ka ba 'yɛr, “A Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa lɛ.” Lɛ lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sofʊ na yɩ kʊ wʊ, na yɩ mɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sofʊ mɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Ban wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, nɛɛ 'maa ba naa ka ba bar wʊ tɩ chen jaa jomm.

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: A Mosesɩ nɛɛr a jaa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ wiilfʊ kpãkpãana na a binfʊ nyãna.”

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: A bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a Saduseemɩne na 'yɛr ɩka a kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, ba wan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr wʊ,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yaa nyɛ yɩn bɔr, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ wa ir a kũu pɔ, ba kʊ̃ lɛ kul taa ɛ, tɩ ban kaara fɩka malakasɩ na be a saaju.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a kũu pɔ ir fʊ yele, yaa kar a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka,

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: n ɩ naa a Abraham Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Isaakɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn ɛ? (A Yifʊ Gãn

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ nɛɛr 'maa ba naa nɩ a wʊ wiilfʊ.

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: A Faraseemɩne na wa wõ ɩka Yesu 'yɛr tʊ̃ɔ a Saduseemɩne, ba mɩ lɔ̃ɔn taa.

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: “Wiwile, A Mosesɩ nɛbinsɩ a jaa pɔ, buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Nyã lɛ ɩ a kpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɛdɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: A ayi sʊɔ mɩ nɩ nyã, ‘Nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ kaar.’ (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Ata

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: A Faraseemɩne na wa 'mataa, Yesu sʊʊr ba naa.

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ‘A Soro 'yɛr kʊ a n Soro, “Jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ka n wa de a fʊ dɔ̃dẽme ka ba ɩ a fʊ gbɛɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie.” ’ (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr a Nɩ-iraa yele? An bie lɛ wʊ ɩ?” Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “David bie lɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ka a nãa David a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ, a wʊ bʊɔl wʊ ɩka, ‘N Soro’? Bojũu wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Ala ɩ David bʊɔl wʊn wʊ ɩka, ‘N Soro,’ ŋmɩnɛ wʊn ɩrɛ wa ɩ a wʊ bie?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: An yɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ chiine, nɩrɛ jaa ba lɛ haa nɛɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ bɩɩ lɛ 'la sʊʊr sʊʊrfʊ jaa go ɛ.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'mɔɔrɔ a wʊ mãɛ ire nɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na suun wʊn pĩlem tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na siir wʊ mãɛ Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn ka wʊ do.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu n bar a fʊ tẽe kʊ fʊ ka wʊ ɩ dakpolo.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: A 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Pa yɩn Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, a yɩn na ma kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na yɩ lɔɔrɔ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ, nyɛ lɛ an ma ɩrɛ ma vɔlaa jã ka n ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka n kaal a fʊ bibiir 'mataa fɩka nʊ̃ɔ na ma ũun a wʊ bil, tɩchɛ ka faa bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ maal a lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ bon jaa na dɔɔl a wʊ ju.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ bala nɩ a wʊ poturbo,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “A nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne jãnɛ na a Mosesɩ dakɔɔ ju.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, yɩ maala a lɛ ban wile tɩ na yɩ turo, tɩ yɩ ta maala a lɛ ban maala ɛ, bojũu ba maa turo a lɛ ban wile ɛ.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: Ba ma lũn tuor tɩɩr tuo nɩbɛ, tɩ maa sɔɔ de a ba nubir sɩɩr ɛ.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: “Yele jaa ban tʊ̃nɔ̃, ba ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka nɩbɛ ba nyɛ ba, na ba ma lũ a fɔɔr a ba jur pɔ, tɩ na ba ma su fuusɩ ban ma sɛ bonsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ jaa vɛvɛɛ.

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Bala wa lɔ̃ɔ taa dire, a ji'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ jie lɛ ba ma nʊ̃ ɩka ba jãnɛ, nɩ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir a jibɛrɛ jiir.

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Ala tɩ na ba ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka bama puoro ba a dar pɔ, na ba ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩbɛ ba bʊɔlɔ ba, Wiwiilbe.

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: “Yɩ ta sɔɔ ka ba bʊɔlɔ yɩ, Wiwiilbe ɛ, nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ yɩ tara. Yɩ jaa ɩn yɛɛr.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Yɩ ta bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa yɩ, sãa a wɛr pɔ ɛ, bojũu yɩ tara na Sãa bʊ̃'yen na be a saaju.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Bɩɩ yɩ ta bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa wiwile ɛ, bojũu yɩ tara na Wiwile bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ na ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma yɔn jitɛbɛ nɩ a man pɔ na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr lɩɛb nɩrɛ tɩɛr, yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wʊ ka wʊ kaara a yɩ kaar, yɩ ma lɩɛb wʊ naa ka wʊn di dɔɔyɛ a bũu dagel pɔ a yɩ kaar.

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: “Nɛbier ben a bala na ɩ jɔ̃n tɩ na ba tɔɔrɔ a jɔ̃n, na ba ma 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a ba ɩ tuo ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɔn a sɛlma 'lɔ na be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a lũ wʊ naa.’

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Yɩ ɩn jɔ̃n na yɩ ɩ jɔlɔ. Buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a sɛlma bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yir 'lɔ na maal a sɛlma ka wʊ ɩ a chɛchɛ?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Yɩ mɩ 'yɛr naa, ‘Ala ɩ nɩrɛ pɔn na a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie, a ba tuo ɛ, tɩchɛ ala ɩ nɩrɛ pɔn na a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ 'lɔ na dɔɔl a jie, a lũ wʊ naa.’

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Yɩn jɔ̃n banyã. Buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a kʊfʊ bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie na ma vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ chɛchɛ?

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn yir, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a be.

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a saaju, pɔn naa a Nãaŋmɩn nãkɔɔ nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na jãnɛ a be.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma ir a bonsɩ pɔ jiir pie pɔ bʊ̃'yen lɔ̃ɔn badʊʊ gba vaar kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, tɩ na yɩ tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛr ala na tɩrɛ bar na ɩ yelmãɛ sɔr tufʊ, nɩbaal kaafʊ nɩ yelmãɛ tarfʊ. A anyã lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka yɩ turo tɩ na yɩ taa ire amɩne bara ɛ.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Yɩn jɔ̃tɔɔrbɔ ba nyã. Yɩ ma chuor a jãajubil bar tɩ ma vɔl a nyɔɔma.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma pɛɛn a ŋmanyuraa nɩ a ladiraa a yõo tɩchɛ ka a pʊɔ sɛɛn bɔgar nɩ nyuur.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ kaara fɩka yar ban pãɛ ka a vɩɛlɛ, tɩchɛ ka a pʊɔ pɔ tara nɩbɛ kɔbɔ nɩ dɛɛr jaa bʊrɔ.

english_verse: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
translation_verse: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a yɩn a yõo, yɩ ɩn nɩtor a yɩ taaba sɛ̃, tɩchɛ a yɩ pʊɔm, yɩ tara na nɛɛr ayi nɩ putuyele.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ mɛn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yar, tɩ na chɔɔr a bala na tor yar,

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: tɩ na yɩ 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Sɩn ta be be a sɩ sãakpãmɩne sɔ̃ɔ, sɩ kʊ̃ naa ta pʊɔ ba kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: A nɩtɔ̃ jũu yɩ din a yɩ mãɛ dãasɩɛ ɩka yɩ ɩn a nɩbɛ bala na kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bibiir.

english_verse: So keep on doing everything they did.
translation_verse: Yɩ baar baar a lɛ a yɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ.

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: “Yɩn waa bibiir, ŋmɩnɛ yɩn ɩrɛ wa chãɛ a bũu dagel kũu.

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ na nyɛn gefʊ a yele anyãna jũu.

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, wa tɩ tãn yɩn na 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃.’ ” (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Yesu na wa yi a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ na wʊ chiine, a wʊ poturbo turo wile ɩka wʊ nyɛ lɛ ban mɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ka wʊ kaara.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Mɩ yɩ mɩ nyɛ naa anyãna a jaa? Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a kʊsɩvɩɛl anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ lɛ wa chɛnɛ dɔɔl a wʊ tɔ ju ɛ. Ban wa dãn a jaa lɔb.”

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Bojũu, nɩyɔɔ na de naa maa yuor waan wa 'yɛrɛ, ‘Maa lɛ a Nɩ-iraa,’ na ban bɛl nɩyɔɔ.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Yɩn wa wõn tam, ka ba 'yɛrɛ a tam 'yɛr, yɩ ta mɩrɛ ɛ. A lɛ na ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a wɛr baarfʊ chɛnɛ na be puor.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Yesu na wa do jãnɛ a Olive Tɔ̃ɔju, a poturbo wan a wʊ sɛ̃ a ba tɛɛ wa sʊʊr wʊ, “'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a anyãna nɩ na ɩ. Bo lɛ na ɩ a jãnɛ a fʊ waa sɔ̃ɔ nɩ a wɛr baarfʊ daar?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Paal dẽdẽme na yin tam, ka a nãmɩne mɩ yɩ tam ka kɔ̃ mɩ lo ka a tẽe jiir amɩne dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: A bɩbir nyãna wa vɩɛ an ɩn tuo kʊ a pɔɔpʊʊr nɩ a bala na tara a bi'yɛnsɩ.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: A dɔɔyɛ anyãna, an kaara fɩka a pɔɔ na ma dɔɔrɔ ka a wõnõ wʊ.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Jir ŋmarba 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na wan wa 'yɛrɛ bɛlɛ nɩyɔɔ.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: A lɛ daar nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr yɩ ɩka, ‘Nyɛ a Nɩ-iraa,’ bɩɩ ‘'Lɔ nɩ nyã,’ yɩ ta sɔɔ de ɛ.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a yɩ jofʊ ta ɩ sãɛ ɛ, bɩɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɛ.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: “Yɩn wa nyɛ naa a yele 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'laar na waara nɩ sɔ̃ɔfʊ ka wʊ be a jichɛchɛ 'lɔ nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Danɩɛl na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr. Vɛ̃ ka a nyã na kara bɔ̃ɔ a par. (Danɩɛl

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: Yɩn wa nyɛ a lɛ a nɩbɛ bala na kpɩɛrɛ a Juda, ba jo do a tɔ̃ɔ ju.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: 'Lɔ mɩ na be a puo pɔ wʊ ta kul a yir tɩ de a wʊ fuu ɛ.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn ba ŋmaa a ber ala bar ɛ, nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ. Tɩchɛ a bala wʊn chɛl ir jũu wʊn ŋmaan a ber bar.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a yelsɩ anyãna, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa gbʊr naa jaa yɛ̃yɛ̃.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: A lɛ an dɔ̃ɔ kaara a Nowa sɔ̃ɔ, lɛ lɛ an mɩ na kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa sɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir nyã ɛ, bɩɩ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a malakasɩ mɩ gba na be a saaju ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bɩɩ a Bie ɛ, ka a Sãa tɛɛ ba lɛ bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Bojũu sɛrɛ, tɩchɛ ka a kɔ̃paalaa wa nɩbɛ dire na, tɩ nyuro, ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ kulo taa, tɩ tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ a Nowa na kpɛ a daka bulomtum pɔ.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Baa bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a kɔ̃paalaa na wa di ba, lɛ mɩ lɛ an kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa daar.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ ayi na ben puo pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a ãsʊɔ chiin tɩ bar a ãsʊɔ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Pɔbɔ ayi na 'mantaa nɩɛnɛ jɔ̃ ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a ãsʊɔ chiin tɩ bar a ãsʊɔ.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: “A lɛ jũu yɩ kaara sʊ̃, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir 'lɔ a yɩ Soro nɩ na wa ɛ.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a yele anyã par, a yir sʊɔ ta bɔ̃ɔn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a nãnyie na waara a tãsɔɔ, wʊ kʊ̃ naa gur ɛ, ka a nãnyie wa kar kpɛ wʊ a yir ɛ.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, yɩ mɩ guro a yɩ mãɛ, bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie na wan a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna ɩn putusʊɔ na wʊ tɩɛr a wʊ nyãa pɔ ɩka, a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kor naa,

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: “An lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a yɛ̃ nɩ a yelmãɛ sʊɔ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ kaara wʊ a yir na wʊ kʊrɔ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ba bʊ̃diir a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ?

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ ka wʊ ɩrɛ a lɛ.

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, wʊn vɛ̃n ka wʊ kaara a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: tɩ piel gere a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala na be a yir tɩ pãa ma di na wʊ ma pʊɔ a dãnyurbo pɔ,

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa dũu wʊ naa a bɩbir 'lɔ wʊn ba tɩɛr nɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn ba bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Wʊn ŋma wʊn gbʊl gbʊl 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme pɔ, be lɛ ban kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: A jɔlɔ bala den a ba fãtɩnɛ tɩchɛ baa de kãa pʊɔ ɛ,

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: tɩchɛ ka a ba anũu bala mɩ na ɩ a yɛ̃ dẽme, de kãa pʊɔ a ba fãtɩnɛ pɔ.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: “A tãsɔɔ bãɩn lɛ ba ŋme chɛlma tãna 'yɛrɛ, ‘A pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ waara, yɩ yi wa tuor wʊ yooo.’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: “A pɔɔsarsɩ pie banyãna vaan ir tɩɛ tɩɛ a ba gonsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba fãtɩnɛ pɔ.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: A jɔlɔ bala 'yɛr kʊ a yɛ̃ dẽme, ‘Yɩ pʊr a yɩ kãa amɩne kʊ sɩ, a sɩ bonsɩ kpĩine naa.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: “Be lɛ ka a yɛ̃ dẽme bala 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Kai, a kʊ̃ ta sɩ nɩ a yɩn ɛ. Yɩ chen a be ban daara na yɩ tɩ da.’

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yelmãɛ jaa, n ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ.’

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: “A lɛ jũu yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir bɩɩ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: “An wa kor bãlãa ka a gbãgbaar bala nɩkpɛ̃ɛ wa wa ɩka ba guor a sɛlbie yele.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: a ta sɛɛn ɩka fʊ ta de a n sɛlbir tɩ yɔ̃ɔ a libie yir pɔ, ka maa ta wa lɩɛb wa ka n nyɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl ɛ?

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: “A mɩ lɛ nyɛntãan go fɩka daba kɔ̃ɔ na bʊɔrɔ yɔfʊ na wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ gbãgbaar 'mataa de a wʊ bonsɩ põ ba.

english_verse: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
translation_verse: A 'lɔ na de a sɛlbie anũu tɩ yar nyɛ sɛlbie anũu tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ma ka de a fʊ sɛlbir tɩ ũu bar. Nyɛ a fʊ sɛlbir.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ̃ʊ gbãgbaputuo nɩ koro sʊɔ nyã. Fʊn ben bɔ̃ɔn ɩka n ma ŋman a be ɩn maa bʊr ɛ, naa wob a jie 'lɔ ɩn maa yaar bar ɛ,

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: “ ‘Yɩ faa a sɛlbir bʊ̃'yen a wʊ sɛ̃ na yɩ kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara a sɛlbie pie.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: A gbãgbaburbur nyã 'lɔ, yɩ de wʊ lɔb bar yõo be lɛ ban kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.’

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: “A maa Nɩsaal Bie wa wa a n tɩɩr pɔ, nɩ a n malakasɩ ba jaa, ɩn wa jãnɛ naa, a n tɩɩr nãalʊ̃ʊ dakɔɔ ju.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: A paalsɩ a jaa na pãa lɔ̃ɔ taa a n niem, ka n pãa oor a nɩbɛ nɩtãa lɛ pɛchinɛ na ma oor a piir nɩ a bʊʊr.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: Ɩn oon a piir bar a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, tɩchɛ ka a bʊʊr mɩ ara a n gʊbaa jie.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: “Ka n pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala na be a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ‘Yɩ wa, yɩn a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ. Yɩ wa kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, de a yɩ nãbomo wʊn chɔɔr bin gun yɩ a wɛr pielfʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: Bojũu kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yɩ kʊ ma ka n di, kɔ̃nyũur dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yɩ kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ka n nyũ, n dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan ka yɩ ir ma sãan.

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: N ba dɔ̃ɔ tara bʊ̃sursɩ ɛ, ka yɩ kʊ ma fuu ka n su, n dɔ̃ɔ bɩɛrɛ ka yɩ sãa ma. N dɔ̃ɔ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yɩ wa piir ma kaa.’

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: “A nãa na 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a lɛ yɩn dɔ̃ɔ maal kʊ a nɩba'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala na ɩ a n sofʊ, maa lɛ yɩ maal kʊ.’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala mɩ na be a wʊ gʊbaa jie, ‘Pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ turo yɩ naa, yɩ tɔɔ chen be na yɩ tɩ kpɛ a bũu 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na gbɔɔ gun a tɩɩ nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Bojũu kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yaa kʊ ma bon jaa ka n di ɛ. Kɔ̃nyũur dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yaa kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ka n nyũ ɛ.

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: N dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan, ka yaa kaa ma sãan kaa fʊ ɛ, n ba tara bon jaa na su ɛ, ka yaa kʊ ma fuu ka su ɛ, ka n bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yaa wa piir ma kaa ɛ.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: “Ka ba pãa mɩ lɩɛb sʊʊr wʊ, ‘Sɩ Soro, sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ sɩ nyɛ fʊ, ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ fʊ, bɩɩ ka a kɔ̃nyũur kpɛ fʊ, bɩɩ ka fʊ ɩ sãan, bɩɩ ka faa tara bon jaa na su ɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka saa kaa fʊ ɛ?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: “Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn ba maal kʊ a nɩba'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala ãsʊɔ ɛ, maa lɛ yaa maal kʊ ɛ.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: “A nɩbɛ banyãna na chen a dɔɔyɛ 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ jie, tɩchɛ ka a nɩmɩn mɩ chen a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ jie.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a chɛn ber ayi ka a Gõl Bar Difʊ na ta, ka ba na de a maa Nɩsaal Bie tɩr ka ba kpa ma a daa ju.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a poturbo sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr ba, “Yɩ chɛnɛ na gã guro na yɩ pɛnɛ wɛ? Nyɛ, a sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka ban gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie de wʊ kʊ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: na ba bʊɔrɔ vuo ban na tu nyɔɔ a Yesu na ba kʊ.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka ba 'yɛr, a ta ɩ a Gõl Bar Difʊ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ka jɛɛr taa ɩrɛ wa ir a chara pɔ ɛ.

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: Fʊn taa den a kãnyũu anyãna da libiyɔɔ de kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme.”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: A poturbo na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba nyɛn suur na ba 'yɛr, “Bo lɛ so ka wʊ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kãnyũu nyã bar a nɩtɔ̃?

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Ban wa yiel a yiel dãn Nãaŋmɩn, ba suun chen a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju.

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: An yɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊ bʊɔrɔ na vuo wʊn na tu de a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba nuur pɔ.

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: Ban wa dire, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a yɩ ãsʊɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: A bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩ naa a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ, ka a Yesu poturbo wa sʊʊr wʊ, “Nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr a Gõl Bar nyã Difʊ bar ka sɩ tɩ di?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Ban wa dire, Yesu den a paanʊ̃ɔ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, “Yɩ de 'wɔb, a maa ãgãn lɛ.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: A anyãna lɛ ɩ a n jãɩ na ɩ a nɛtɩraa ɩn tara nɩ yɩ na tara kpɛ̃ɔ. An kpaar na a faa nɩyɔɔ ka yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ be be.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, n kʊ̃ lɛ nyũ a dãa anyãna ɛ, wa tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩn na 'mataa nyũn yɩ a dãpaalaa a n Sãa nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ jaa na jon a tãsɔɔ nyãna tɩ bar ma a n yõ, a maa jũu. A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ‘Ɩn ŋme naa pɛchinɛ kʊ ka a piir yɛ̃yɛl.’ (Jekariya

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, ɩka a tãsɔɔ nyãna a nɔrdɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ kõ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka fʊ chiir ma gbɛɛ ata.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo chen a jie 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Getɩsemanɩ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ jãnɛ a kana tɩchɛ ka n chen tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N tɩɛr sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩ nyɛtãan kũn, yɩ jãnɛ a ka na gu ma.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ala ɩ ba jaa sɔɔn bar fʊ a fʊ jũu, maa 'lɔ kʊ̃ bar fʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka, “Ala ɩ maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ na 'mataa kpi gba, tɩ n kʊ̃ 'yɛr ɩka n ba bɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɛ.” Ka a poturbo bala mɩ na chɛ 'yɛr a lɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Wʊ lɛ chen a gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. “N Sãa, ala ɩ a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyã kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ po ma ɛ, ka maa ba nyũn wʊ ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ bɔfʊ wʊ ɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Wʊ chen a niem bãlãa tɩ lo kpa tẽe sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ, “N Sãa ala ɩ a tara naa ɩfʊ kɔ̃ɔ, vɛ̃ ka a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyãna po ma. Tɩchɛ a ta ɩ lɛ maa na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, tɩ a ɩ a lɛ fʊ̃ʊ na bʊɔrɔ.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Yɩ vaa ir ka sɩ chen. Yɩ nyɛ a gagaraa na waara.”

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ tara Pita nɩ Jebedee bibiir ayi ka a wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa ka tɩɛr nyɔɔ wʊ.

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a poturbo sɛ̃ wa nyɛ ka ba guro. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A mɔ̃ yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ jɛ̃ bãlãa jaa gu ma ɛ?

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Yɩ jɛɔ chɛlɛ, na yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a bɛɛr kaa ta wa nyɔɔ yɩ ɛ. A ba tɩɛr bʊɔrɔ naa tɩchɛ ka a ba ãgãma ba tʊɔr ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “N badaba, tʊ̃ a lɛ fʊn bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ tʊ̃.” Be lɛ ba wa nyɔɔ a Yesu.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: A Yesu na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, ka Judas na ɩ a wʊ poturbo Pie nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ, tɩ daasãa nɩ nɩbɛ na yi a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃ waara nɩ sɔbɛrɛ nɩ dakʊrsɩ a wʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: A Judas dɔ̃ɔ man ba ɩka, a 'lɔ wʊn tɩ puor tɩ mʊʊr a kɔtɩr 'lɔ lɛ, yɩ nyɔɔ wʊ.

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Ban wa tɩ ta, Judas pɔɔn chen tɩ 'yɛr, “Wiwile, n puor fʊ naa,” tɩ mʊʊr a Yesu kɔtɩr.

english_verse: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a Yesu poturbo ãsʊɔ na ara gbʊr wʊ vʊ̃ɔ a sʊɔ chen a Juu dẽme bɔɔrlo nãa gbãgbaa toor ŋmaa.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “De a sʊɔ su a sʊkpa pɔ, bala na jɛɛrɛ nɩ a sʊɔ na kpin a sʊɔ kũu.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Ka a bɔɔrlo nãa tɔɔ a fuu chɩɛr tɩ 'yɛr, “Wʊ sɔ̃ɔn Nãaŋmɩn yuor. Bo lɛ so sɩn chɛnɛ bʊɔrɔ dãasɩdirbe? Yɩ wõn wʊ tʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu ɩ jaa gbul. Be lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa 'yɛr, “N sʊrɔ fʊ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ɩ a vʊr niem, ɩka fʊ 'yɛr kʊ sɩ, ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Wʊn wa tɩ ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, a pɔɔbile ãsʊɔ nyɛ wʊ naa na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ na ara a be, “A daba nyã mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: A bala na nyɔɔ a Yesu, chiin wʊ naa a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa Kayafasɩ sɛ̃, ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa wa lɔ̃ɔ taa.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka Pita be a puor jaa turo, na wʊ tɩ kpɛ a bɔɔrlo nãa yir bɛchaar pɔ, tɩ lɔ̃ɔ jãnɛ nɩ a gugurbo, ɩka wʊ nyɛ lɛ an na baar.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nɩbɛrɛ pãa bʊɔrɔ jir ŋmarba ka ba na ŋmaa jire 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, ka ba na tu a be kʊ a Yesu.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ baa nyɛ yele jaa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Nɩyɔɔ daan yi ŋma jiryɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, tɩchɛ ka a ba 'yɛrbie ɩ jirsɩ. A baaraa nɩbɛ ayi daan yi

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
translation_verse: na ba 'yɛr ɩka, “A daba nyãna lɛ 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Ɩn tʊ̃ɔ ŋmɛr a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir nyãna bar tɩ de ber ata lɛ mɛn wʊ.’ ”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ɔ̃ɔ, a lɛ nɛ fʊn 'yɛr lɛ. Tɩchɛ yɩ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ɩka daar kɔ̃ɔ, yɩn nyɛn a maa Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ a Kpɛ̃ɔ Sʊɔ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie. Yɩ mɩ na lɛ nyɛ ma naa ka n yɩ a julõjuur pɔ waara.”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Ba chir nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a nie pɔ, tɩ ma gbul nũu ŋmen wʊ, ka bamɩne mɩ ma 'ma wʊ,

english_verse: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: tɩ sʊʊr, “Nɩ-iraa, ala ɩ fʊ bɔ̃ɔn yele jaa wiil sɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ŋme fʊ?”

english_verse: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a Pita lɛ chiir pɔ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, “N ba bɔ̃ɔ a daba nyãna ɛ.”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Pita jãnɛ naa a yõo a bɛchaar pɔ. Ka a gbãgbapɔɔbile ãsʊɔ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr, “Fʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Galilee.”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
