translation_verse: Nɛbier ben a yɩn Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe sɛ̃, bojũu yɩn lɛ tara a bɔ̃ɔfʊ pãn bir, yɩn mãɛ ba kpɩɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ pɔɔ var a bala na bʊɔrɔ a kpɛfʊ.”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Yesu yin a be wʊn 'yɛr a anyãna, ka a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe kpãkpãana wʊ nɩ suur sʊrɔ nɩ wʊ 'yɛr yɔɔ.

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Na ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ 'yɛr 'yɛr lɩɛb nyɔɔ wʊ mãɛ.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ, a nɩyɔɔ turo turo tire taa na ba nɛɛrɛ taa, ka Yesu piel kpãana a wʊ poturbo na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne dãbɩl pɔ, ala lɛ ɩ a ba nɛɛr ayi.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Ala ɩ nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ma pãɛ a bonsɩ puul a mʊ̃ɔ pɔ ka a vɩɛlɛ a dɩna tɩchɛ ka a wɛr chaar ka a bũu di, wʊ kʊ̃ pãɛ a yɩn gar a lɛ ɛ? Yɩn sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ.

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Yɩ de nie bɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ, ka anyãna a jaa wʊn den pʊɔ yɩ.

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: “N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ a yɩn na ɩ a n barmɩne ɩka, yɩ ta joro a bala na kʊ a yɩ ãgãn ɛ, tɩ na ba kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ yele jaa ɩ ɛ.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Sɩrɛ jaa, Nãaŋmɩn sɔr a yɩ jukɔɔl a jaa bɔ̃ɔ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, yɩ gar tiita yɔɔ.

english_verse: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
translation_verse: “Yɩn piir na ba jã bon ɛ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, bojũu a yɩ Sãa pʊɔ pɛl naa wʊn kʊ yɩ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Yɩ da a yɩ bonsɩ de a libie põ nɔ̃n dẽme, yɩn tara naa a libie wʊʊr ala na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ. Ka a yɩ bʊ̃tarsɩ be a saaju, naa kʊ̃ wur ɛ. Nãnyie kʊ̃ gbʊr be ɛ, kpolo ba be be na mʊʊ ɛ.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: Bojũu a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wiil yɩ naa a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr.”

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Jɔl nyã, fʊn kpin a tãsɔɔ nyãna, tɩ a bonsɩ ala fʊn bin kʊ a fʊ mãɛ, an lɛ na de?’

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: “N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na wa 'yɛr chã a nɩbɛ niem, ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa a maa Nɩsaal Bie, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na 'yɛr chã ɩka, n bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ niem.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ na chiir ma a nɩbɛ niem, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na chiir a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ niem.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: Bala wa nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme sɛ̃, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a ɩ yɩ tɩɛr a lɛ yɩn na tɩ 'yɛr faa yɩ mãɛ ɛ, bɩɩ a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr ɛ.

english_verse: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
translation_verse: Ka nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ na be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Wiwile 'yɛr a n yɛbɛ ka wʊ ɩ a sɩ põ a kpĩi bonsɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, “Daba, an 'yɔ̃ɔ ma ɩka n ɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ naa põnõ yɩ kpĩi bomo?”

english_verse: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
translation_verse: Tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ kaara sʊ̃, na yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a bʊ̃yɔɔ bɔfʊ pɔ, nɩrɛ bʊ̃yɔɔ ba kʊrɔ wʊ nyɛvʊr ɛ.”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Na wʊ piel tɩɛrɛ ɩka ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn ɩ, ‘Ɩn ba tara jie na bin a bonsɩ ɛ?’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ ɩn ɩ, ‘Ɩn ŋmɛr a bobo anyãna bar, tɩ naa mɛ bʊ̃bɛrɛ naa su a chi 'yɔ̃ɔn a bonsɩ a be.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Kaa Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, “A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, yɩ ta waala a yɩ nyɛvʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, 'yɛrɛ ɩka bo lɛ yɩn di ɛ. Bɩɩ waala ɩka bo lɛ yɩn su pɔɔ a ãgãn ɛ.

english_verse: Life is more than food or clothing.
translation_verse: A nyɛvʊr gar naa a bʊ̃diir, ka a ãgãn mɩ gar a fɔɔr.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: “Yɩ kaa nyɛ a lɛ a bonsɩ puul na ma nʊ̃ɔ. Ba maa tʊ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ wob fuu su ɛ, tɩchɛ a nãa Solomon gba nɩ a wʊ bʊ̃tarsɩ ba dɔ̃ɔ su fuu ka wʊ vɩɛlɛ gar naa ɛ.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ jaa, ala puor lɛ ba turo, tɩchɛ a yɩ Sãa bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa a bonsɩ anyãna.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna tɩɛr a wʊ nyãa pɔ ɩka, a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kor a waa, tɩ piel pɔɔrɔ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala na be a yir, tɩ pãa ma di na wʊ nyũ buu.

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Yɩ maa tãan nɛbʊ̃'yen yele lɛ n waan a wɛr pɔ bɩɩ? Kai, n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, tɩchɛ ootaa lɛ n waan.

english_verse: Be ready and keep your lamps burning
translation_verse: “Yɩ chɔɔr yɩ mãɛ gu tɩ chʊm a yɩ fãtɩnsɩ ka a lɩrɛ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ banyãna a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ ba ka ba chɛnɛ jɛ̃ gu wʊ. Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka wʊn chɔɔr naa a wʊ mãɛ ka ba 'mataa jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir jie ka wʊ põ ba a bʊ̃diir.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ ban chɔɔr, ala ɩ gba ka tãsɔɔ põn lɛ bɩɩ, ka nɔrsɩ kõnõ naa.

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par, ɩka ala ɩ a yir sʊɔ bɔ̃ɔ naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a nãnyie na waara, wʊ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ka a nãnyie kar a pãn tɩ kpɛ a yir ɛ.

english_verse: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: A yɩn mɩ yɩ chɔɔr gu, bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie na lɩɛb wa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn ba lɛ tɩɛrɛ ma ɛ.”

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Ka a Pita sʊʊr wʊ, “N Soro, sɩn lɛ fʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ bɩɩ nɩrɛ jaa lɛ?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “An lɛ pãa ɩ a yelmãɛ nɩ a yɛ̃ sʊɔ, a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ kaara wʊ a yir, na wʊ kʊrɔ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ba bʊ̃diir a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ na sɛɛ?

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ wʊn ɩrɛ a lɛ.

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, wʊn vɛ̃n ka wʊ kaara a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna, a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa dũu wʊ naa a bɩbir 'lɔ wʊn ba tɩɛrɛ nɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Wʊn ŋma wʊn jaa gbʊl gbʊl, tɩ lɔb wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba sɔɔ de pɔ ɛ.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ 'lɔ na bɔ̃ɔ lɛ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na bʊɔrɔ tɩ na waa chɔɔr tʊ̃n a lɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, wʊn nyɛn ŋmefʊ yɔɔ.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ na ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ maal bɔr ban ŋme wʊn jaa bãlãa. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ ban kʊ yɔɔ ban bʊɔrɔ na yɔɔ yi a wʊ sɛ̃, fʊ̃ʊ nũu pɔ ban 'yɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ban sʊʊr yɔɔ.

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: “Bũu lɛ n waan ɩka n wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a wɛr pɔ, ɩn taa bʊɔrɔ naa ala ɩ a ben ta 'lɩrɛ.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ n chɛnɛ tara yele ɩn na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ kaar ka a ɩ ma tɩɛr tɩ tãn ɩn na wa baar.

english_verse: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
translation_verse: An yi a dɩna nɩbɛ anũu na kpɩɛrɛ yir na ŋman jiir ayi. Ka ata jɛɛrɛ nɩ a ayi ka ayi mɩ jɛɛrɛ nɩ a ata.

english_verse: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Ban ooro nɩ taa, sãa na oon bie ka a bie mɩ oo a sãa, ka a ma oo a bipɔɔ ka a bipɔɔ mɩ oo a ma. Pɔɔ na oo naa a wʊ bidaba pɔɔ, ka a bidaba pɔɔ mɩ oo a wʊ sɩrɛ ma.”

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ bala, “Yɩn wa nyɛ julõjuur na ire a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ mʊrfʊ jie yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka saa na wa naa, ka a ma ɩ a lɛ.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Yɩn wa nyɛ sɛsɛbɛ mɩ na furo duoro jũu, yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka tʊl na wa naa. Ka a ma ɩ a lɛ.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: A yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme banyãna. Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a tẽe nɩ a saaju 'yɛr ma 'yɛr, ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ a dɩna 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ?

english_verse: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
translation_verse: “Bo lɛ so ka yɩn mãɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɔɔr a lɛ na tor ɛ?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: An ɩ a yɩn nɩ a yɩ sãn sʊɔ lɛ 'mataa chiine, yɩ mʊlɔ wʊ a sɔr pɔ, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, wʊn de yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ de yɩ kʊ a nɩpɔɔrɔ ka wʊ mɩ pɔɔ yɩ bar.

english_verse: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
translation_verse: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, ala be ɩ a lɛ, faa be na wa yi ka faa yab a fʊ sãn baar ɛ.”

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ ben be a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, na ba pãa mana kʊrɔ a Yesu a lɛ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate na dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a Galilee dɔɔr bamɩne, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban be a bɔɔr lofʊ jie.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ tɩɛr ɩka bala lɛ maal dɛɛr gar a Galilee nɩbɛ ba jaa, ala lɛ so ban di a dɔɔyɛ nyã bɩɩ?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: A ba ɩ lɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩn ba wa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr ɛ, yɩ mɩ na wɛ naa.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: A Yesu pãa 'yɛr, “Bo lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛtãan? Bo lɛ ɩn de man?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn mɩ baa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr ɛ, yɩ mɩ na wɛ naa.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: A sʊkpa nyã lɛ Yesu pãa lɔb kʊ ba, “Daba kɔ̃ɔ lɛ tara kɔ̃kɔ̃tɩɛ a wʊ puo pɔ, a wʊ wa chen ɩka wʊ tɩ kaa a tɩɛ wɔ̃n bɩɩ, tɩchɛ a tɩɛ ba wɔ̃ ɛ.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: Ka a 'lɔ na kaara a puo 'yɛr, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, bar wʊ ka n tuu jilʊ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ pʊɔr ka yuon waara,

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: 'lɔ wa wɔ̃ go, tɩ 'lɔ ba wa wɔ̃ ɛ, ka n ŋmaa wʊ lɔb.’ ”

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar Yesu ben a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir ãsʊɔ pɔ wile ba,

english_verse: and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for
translation_verse: be lɛ a pɔɔ be a sɩdɛbɛ na maal ka wʊ kab gʊ̃mɔ̃ yuom pie nɩ aniin (

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Yesu na nyɛ a pɔɔ a lɛ, wʊ bʊɔl wʊ naa ka wʊ wa a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Pɔɔ, a fʊ baal bar fʊ naa.”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme banyãna, a yɩ buor sʊɔ lɛ maa yuo a wʊ niin bɩɩ a wʊ bon chiin tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ɔ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ɛ?

english_verse: This woman belongs to the family of Abraham, but Satan has kept her bound for
translation_verse: Ka a pɔɔ nyãna na ɩ a Abraham bipɔɔ, a tɩɩ na lũ yuom pie nɩ aniin (

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Wʊn wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ ka a vĩ kpɛ a bala jaa na bɛrɛ wʊ a nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ chɩlɛ a tʊ̃vɩɛlɛ ala wʊn tʊ̃.

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ a Faraseemɩne bamɩne wa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Yi a ka chen jiyuo. A Hɛrɔd bʊɔrɔ na a fʊ kʊfʊ.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ jaa an ɩ, ɩn chɛnɛ na chiine a dɩna nɩ bio nɩ a dayere. Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ kʊ̃ kpi ka Jerusalɛm ba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Sɩrɛ jaa, nɩbɛ ben puor tɩ na ban wa de nie, ka a bala mɩ na be a niem mɩ wa be a puor.”

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ chen tɩ 'yɛr a mɔbaa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, ɩn dii naa a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi ka n sãa a nɩbɛ baalsɩ a dɩna nɩ bio ka a dayere daar ka n baar a n tʊ̃mɔ̃.

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Wʊ kaara fɩka kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir nɩrɛ na de bʊr a puo pɔ ka wʊ bul na wʊ ɩ tɩɛ, ka a lubil wa 'yɔ̃ɔ choor a wilsɩ ju.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Wʊ lɛ sʊʊr ban go, “Bo lɛ ɩn de a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ man?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Yesu wile naa nɩbɛ be a tẽbɛrɛ nɩ a tẽbil ala pɔ tɩ na wʊn chen Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Yɩ mɔ̃ tu a dɔ̃dɔ̃ bile pɔ kpɛ, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka nɩyɔɔ lɛ na mʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kpɛ tɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: A yir sʊɔ na wa ir pɔɔ a pãn var yɩ bar be a yõo, ka yɩ ara kpooro sʊɔrɔ, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, yuo sɩ a pãn.’ Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, ‘N ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, bɩɩ a jie yɩn yi ɛ.’

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka wʊn 'yɛr, ‘N ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, bɩɩ a jie yɩn yi ɛ, yɩ tɔɔ yi tɩ chɛ ma a yɩn na tʊ̃nɔ̃ a dɛbɛ.’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: “Be lɛ yɩn kõnõ ɔɔrɔ a nyɩmɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a Abraham, Isaakɩ nɩ Jekɔb nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa be a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a yɩn 'lɔ be a yõo.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ na yin a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie, a mʊrfʊ jie, a jũu nɩ a par na ba wa jãnɛ dire be a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Gboo yɩn Jerusalɛm dẽme, yɩn lɛ ma kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ tɩ lɔɔrɔ a bala Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ. N ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n kaal a fʊ bibiir 'mataa nɩtãa lɛ nʊ̃ɔ na ma kaal a wʊ bil 'mataa a wʊ pɩrɛ pɔ, tɩ yɩ maa sɔɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Yɩ nyɛ, a yɩ yir na lɩɛb dakpolo, tɩ ka n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kõ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ yɩn na 'yɛr, ‘Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃!’ ” (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka daba kɔ̃ɔ na tara a baal vʊ̃ naa ara a wʊ niem.

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ, “A tara na sɔr ka fʊn sãa nɩrɛ na bɩɛrɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir bɩɩ a ba tara sɔr ɛ?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka ba ɩ jaa gbulo, ka Yesu de a nũu dɔɔl a daba sãa a wʊ baal tɩ bar wʊ sɔr.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Baa lɛ tara 'yɛr na 'yɛr ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Yesu nyɛn a nɩbɛ bala ban bʊɔl ɩka ba wa di, ka ba tuur a nɩbɛrɛ jaa jiir jãnɛ, ka wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Nɩrɛ wa bʊɔl fʊ pɔɔ kulfʊ jie, fʊ̃ʊ wa chen ta tɩ jãnɛ a nɩbɛrɛ jaa jie ɛ, amɩne wʊ bʊɔl nɩrɛ na gar fʊ ka wʊn wa.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Bojũu, nɩrɛ jaa na 'mɔɔrɔ wʊ mãɛ saaju, Nãaŋmɩn na siir wʊ naa, 'lɔ mɩ na siir a wʊ mãɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn saaju.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Ka Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na bʊɔl wʊ, “Fʊ̃ʊ wa bʊɔl nɩrɛ ɩka wʊ wa di, taa wa bʊɔlɔ a fʊ barmɩne, bɩɩ a fʊ yɛɛr, bɩɩ a fʊ bal nɩbɛ, bɩɩ a fʊ tɔ dẽme bala na ɩ tara dẽme ɛ. Ala ɩ fʊ ɩn a lɛ, ban bʊɔl fʊn daar kɔ̃ɔ tɩ yaa a sãn.

english_verse: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
translation_verse: Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ. Ban ba tara bon na yab fʊ ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na nyɛ sãyar a lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na wa ir a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ wa 'yɛr, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, n ɩ naa a lɛ fʊn 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ, tɩchɛ ka a jie chɛnɛ be be.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Ka a bala na lɔ̃ɔ dire ãsʊɔ na wõ a nɩtɔ̃, na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, “An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a 'lɔ na wa jãnɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ dire.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a Yesu lɔb a sʊkpa nyã, “Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ chɔɔrɔ na wʊ bʊɔlɔ nɩyɔɔ ɩka ba wa di.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ɩka wʊ chen tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl ɩka wʊ maal yele a jaa baar.

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a ba ãsʊɔ jaa 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ̃ nyɛ jie ɛ. Ka a 'lɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ ta ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘N dãn popaalaa naa chiine ɩka n tɩ kaa, n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɩ ɛ.’

english_verse: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: Ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ 'yɛr, ‘N dãn narkʊɔrsɩ pie naa jãna n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɩ ɛ.’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: “A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɩɛb wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ kʊ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ka a suur kpɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃, ‘Lɩɛb chen fɔfɔɔ jaa a tẽe sãlsɔɔ nɩ a kõkorsɩ pɔ, na fʊ tɩ bʊɔl a nɔ̃n dẽme, a gbɛrsɩ, jɔ̃n nɩ a nɩbaalsɩ waan.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ pãa lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃, ‘Yi chen a sɔɩ pɔ, nɩ a sɛɛr pɔ tɩ bʊɔl nɩbɛ waan ka n yir wa sɛɛ.

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ lɛ̃ maa ɛ.’ ”

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Yesu nɩ a nɩyɔɔ 'mantaa chiine, ka wʊ lɩɛb kaa ba tɩ 'yɛr,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Ala ɩ nɩrɛ na wa naa a n sɛ̃, na waa 'laar a wʊ sãa nɩ a wʊ ma, nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ, a wʊ bibiir, a wʊ yɛɛr nɩ a wʊ yɛpɔbɔ 'matãan a wʊ mãɛ nyɛvʊr ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ba de a wʊ daa tuo turo nɩ ma ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
translation_verse: “Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ bʊɔrɔ na yir kpɛ̃ɛ mɛfʊ, a ba ɩ wʊn den nie jãnɛ guor kaa ala ɩ wʊ tara naa a libie na tʊ̃ɔ mɛn baar ɛ?

english_verse: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
translation_verse: Na wʊ taa kpar a yir tɩ ba tʊ̃ɔ baar ɛ, ka a wʊ taaba nyɛ laara wʊ

english_verse: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
translation_verse: 'yɛrɛ, ‘Jaal piel a mɛfʊ tɩ na waa baar ɛ.’

english_verse: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɛ, wʊn tʊ̃n nɩbɛ ka ba chen tɩ 'maal a nãa 'lɔ na chɛnɛ be a tɛɛr.

english_verse: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ mɩ lɛ, a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa baa bar a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

english_verse: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
translation_verse: “Nyaar ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a wʊ nʊ̃ɔ wa baar, ŋmɩnɛ ban lɛ ɩ wʊ ka wʊ lɛ nʊ̃mɔ̃ go?

english_verse: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
translation_verse: Wa vɩɛl kʊ a tãpuor tãna ɛ, ban lɔb bar. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara toor wõnõ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ wõ naa.”

english_verse: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
translation_verse: Ɩn lɩɛb kul a n sãa sɛ̃ naa tɩ 'yɛr wʊ, n maal dɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ nɩ a fʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
translation_verse: Ka a bile sʊɔ wa 'yɛr kʊ a sãa, ‘N sãa, põ a fʊ kpĩi bomo ir a n bon kʊ ma.’ Ka a sãa de a wʊ kpĩi bomo põ ba.

english_verse: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
translation_verse: Wʊn wa sɔ̃ɔ a jaa baar, ka a kɔ̃ lo a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ, ka a wõm piel kpɛ wʊ.

english_verse: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
translation_verse: A lõpodierbe nɩ a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ lɔ̃ɔn taa na chɛl a Yesu 'yɛr.

english_verse: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe hũhũnõ 'yɛrɛ, “A daba nyã diere naa tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ ka ba 'mataa dire.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: A Yesu pãa lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba,

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: 'Lɔ wa nyɛ a pɛr nʊ̃ɔ ma kpɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ 'mɔɔ dɔɔl bɔɔ ju

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: kulo nɩ a yir, tɩ pãa bʊɔlɔ a wʊ barmɩne nɩ a wʊ taaba lɔ̃ɔ taa tɩ 'yɛr, ‘Yɩ pʊɔ ma chɩlɛ, bojũu n nyɛ naa a n pɛr 'lɔ na bɔr.’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way there is more happiness in heaven because of one sinner who turns to God than over
translation_verse: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, chɩl kpɛ̃ɛ na ben a saaju a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen na lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr gar a kɔbaa forbir bala na ba tara tɩɛr lɩɛb fʊ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: “Bɩɩ pɔɔ buor lɛ tara libibie pie ka ãsʊɔ bɔr, wʊ kʊ̃ chʊm bũu den saar pɩɩr a die wʊ jaa na wʊ wa tɩ nyɛ wʊ bɩɩ?

english_verse: Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.”
translation_verse: 'Lɔ wa nyɛ wʊ, wʊ ma bʊɔl a wʊ barmɩne nɩ a wʊ taaba lɔ̃ɔ taa tɩ 'yɛr, ‘Yɩ pʊɔ ma chɩlɛ, n nyɛ naa a n libir na bɔr.’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
translation_verse: Lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, nʊ̃ɔ mɩ na kpɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ ala ɩ a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr.”

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Yesu chɛnɛ na 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba, “Daba lɛ tara wʊ dabil ayi.

english_verse: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
translation_verse: A ba kor ɛ, ka a bile 'lɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa yi chiin tẽyuo pɔ, na wʊ tɩ bũbur a bonsɩ sɔ̃ɔ a jaa bar.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Wʊ chen a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ yir tɩ bɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ ma tʊ̃ wʊ ka wʊ chen a puo pɔ tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ dor bʊ̃diir.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: A jɔ̃ pɛbɛ ala ma ɩrɛ wʊn vɔlaa ka wʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ 'wɔb tɩɩ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ jaa maa kʊ wʊ bon ɛ.

english_verse: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
translation_verse: “Ka a yɛ̃ wa wʊ ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘N sãa tara na tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ŋmɩnɛ a ba ma di bʊ̃diir faa, tɩchɛ ka a maa wa kpire nɩ kɔ̃ ka?

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: N ba lɛ chɛnɛ sɛɛ ka ban bʊɔl ma a fʊ bie ɛ, maal ma ka n ɩ a fʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ.’

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Lɛ wʊ ir kulo a sãa sɛ̃. Tɩchɛ wʊn chɛnɛ be a nɩdaa waara, a sãa nyɛ wʊ naa ka nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ ka wʊ jotuor a bie kõr tɩ puor wʊ.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: Ka a bidaba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘N sãa, n maal dɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ nɩ a fʊ sɛ̃, n ba lɛ sɛɛn ka ban bʊɔl ma a fʊ bie go ɛ.’

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a sãa 'yɛr kʊ a gbãgbaar, ‘Yɩ pɔɔ fɔɔ tɩ de fuu nyã na vɩɛlɛ wa su wʊ, na yɩ de bɔ̃bile 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a nubir pɔ, na yɩ de nafɔbɔ wa su wʊ.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Yɩ tɩ kaa nele na 'wɔr na yɩ waan wa kʊ ka sɩ 'wɔb tɩ chɩlɛ.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Bojũu a n bidaba nyãna dɔ̃ɔ kpi tɩ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr, wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bɔr tɩ sɩ lɛ nyɛ wʊ.’ Ka ba piel chɩlɛ.

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ ka a bidakpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ be a puo pɔ, na wʊ wa ir waara, wʊn wa gbʊrɔ a yir wʊ wõn bãnɛ nɩ chɛlsɩ.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a gbãgbaa ãsʊɔ na wʊ sʊʊr a yelnyɩnɛ na be be.

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: Ka a gbãgbaa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘A fʊ yɛbile lɛ wa ka a fʊ sãa ɩ a ba kʊ nel'wɔr jaa, bojũu wʊ nyɛn a wʊ bie na lɩɛb wa nɩ lãafɩɛ tɩ chɛnɛ kpɛ̃mɛ.’

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: A suur kpɛ a yɛkarma ka wʊ jɛ, waa kpɛ ɛ. Ka a sãa yi 'maala wʊ ɩka wʊ kpɛ.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a sãa, ‘Nyɛ lɛ ɩn wõnõ a fʊ toor naa tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ fʊ a yuom anyãna a jaa, n ba gɔ̃ɔ fʊ nɛɛ jaa ɛ. Tɩchɛ faa mɩ wa kʊ ma bule gba ɩka, n mɩ kʊ ka maa nɩ a n barmɩne mɩ 'mataa di tɩ chɩlɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a fʊ bibawõno nyãna na de a fʊ bonsɩ yin ka a 'lɔ nɩ a pɔɔ baar tɩ 'mataa sɔ̃ɔ bar, tɩ lɩɛb wa a yir ka fʊ ir kʊ nel'wɔr kʊ wʊ.’

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: Ka a sãa 'yɛr, ‘Fʊ̃ʊ 'lɔ, fʊ ben a n sɛ̃, ka a bomo a jaa ɩn tara ɩ a fʊ bonsɩ.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a sɛɛ naa ka sɩn dãɛnɛ na sɩ chɩlɛ bojũu a fʊ yɛbile nyãna dɔ̃ɔ kpi tɩ lɛ ɩ vʊr go, na wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bɔr tɩ sɩ lɛ nyɛ wʊ.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo ɩka, “Taraa sʊ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, na wʊ tara nɩrɛ na kaara wʊ a bonsɩ, na wʊ pʊ̃pʊrɔ bara.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Ka wʊ bʊɔl wʊ waan wa sʊʊr, ‘N wõn ban 'yɛrɛ a fʊ 'yɛr yʊɔrɔ nɩ, man a lɛ fʊn tʊ̃ kʊ ma, bojũu fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ.’

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ mãɛ, ‘Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn pãa ɩ a pʊ̃pãanyã? A n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ faa man a tʊ̃mɔ̃, n mɩ ba lɛ kpɛ̃mɛ na tʊ̃ɔ kɔ ɛ, a mɩ na ɩn vĩ ɩn na sʊɔrɔ.

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: N bɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɩn na ɩ maa ba lɛ wa tara tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka nɩbɛ chɛnɛ de ma a ba yie.’

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: Tɩ na wʊ lɛ sʊʊr a ayi sʊɔ, ‘A ŋmɩnɛ fʊ mɩ di a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sãn?’ Ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘Chi bɔtɔr kɔbaa (

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ dãn a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna na ba tara yelmãɛ ɛ, bojũu wʊ tɩɛr nɩdaar. A wɛr nyã nɩbɛ tara na yɛ̃ a ba taaba jie gar a chãa nɩbɛ.

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: “N 'yɛr na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, yɩ de a wɛr nyã bʊ̃tarsɩ na yɩ ŋman barmɩne ala wa baar, Nãaŋmɩn na de yɩn kpɛ̃n a yir kpo kpo 'lɔ pɔ.

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: Ka a Abraham 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Ala ɩ ba kʊ̃ chɛl a lɛ a Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na 'yɛr lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr ɛ, nɩrɛ sɔɔ ir kũu pɔ tɩ 'yɛr an ɩn pɔrɔ.’ ”

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: A Faraseemɩne na nʊ̃ libie na wõ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr, ka ba laara wʊ.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ ma maal yɩ mãɛ nɩtorsɩ a nɩbɛ niem, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔn a yɩ tɩɛr. Bon 'lɔ nɩbɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃chiiraa.

english_verse: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
translation_verse: “A Mosesɩ Nɛɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ 'yɛr wa tãn a Jɔɔn sɔ̃ɔ. An yi a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊ jaa, ba 'yɛr naa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr sʊ̃n ka a ãsʊɔ jaa mʊɔrɔ wʊ sɔr, ɩka wʊ kpɛ.

english_verse: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
translation_verse: An ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ kʊ a saaju nɩ a tẽe ka a po tal, gar a Nɛɛ 'yɛr bir na yi lo.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ jaa na dii a wʊ pɔɔ bar tɩ kul pɔɔ yuo, wʊ maal dɛɛr, ka a daba 'lɔ mɩ na kul a pɔɔ a sɩrɛ na dii bar, mɩ maal dɛɛr.

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: “Tara sʊɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, na wʊ ma su kparsɩ nɩ fuu pɩɛl na wʊ dire bʊ̃nʊ̃n bɩbir jaa.

english_verse: But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house.
translation_verse: Ka a sʊsʊɔrɔ kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Lajarusɩ mɩ be be na ɩ natɩnara ma jãnɛ a tara sʊɔ dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

english_verse: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
translation_verse: Na wʊ ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ nyɛ a bʊ̃di pʊr pʊr ala a tara sʊɔ na dire ka a ŋmara loro, ka a baar gba ma wa lɛnɛ a wʊ natɩɛ.

english_verse: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
translation_verse: A sʊsʊɔrɔ nyãna wa kpi naa, ka a malakasɩ wa de wʊ chiine tɩ bin a Abraham sɛ̃. Ka a tara sʊɔ 'lɔ mɩ wa kpi ka ba ũu wʊ.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: “A be a kpĩisɩ tẽe, ka a ɩ dɔɔyɛ jie lɛ, ka wʊ gbã nyɛ Abraham tɛɛr jaa ka a Lajarusɩ jãnɛ a wʊ lõboor.

english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl, ‘N sãa Abraham, kaa n nɩbaal tɩ na fʊ tʊ̃ a Lajarusɩ ka wʊ 'me kʊ̃ɔ nɩ nubir wa 'maan ma jãlbir, bojũu a wõnõ ma naa ka a bũu pɔ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Abraham 'yɛr, ‘N bie, tɩɛr kaa fʊn dɔ̃ɔ be a wɛr pɔ dire nʊ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ ka a Lajarusɩ mɩ dire dɔɔyɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã, wʊ mɩ dire naa a nʊ̃ɔ tɩchɛ ka fʊ mɩ dire a dɔɔyɛ.

english_verse: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
translation_verse: “Ka wʊ lɔb nũu 'yɛr, ‘Yãayãaabɩlɛ lɛ n sʊɔrɔ, n sãa, tʊ̃ a Lajarusɩ ka wʊ kul a be a sãa yir,

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: n tara na yɛɛr anũu wʊ tɩ kpãa ba ka ba taa ɩrɛ mɩ wa wa a dɔɔyɛ nyã pɔ ɛ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: Ka Abraham 'yɛr, ‘Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ a lɛ ban sɛb bin ben a ba sɛ̃, ba chɛlɛ a ala ban 'yɛrɛ.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘Kai n sãa Abraham, ala ɩ nɩrɛ lɛ ir a kũu pɔ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba ban lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa ba lɩɛb wa ɩka wʊ wa puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan ka a sãan nyã ba lɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn tara sɔɔfʊ jaa pʊʊr fɩka kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir, yɩn tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a tɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyãna, ‘Vʊ̃ɔ tɩ ba man pɔ, ka wʊ tu a lɛ yɩn 'yɛr.’

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: “Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ tara na gbãgbaa ka wʊ kʊɔrɔ bɩɩ ŋmaara piir, 'lɔ wa yi a tʊ̃mɔ̃ jie wa, fʊn 'yɛr wʊ naa ɩka wʊ wa jãnɛ di ɔ?

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Bɩɩ ka nɩbɛ 'yɛr, yɩ nyɛ wʊ ka, bɩɩ yɩ nyɛ wʊ kasɛn, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ben a yɩ pɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo, “Sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ waara na a yɩn wa bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie nɩ nɩbir, tɩchɛ yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma ɛ.

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Wʊn puor naa a gbãgbaa nyãna wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ maal bɩɩ?

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, “Yelsɩ ala na vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ ɩ bier ben be na wa, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jie an tu wa ben pɔ'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ pɔ.

english_verse: would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their neck.
translation_verse: A nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, bala 'mɔɔ nɩɛr lũ yɔɔl a nyĩe pɔ daa lɔb a man kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, ala sa wʊn na vɛ̃ ka a bʊ̃bil banyãna ãsʊɔ ɩ bier.

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Yɩ guro yɩ mãɛ, ala ɩ fʊ yɛbɛ maal fʊn dɛɛr gɔ̃ wʊ, 'lɔ wa de a bɔrfʊ vɛ̃ kʊ wʊ.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ a bɩbir bʊ̃'yen pɔ lɛ wʊ maal fʊ dɛɛr gbɛɛ ayopõi tɩ mɩ lɩɛb wa a gbɛɛ ayopõi wa de bɔrfʊ, vɛ̃ kʊ wʊ.”

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ 'yɛr, “Sɩ Soro, pʊɔ sɩ sɔɔfʊ.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: A ba ɩ fʊn 'yɛr wʊ naa, ‘Maal a bʊ̃diir fɔɔ na fʊ chɔɔr a fʊ mãɛ wa ara gu ma, ka n di tɩ nyũ baar ka fʊ mɩ pãa di tɩ nyũ ɛ.’

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: A yɩn mɩ lɛ lɛ, yɩn wa maal ala a jaa Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ, yɩn 'yɛr naa, ‘Saa sɛɛn ɛ, gbãgbaar lɛ sɩ ɩ na sɩ maal a lɛ na ɩ a sɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃.’ ”

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu den a sɔr chiine a Jerusalɛm, wʊ kpɛ̃n a Samariya nɩ a Galilee chara pɔ.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Na wʊ chiine kpɛ a tẽbile ãsʊɔ pɔ, ka a kɔ̃kɔ̃n pie tuor wʊ a be. Ba ara na tɛɛr jaa

english_verse: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: tɩ gboolo bʊɔlɔ, “Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Yesu, kaa sɩ nɩbaal.”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Wʊn nyɛ ba a lɛ, na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ chen tɩ wiil a yɩ mãɛ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo.” Ban chen a lɛ, ka a ɩ ba sa naa.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: A ba ãsʊɔ na nyɛ ka a ɩ wʊ sa naa, wʊ lɩɛb wa de nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na wʊ pɛɛrɛ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: Wʊ lon kpa a Yesu gbɛɛ par tɩ puor wʊ, Samariya lɛ wʊ yi.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr, “A ba ɩ pie lɛ sa ɛ? Ka nyɩnɛ a pie forbir be?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ir kulo, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ sa fʊ naa.”

english_verse: The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky.
translation_verse: A maa Nɩsaal Bie bɩbir na kaara naa fɩka a saa na ma nyɩɩr a saaju yi a lõboor nyã tɩ gbem a lõboor ãsʊɔ.

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ na 'yɛr yɩ naa, ‘Kasɛn lɛ wʊ be,’ bɩɩ ‘Ka lɛ wʊ be.’ Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa joro turo ba chiine be ɛ.

english_verse: When Lot lived, people were also eating and drinking. They were buying, selling, planting, and building.
translation_verse: “Lɛ mɩ lɛ a kaara a Lotɩ sɔ̃ɔ, nɩbɛ dire naa, na ba nyuro, na ba daara lɩɛb daara, na ba bʊrɔ na ba mɩɛrɛ nɩ yie.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, “Sɩ Soro, nyɩnɛ?” Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Jie bʊ̃kũu na be, be lɛ sɩdumosɩ ma loro.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus when God's kingdom would come. He answered, “God's kingdom isn't something you can see.
translation_verse: A Faraseemɩne lɛ sʊʊr naa Yesu ɩka dabor lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ na wa, ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kʊ̃ wa ka ba bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ɩn den nie di dɔɔyɛ yele yɔɔ jaa pɔ, ka a dɩna nɩbɛ tɔr ma.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: “Lɛ an dɔ̃ɔ kaara a Nowa sɔ̃ɔ, lɛ mɩ lɛ an kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie sɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ dire naa na ba nyuro ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ kulo taa, tɩ tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ a Nowa na kpɛ a gbor kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, ka a kʊ̃ɔ paal wa sɔ̃ɔ ba jaa bar.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a bɩbir 'lɔ a Lotɩ nɩ wa yi a Sodom bũu nɩ kʊsɩlirsɩ yin saaju lo pɔɔ a nɩbɛ ka ba jaa kpi.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie bɩbir mɩ na kaara.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: “A bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, nɩrɛ na be a wʊ gar ju, ka a wʊ bonsɩ be a die pɔ, wʊ kʊ̃ suu tɩ de ɛ. Lɛ mɩ lɛ nɩrɛ na be puo pɔ kʊ̃ kul tɩ de bon ɛ.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Yɩ tɩɛr a Lotɩ pɔɔ.

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a wa nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na lɔb a wʊ nyɛvʊr bar, wʊn tara wʊ naa.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: “N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, nɩbɛ ayi na gãn gaa jie, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de bʊ̃'yen tɩ chɛ bʊ̃'yen bar.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Dɔɔr ayi mɩ na ben puo pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de ãsʊɔ tɩ chɛ bʊ̃'yen bar.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Yesu pãa lɔb sʊkpa kʊ a wʊ poturbo na wʊ wiil ba a lɛ ban na sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn taa baala ɛ.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: Wʊ 'yɛr naa, “A tẽe kɔ̃ɔ pɔ, 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be, waa joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ɛ, waa mɩ kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal ɛ.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Ka a pɔɔ kʊɔr kɔ̃ɔ mɩ be be a tẽe pɔ, na wʊ ma waara a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛrɛ, ‘'Yɛr faa ma a n sãn sʊɔ sɛ̃.’

english_verse: For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, “Even though I don't fear God or care about people,
translation_verse: Bojũu, gbɛɛ aŋmɩnɛ jaa lɛ wʊ ma tɔr, tɩ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ mãɛ, ‘N gba ba joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ɛ, bɩɩ kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal lɛ.

english_verse: I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ bojũu, a pɔɔ kʊɔr nyãna na yʊɔrɔ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma, ɩn tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ka wʊ ta yʊɔrɔ bala ma ɛ.’ ”

english_verse: The Lord said: Think about what that crooked judge said.
translation_verse: Ka a Soro 'yɛr, “Yɩ nyɛ lɛ a 'yɛr tɔɔr filefile nyã na 'yɛr.

english_verse: Won't God protect his chosen ones who pray to him day and night? Won't he be concerned for them?
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr faa a wʊ nɩ-irsɩ na kõnõ kʊrɔ wʊ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ jaa ɛ? Bɩɩ wʊn lɩɛrɛ ban bara?

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr a ba 'yɛr fɔɔ jaa kʊ ba. A maa Nɩsaal Bie wa lɩɛb wa a wɛr pɔ, ɩn nyɛn nɩbɛ ka ba tara sɔɔfʊ ɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ lɔb sʊkpa de man a nɩbɛ bamɩne na tɩɛr ɩka ba ɩn nɩtorsɩ tɩ kaara a ba taaba pɔrɔ.

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: Ka a Farasee nɩrɛ 'lɔ ir ara, tɩ sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ nɩ, ‘Nãaŋmɩn n puor fʊn yãan, nɩ ɩn ba kaara a nɩbɛ bamɩne kaar ɛ, nɩfarba, tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ, sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ, bɩɩ a lõpodiere nyã kaar gba ɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: A lõpodiere nyã yele tor naa a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, ka wʊ lɩɛb kul gar, a Farasee nɩrɛ 'lɔ. Bojũu nɩrɛ jaa na 'mɔɔrɔ wʊ mãɛ saaju, Nãaŋmɩn na siir wʊ naa, tɩ nɩrɛ nyã mɩ na siir a wʊ mãɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn saaju.”

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔ waara nɩ bibibil a Yesu sɛ̃ ɩka wʊ sɩɩr ba, ka a poturbo na nyɛ a lɛ na ba mĩi 'yɔ̃ɔ ba.

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: wʊn nyɛn dɔɔl taa a dɩna nɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ na wa lɔɔn nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Yesu tɔɔn a Pie nɩ Ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Sɩ ben duoro a Jerusalɛm, ala a jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb 'yɛr a maa Nɩsaal Bie 'yɛr, an tɩ ɩ naa.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: A nɩdiere kɔ̃ɔ sʊʊr a Yesu, “Wiwiilsʊ̃, bo lɛ ɩn ɩ naa nyɛ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Ka a daba 'yɛr, “A anyãna a jaa lɛ n turo a n bile sɔ̃ɔ wa tãn dɩna.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Yesu na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “A chɛ fʊn yelbʊ̃'yen, tɩ da a fʊ bonsɩ a jaa, de põ nɔ̃n dẽme ka a fʊ tara bomo be a saaju, tɩ na fʊ pãa wa turo ma.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: A daba na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, bojũu tara sʊɔ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Yesu kaa wʊn tɩ 'yɛr, “An ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ a tara sʊɔ na kpɛ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, an ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ nyɔɔma na kpɛ kparpĩi bɔɔ pɔ, gar tara sʊɔ na kpɛ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Ka a bala na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ sʊʊr a Yesu, “An lɛ na pãa nyɛ faafʊ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Ala na ɩ tuo nɩrɛ sɛ̃, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ yele jaa ba ɩ tuo ɛ.”

english_verse: Peter said, “Remember, we left everything to be your followers!”
translation_verse: A Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Sɩn bar bonsɩ a jaa, tɩ wa turo fʊ.”

english_verse: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
translation_verse: Ban tɩ nyɔɔ man kʊ a nɩbɛ bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba la ma, tʊrɔ ma, na ba chɩr nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma, na ba ge ma, tɩ kʊ ma.

english_verse: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
translation_verse: Ka a ber ata daar ka n ir ɩ vʊr go.”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
translation_verse: Yesu na wa gbʊrɔ a Jeriko, jɔ̃ɔ jɛ̃n be a sɔr kõkoor na wʊ sʊɔrɔ.

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: Wʊn wa wõ a nɩyɔɔ na chiine tala, a wʊ sʊrɔ, ɩka bo lɛ ɩrɛ.

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Yesu na yi Najaretɩ lɛ tala.”

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: A wʊ gboolo, “Yesu, David Bidaba, kaa a n nɩbaal.”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Ka a bala na de a nie tãn wʊ 'yɛrɛ pɔɔ a nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ chɛnɛ gboolo bʊɔlɔ, “Yesu, David Bidaba, kaa n nɩbaal.”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Ka Yesu tɛɛ ara tɩ 'yɛr ɩka ba tara a daba waan. Lɛ a jɔ̃ɔ na wa gbʊr a Yesu ka wʊ sʊʊr wʊ,

english_verse: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
translation_verse: “Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n ɩ kʊ fʊ?” Ka a jɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, “N Soro n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n nyɛrɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Nyɛrɛ a fʊ sɔɔfʊ sa fʊ naa.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Ajienaa ka a ɩ wʊ nyɛrɛ naa, na wʊ dãna Nãaŋmɩn turo nɩ a Yesu. A nɩbɛ ba jaa na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba mɩ dãna naa a Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ de nie jo chen tɩ do kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ ju ɩka wʊ nyɛ a Yesu an ɩ a be lɛ wʊ tu tala.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Yesu na wa ta a tɩɛ pĩlem, wʊ dʊɔn bʊɔl wʊ, “Jakiyas wa suu fɔɔ, a fʊ yir lɛ ɩn gã dɩna.”

english_verse: Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka a Jakiyas suu fɔɔ jaa na wʊ tuor de a Yesu nɩ pʊpɛl.

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Jakiyas ir ara tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, “Nyɛ n Soro, ɩn põ naa a n bʊ̃tarsɩ ŋmaa, naa kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme, ala ɩ n mɩ fɔl nɩrɛ jaa bon, ɩn yab a lɛ dɔɔl gbɛɛ anaar.”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Dɩna faafʊ wan a yir nyã pɔ, bojũu a daba nyã mɩ ɩn a Abraham bie.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie wa naa ɩka n wa bɔ na n faa a bala na bɔr.”

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: Ka ba 'yɛr, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, pie lɛ wʊ tara.’

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a wʊ tẽe dẽme 'laar wʊ, ir nɩbɛ tʊ̃ wʊn chen baar ka ba tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Baa bʊɔrɔ a daba nyã ɩka wʊ wa ɩ a ba nãa ɛ.’

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: “Yɩ chen a tẽbile 'lɔ a yɩ niem, yɩn wa kpɩɛrɛ a tẽe pɔ yɩn nyɛn bʊ̃pɔlbile ban lũ, baa do wʊ jaa sɛrɛ ɛ, yɩ for wʊ waan.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: A 'lɔ na de nie wa 'yɛr naa, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, a fʊ libir dɔɔn pie dɔɔl.’

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: A ayi sʊɔ wa 'yɛr, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, a fʊ libir mɩ dɔɔn anũu dɔɔl.’

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: “Ka ata sʊɔ mɩ wa 'yɛr, ‘Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyɛ a fʊ libir ɩn lũ 'yɔ̃ɔ fɔbaa pɔ bin.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: Bo lɛ so ka faa de a n libir tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a be a libie binfʊ jie ɛ, ɩn ben ta wa, n kʊ̃ naa de ka bon dɔɔl ɛ?’

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala na ara a be, ‘Yɩ faa a libir 'lɔ a wʊ sɛ̃, na yɩ kʊ a 'lɔ na tara a libie pie.’

english_verse: The king replied, “Those who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: ) Nyã'maar ben a saaju, ka a tɩɩr be a saaju jaa saaju.”

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a n dɔ̃dẽme bala na ba bʊɔrɔ ma ɩka n ɩ a ba nãa ɛ, yɩ nyɔɔr ba waan wa kʊ a ka a n niem.’ ”

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yesu na 'yɛr a anyãna baar wʊ de ban nie ka ba duoro a Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: A lɛ wʊn wa gbʊrɔ a Betefagɩ nɩ a Betanɩ, tɔ̃ɔ ben be ka ba bʊɔlɔ Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ, wʊ tʊ̃ naa a wʊ poturbo ayi tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba.

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
translation_verse: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ naa, ‘Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃pɔlbile?’ Yɩ 'yɛr wʊ, ‘A sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ wʊ.’ ”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: A bala wʊn tʊ̃ chen tɩ nyɛ a lɛ nɛ wʊn 'yɛr ba.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Ban wa foro a bʊ̃pɔlbile, a bʊ̃pɔlbile dẽme sʊʊr ba naa, “Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃pɔlbile?”

english_verse: They answered, “The Lord needs it.”
translation_verse: A ba 'yɛr, “A sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ wʊ.”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Ba waan naa a bʊ̃pɔlbile wa kʊ a Yesu, na ba 'yɩɛr a ba fɔɔr dɔɔl a bʊ̃pɔlbile ju, tɩ nyɔɔ a Yesu ka wʊ do jãnɛ.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: A lɛ wʊn chiine, lɛ mɩ lɛ a nɩbɛ 'yɩɛrɛ a fɔɔr den a sɔr.

english_verse: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
translation_verse: Wʊn chen wa gbʊrɔ a jie a sɔr na suu a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju, a nɩyɔɔ bala na turo wʊ piel chɩlɛ nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na ba pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala a jaa ban nyɛ jũu.

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Ka a Faraseemɩne na be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ, 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, “Wiwile tãn a fʊ poturbo a ba bar a gɔ̃n.”

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “If they keep quiet, these stones will start shouting.”
translation_verse: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ala ɩ ba bar a gɔ̃n, kʊsɩbɛ na pɔɔrɔ naa gɔ̃mɔ̃.”

english_verse: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
translation_verse: Yesu na wa tara a Jerusalɛm na wʊ nyɛ a tẽe wʊ kõn kʊ wʊ,

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: tɩ 'yɛr, “Ala ɩ a fʊ̃ʊ, fʊ̃ʊ gba ta bɔ̃ɔ a bon 'lɔ a dɩna bɩbir nɩ taa waan nyã'maar, tɩchɛ a sɔɔl naa a fʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
translation_verse: A bɩbir na wa vɩɛ yɩ naa, ka a yɩ dɔ̃dẽme wa jilʊ̃ yɩ gʊɔr gʊɔr a jie wʊ jaa.

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Ban nɛb yɩn ŋmɛr nɩ a yɩ bibiir nɩ a yɩ tẽe ba kʊ̃ wa bar kʊsɩr ka wʊ dɔɔl wʊ tɔ ju ɛ, bojũu yaa tɩɛr tɔ a sɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na waara a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Wʊ pãa tɩ kpɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ na wʊ piel dire a bala na daara bara.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ‘A n yir na ɩ naa a puorfʊ jie,’ (Isaiya

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Bɩbir jaa lɛ wʊ wile ba be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bʊɔrɔ a wʊ kʊfʊ.

english_verse: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ lɛ ban na ɩ ɛ, bojũu nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɔ̃ɔn toor chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: A nɛbɛr nɩbɛ banyãna sʊʊr wʊ naa, “Wiwile sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka fʊ ma 'yɛr naa na fʊ wiil a lɛ na tor, faa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩbɛ gar taa ɛ, tɩchɛ na fʊ wile a nɩbɛ Nãaŋmɩn sɔr nɩ yelmãɛ.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: A Saduseemɩne na 'yɛr ɩka a kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, bamɩne wan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr wʊ.

english_verse: When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? All seven brothers had married her.
translation_verse: An be a lɛ, bala wa ir a kũu pɔ, buor sʊɔ pɔɔ lɛ wʊn ɩ, an ɩ ba jaa ayopõi kul wʊ naa?”

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Na ba sʊʊr wʊ, “'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a jie fʊn nyɛ a sɔr maala nɩ a anyãna, an kʊ fʊ a sɔr nyã?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: A Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ an lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ nɩrɛ lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ?”

english_verse: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: A ba lɩɛb guor taa 'yɛrɛ, “Ala ɩ sɩ ben 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, wʊn lɩɛb sʊʊr sɩ, ŋmɩnɛ a saa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ?

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a ba 'yɛr, “Saa bɔ̃ɔ a jie an yi ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: A Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a jie ɩn nyɛ a sɔr maala nɩ a anyãna ɛ.”

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ba nʊ̃ naa fuu vɛɛsɩ sufʊ, na ba nʊ̃ ɩka ba puoro ba a dar pɔ. A ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie a niem lɛ ba ma jãnɛ na ba jãnɛ a jibɛrɛ jie bala wa lɔ̃ɔ dire.

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story: A man once planted a vineyard and rented it out. Then he left the country for a long time.
translation_verse: Yesu chɛnɛ na 'yɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛ na wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba, “Daba lɛ bʊr a tɩwɔ̃nsɩ de kʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ba kaara, tɩ yɔ chen tɛɛr na wʊ tɩ kor be jaa.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Ka a tɩɩr wɔ̃mɔ̃ mʊɔfʊ sɔ̃ɔ wa ta ka wʊ ir a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ tʊ̃ a bala na kaara wʊ a puo ka ba pɔr a wɔ̃mɔ̃ amɩne tɩr. Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bala ŋme wʊ bar ka wʊ kul nũjaal.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na wa nyɛ a bidaba na waara a ba guor taa 'yɛr, ‘Nyã lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃dire, yɩ ɩ a sɩ kʊ wʊ bar tɩ de a bonsɩ.’

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: Wʊn wa kʊ naa a nɩbɛ banyãna bar, tɩ de a puo kʊ nɩyor.” Lɛ a nɩbɛ na wa wõ a 'yɛrbir nyã a ba 'yɛr, “A kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a lɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ kaara sɔr 'lɔ ban na tu nyɔɔ wʊ fɔɔ, bojũu ba bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka bala lɛ wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa gbɛb, tɩchɛ na ba joro a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ.

english_verse: Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Ba jʊrɔ naa Yesu, na ba ir nɩbɛ tʊ̃ ka ba tɩ maala fɩka nɩsʊ̃n tɩ jʊrɔ wʊ. Ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba wõ a wʊ nɛɛ 'yɛrbie de nyɔɔn wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ nũu pɔ, na ɩ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ.

english_verse: “Show me a coin.” Then he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: “Yɩ wiil ma a libikur. An ju ŋmaa nɩ a wʊ yuor lɛ be be?”

english_verse: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba bɛr wʊn a nɛɛ faa a anyãna wʊn 'yɛr a chãsara, ka nɛɛr 'maa ba nɩ a 'yɛr nyãna wʊn 'yɛr, a ba ɩ gbulo gbulo.

english_verse: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.

translation_verse: Sʊkpa nyã lɛ a Yesu lɔb kʊ ba, na wa 'yɛr, “Yɩ kaa a pɔlpɔl tɩɛ nɩ a tɩɩr a jaa.

translation_verse: Ka a poturbo 'yɛr, “Sɩ Soro nyɛ sɔbɛrɛ ayi.” A wʊ 'yɛr, “A sɛɛ naa.”

english_verse: Peter said, “Lord, I am ready to go with you to jail and even to die with you.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Simon 'yɛr, “N Soro, n chɔɔr naa ɩka maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na lɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, na sɩ 'mataa kpi.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Peter, I tell you that before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.” Moneybags, Traveling Bags, and Swords
translation_verse: A Yesu 'yɛr, “A fʊ̃ʊ Pita, sɛrɛ ka a nɔraa kõ a dɩna fʊn chɩɩr gbɛɛ ata ɩka faa bɔ̃ɔ ma ɛ.”

english_verse: Do this because the Scriptures say, ‘He was considered a criminal.’ This was written about me, and it will soon come true.”
translation_verse: A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ‘Ba sɔr wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a tʊ̃dɛbɛ dẽme pɔ.’ (Isaiya

english_verse: He said to them, “Why are you asleep? Wake up and pray that you won't be tested.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ gã guro? Yɩ ir sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn na yɩ taa lo a bɛɛr kaa pɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: “Father, if you will, please don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: “N Sãa, ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa, de a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyãna bar ma, a ɩ a fʊ bɔfʊ a ta ɩ a maa bɔfʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus went out to the Mount of Olives, as he often did, and his disciples went with him.
translation_verse: Yesu yin duoro a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju, nɩtãa lɛ wʊn ma ɩrɛ ka a wʊ poturbo turo wʊ.

english_verse: When they got there, he told them, “Pray that you won't be tested.”
translation_verse: Wʊn wa ta a jie wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn na yɩ taa lo a bɛɛr kaa pɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way before he knelt down and prayed,
translation_verse: Wʊ tɔɔn jãa ba bãlãa fɩka kʊsɩr fʊn na lɔb ka wʊ ta, na wʊ gbʊr dumo sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ,

english_verse: Jesus was in great pain and prayed so sincerely that his sweat fell to the ground like drops of blood.
translation_verse: A nɩbaal na kpɛ wʊ a lɛ, ka wʊ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa ka a nyĩe hãna wʊ loro tẽe fɩka jãɩ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd came up. It was led by Judas, one of the twelve apostles. He went over to Jesus and greeted him with a kiss.
translation_verse: Wʊn chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a lɛ ka a ɩ a nɩyɔɔ daan sãa, ka a 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Judas a Pie Nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ lɛ de ba a nie. Wʊ tɔɔn ta a Yesu puor wʊ.

english_verse: and a servant girl saw him. Then after she had looked at him carefully, she said, “This man was with Jesus!”
translation_verse: Ka a gbãgbapɔɔbile nyɛ wʊ a bũu na chaar na wʊ kaara wʊ jaa tãɩɩ tɩ wa 'yɛr, “A daba nyã mɩ ɩn a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ.”

english_verse: One of the disciples even struck at the high priest's servant with his sword and cut off the servant's right ear.
translation_verse: Ka a ba ãsʊɔ chɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ gbãgbaa nũsʊ̃ɔ toor ŋmaa.

english_verse: Jesus asked Judas, “Are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, “Judas, fʊ ben de puorfʊ garʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie?”

english_verse: When Jesus' disciples saw what was about to happen, they asked, “Lord, should we attack them with a sword?”
translation_verse: Yesu poturbo na nyɛ a lɛ na ɩrɛ ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, “Sɩ Soro, sɩ chɩɛrɛ nɩ a sɔbɛrɛ bɩɩ?”

english_verse: “Enough of that!” Jesus said. Then he touched the servant's ear and healed it.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Yɩ ta lɛ maal a lɛ ɛ.” Tɩ na wʊ sɩɩr a daba toor ka wʊ choo.

english_verse: Jesus spoke to the chief priests, the temple police, and the leaders who had come to arrest him. He said, “Why do you come out with swords and clubs and treat me like a criminal?
translation_verse: A Yesu pãa sʊʊr a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir gugurbo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe, “A mɔ̃ nɩbier lɛ n ɩ ka yɩ waan sɔbɛrɛ nɩ dakʊrsɩ a n nyɔɔfʊ jie bɩɩ?

english_verse: Peter said, “Woman, I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita chiir 'yɛr, “Pɔɔ, n ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: A little later someone else saw Peter and said, “You are one of them!” “No, I'm not!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: A ba kor ɛ ka a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ nyɛ wʊ na wʊ 'yɛr, “Fʊ mɩ ɩn a ba ãsʊɔ.” Ka a Pita 'yɛr, “Daba, n ba ɩ a ba ãsʊɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: They said, “Tell us! Are you the Messiah?” Jesus replied, “If I said so, you wouldn't believe me.
translation_verse: A ba sʊʊr wʊ, “Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ, 'yɛr kʊ sɩ.” Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Maa 'yɛr, yɩ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was arrested and led away to the house of the high priest, while Peter followed at a distance.
translation_verse: Ba nyɔɔ naa Yesu, na ba chiine a bɔɔrlo nãa yir, ka a Pita mɩ be a puor na wʊ turo.

english_verse: At daybreak the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses got together and brought Jesus before their council.
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar, a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe nɩ a ba bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe lɔ̃ɔn taa, ka ba tara a Yesu waan a be.

english_verse: Peter replied, “I don't know what you are talking about!” Right then, while Peter was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita 'yɛr, “Daba, n ba bɔ̃ɔ a ala fʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ.” Ka a nɔraa mɩ tɔ̃ɔ kõ a lɛ nɛ.

english_verse: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered that the Lord had said, “Before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Ka a Yesu lɩɛb na wʊ kaa a Pita, a wʊ tɔ̃ɔ tɩɛr tɔ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka, “Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a nɔraa kõ a dɩna fʊn chiir man gbɛɛ ata.”

english_verse: Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita ir yi a yõo na wʊ tɩ kõ nɩbaal kõn.

english_verse: The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him and beat him.
translation_verse: A dɔɔr bala na nyɔɔ a Yesu, piel na ba ɩrɛ wʊ laara tɩ ŋmiere wʊ.

english_verse: They kept on insulting Jesus in many other ways. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba lɛ den 'yɛrbidɛbɛ yɔɔ tʊ tʊn wʊ.

english_verse: And if I asked you a question, you wouldn't answer.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ n sʊʊr yɩn 'yɛr yɩ kʊ̃ haa ma nɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right side of God All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ an yi a dɩna chiine, a maa Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ naa Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ sɔɔ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.”

english_verse: Then they asked, “Are you the Son of God?” Jesus answered, “You say I am!”
translation_verse: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, “Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie ɔ?” Kaa a Yesu 'yɛr, “Yɩn lɛ 'yɛr ɩka lɛ n ɩ.”

english_verse: They replied, “Why do we need more witnesses? He said it himself!”
translation_verse: Ka ba pãa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, “Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ lɛ bʊɔrɔ dãasɩɛ go? Sɩ wõn yi a wʊ mãɛ nɛɛ pɔ.”

english_verse: Everyone in the council got up and led Jesus off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a nɩyɔɔ bala pãa tara a Yesu chiin a be a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate sɛ̃.

english_verse: They started accusing him and said, “We caught this man trying to get our people to riot and to stop paying taxes to the Emperor. He also claims that he is the Messiah, our king.”
translation_verse: Na ba tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛrsɩ anyãna dɔɔlɔ wʊ, “Sɩ nyɛn a daba nyãna ka wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɩbɛ 'yɛr, a ba ta yab a lõpo kʊ a Nãa Siisa ɛ, na wʊ lɛ 'yɛr ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa na ɩ a nãa.”

english_verse: Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
translation_verse: Hɛrɔd nɩ a wʊ sojasɩ maal a Yesu dɛɛr na ba ɩrɛ wʊ laara. Ba su wʊ naa a nãa fuu tɩ lɩɛb wʊ chiin a Pilate sɛ̃.

english_verse: He told them, “You brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done.
translation_verse: Na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn lɛ waan a daba nyãna a n sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr ɩka wʊ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa a nɩbɛ. N vɩɛr naa a wʊ 'yɛr a yɩ jaa niem ka a ala yɩn 'yɛr dɔɔl wʊ ba ɩ yelmãɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But they all kept on saying, “He has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.” Jesus Is Brought before Herod
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ tɔr tɩ chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, “Wiilfʊ nyãna lɛ wʊ tara dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a nɩbɛ be a Judiya. Galilee lɛ wʊ piel a yele nyãna be wa tãn a ka.”
