translation_verse: wa bin a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃, ka ba ma põ ir kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa lɛ an sɛɛn wʊ.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Wʊ ir naa libie amɩne bin ka a pɔɔ bɔ̃ɔn, tɩchɛ na wʊ chiin a ŋmaa tɩ bin a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Ka a Pita sʊʊr a Ananias, “Bo lɛ so ka a Satan sɛɛ fʊ a nyãa a fʊ ŋmaa jir kara yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, na fʊ ir a libie amɩne a lɛ fʊn da a puo bin?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: A ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ so na fʊ de da ɛ? Fʊn wa da baar a ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ so a libie ɛ? Bo lɛ ɩ a fʊ tɩɛr a fʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃? Nɩrɛ ba lɛ fʊ ŋmaa a jir nyã 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ.”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Ananias na wõ a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ lo kpi a jie. Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a bala jaa na wõ naa.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Ka a pɔlbil ir a ba pɔ 'mɔɔ a kũu lũ tuo yin tɩ ũu.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: An wa kor bãlãa ka a wʊ pɔɔ mɩ wa bʊ̃n dɔɔl ba bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ na ɩ ɛ,

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: ka a Pita sʊʊr a pɔɔ, “A libie a jaa lɛ a anyãna a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a Ananias na da a puo nyɛ ɔ? 'Yɛr kʊ ma.” A wʊ 'yɛr, “Ɔ̃ɔ, lɛ lɛ.”

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: A Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ɩ a yɩ guor taa ɩka yɩ bɛɛr a sɩ Soro Sɩɛ kaa? Nyɛ a nɩbɛ bala a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ bala lɛ tuo a fʊ sɩrɛ yin tɩ ũu, ba mɩn tuo fʊn yin.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Ajienaa ka a ɩ wʊ mɩ lon kpi be a Pita niem. A pɔlbil bala na ben wa kpɛ nyɛ ka a ɩ wʊ kpi naa, ka ba tuo wʊ yin tɩ ũu lan a sɩrɛ.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a pupuorbiir ba jaa nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na wõ naa.

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃ yɔɔ a nɩbɛ sɔ̃ɔ. Ka a bala na sɔɔ de, ba jaa tara na nɛbʊ̃'yen ma lɔ̃ɔ taa a be a Solomon Bɛchaar pɔ.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ jaa kpire ba naa, tɩ ãsʊɔ jaa maa 'la lɔ̃ɔn ba ɛ.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ nɩyɔɔ jaa chɛnɛ na waara, ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ na sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: A lɛ vɛ̃ naa ka a nɩbɛ yin a ba baalsɩ tɩ gaal ba a gador ju nɩ a sɔ̃n pɔ a sɔr kõkoor chɛlɛ nɩ a Pita, ɩka 'lɔ wa tala a wʊ gãagiel wʊ pɔɔ ba.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Nɩyɔɔ yin a tẽbil ala na jilʊ̃ a Jerusalɛm waan a ba baalsɩ wa lɔ̃ɔn 'matãan a bala a sɩdɛbɛ na tara, ka ba jaa sa.

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Ka a nyuur kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a wʊ taaba bala na pʊɔ a Saduseemɩne gbul pɔ.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: A ba nyɔɔr a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ pɔɔfʊ die pɔ bar.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a tãsɔɔ a sɩ Soro tʊ̃ naa a wʊ malaka ka wʊ wa yuo a pɔɔfʊ die pãn na wʊ tɔɔ ba yin

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: tɩ 'yɛr, “Yɩ chen a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr a nyɛvʊr paalaa nyã 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar ba kpɛ̃n be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, nɩtãa lɛ a malaka na 'yɛr ba tɩ piel wile a nɩbɛ. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a wʊ taaba mɩ wa lɔ̃ɔn taa na ba bʊɔl a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa na ba na guor a 'yɛr, na ba pãa tʊ̃ ɩka ba chen a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ tɩ yuo a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ waan.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ban wa tɩ ta a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ baa nyɛ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ. A ba lɩɛb chen tɩ mana kʊrɔ ba,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Sɩ chen tɩ nyɛ a pɔɔfʊ die dɔ̃dɔr na chɛnɛ kpal, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ara gu chɛnɛ ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, tɩchɛ sɩn wa yuo a pãn nɩrɛ jaa ba be be a die pɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Ban wa wõ a anyãna ban man ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir guguro nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãmɩne ka a ɩ ba chĩcham, a ba baala baa bɔ̃ɔ lɛn na ɩ ɛ.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Ka a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ wa 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ bala yɩn nyɔɔr pɔɔ, ba ben be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ wile a nɩbɛ.”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir guguro nɩkpɛ̃ɛ tara a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ chiin tɩ tara a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ jaa jom waan. Baa tɩ fɩr ba ɛ, bojũu ba joro na dãbãɛ ɩka a nɩbɛ na lɔb ban nɩ kʊsɩbɛ.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Ban wa waan ba, ba vɛ̃n ka ba ara a nɩbɛrɛ niem, ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na sʊʊr ba 'yɛr,

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Saa kpãkpãan yɩ jaa 'yɛr ɩka, yɩ taa lɛ ɩrɛ wa pʊrɔ a yuor nyãna wile nɩ nɩrɛ jaa go ɛ? Tɩchɛ a yɩ chɛnɛ tɔr tɩ sɛɛ a Jerusalɛm nɩ a yɩ wiilfʊ nyãna, na yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ de a daba nyãna jãɩ yele dɔɔl sɩ.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ bala na chɛ 'yɛr, “Sɩn tun Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ gar nɩsaal.

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: A sɩ sãakpãmɩne Nãaŋmɩn lɛ sãɩ a Yesu a kũu pɔ, a 'lɔ yɩn yɔɔl a daa ju ka wʊ kpi.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn 'mɔɔ wʊn don bin be a wʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ka wʊ ɩ Nãa nɩ Fafaara ɩka wʊ kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ sɔr ka ba lɩɛb ba tɩɛr, na ba nyɛ vɛ̃ kʊfʊ.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Sɩn lɛ ɩ a anyãna dãasɩɛ dẽme nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'lɔ wʊn de kʊ a bala na tu a wʊ nɛɛ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Ban wa wõ a anyãna, ba suur ir naa ka ba bʊɔrɔ a ba kʊfʊ.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Farasee ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Gamaliel na ɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwile, nɩrɛ jaa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ naa, ka wʊ ir ara a nɩdierbe pɔ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, ba vɛ̃ ka a dɔɔr bala yi yõo bãlãa.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Tɩ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Isɩral dɔɔr, yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃ a lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ maal a nɩbɛ banyãna.

english_verse: Not long ago Theudas claimed to be someone important, and about
translation_verse: Sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Tiudasɩ dɔ̃ɔ ir 'yɛr gãdaa 'yɛr ɩka nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ ka a dɔɔr kɔɔr anaar (

english_verse: men joined him. But he was killed, and all his followers were scattered. That was the end of that.
translation_verse: ) ir turo wʊ. Ba kʊ wʊ naa ka a nɩbɛ bala yɛ̃yɛl ka a jaa ɩ pɔrɔ.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Ala puor lɛ ka a Judas na yi a Galilee a nɩbɛ sɔrfʊ yuon ir nɩyɔɔ ka ba turo wʊ. Ba mɩ kʊ wʊ naa ka a bala jaa na turo wʊ mɩ bũbur.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, maa na 'yɛr yɩ naa ɩka yɩ ir nũu, tɩchɛ a nɩbɛ ba nyã bar. Yɩ bar ba a ba chiine. Ala ɩ a lɛ ban maala ɩn nɩrɛ lɛ bin an ɩn pɔrɔ.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ lɛ a yi, yaa be jaa na wa tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaa a nɩbɛ banyãna sɔr ɛ, yɩn wa bɔ̃ɔn naa ka a ɩ Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ yɩ jɛɛrɛ nɩ.”

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛrɛ tu naa a lɛ a Gamaliel na 'yɛr, tɩ na ba bʊɔl a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ fɔb ba tɩ kpãkpãan ba ɩka ba taa lɛ ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr pʊrɔ nɩ a Yesu yuor go ɛ, tɩ bar ba a ba chen.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na wa yi a nɩbɛrɛ banyãna niem, ba chɩlɛ naa, bojũu ba sɛɛn naa a dɔɔyɛ nɩ a vã difʊ a Yesu yuor jũu.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Bɩbir jaa lɛ ba ma be a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ wile a nɩbɛ na ba mɩ yʊɔrɔ a yie ba gala ɛ, na ba wile a nɩbɛ tɩ 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ ɩka a Yesu lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: A ber ala pɔ, a poturbo na waara pʊɔrɔ a bala ban dɔɔ a paal yuo pɔ, ka baa tʊɔrɔ 'yɛrɛ a Juu kɔkɔr ɛ, hũhũnõ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bala na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, bojũu a ba pɔɔkɔɛ maa nyɛ a bɩbir jaa bʊ̃diir ban ma põ ɛ.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi bʊɔl a poturbo ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa wa 'yɛr, “A ba tara sɔr ka sɩn bar a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ tɩ põnõ bʊ̃diir ɛ.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu yɛɛr, yɩ kaa a yɩ pɔ na yɩ ir dɔɔr ayopõi, bala yɩn bɔ̃ɔ ka ba sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ tɩ mɩ tara yɛ̃, ka sɩn de a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã kʊ ba.

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a sɩn 'lɔ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ nɩ a wʊ 'yɛr.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: A 'yɛr nyã pɛl ba jaa na duo a be pʊɔ, ka ba ir a nɩbɛ banyãna. Sɩteven a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na sɛɛ ka wʊ mɩ tara sɔɔfʊ, nɩ Filip, nɩ Porokorusɩ, nɩ Nɩkanor, nɩ Tɩmon, nɩ Pamenasɩ, nɩ Nikolasɩ na yi Antiok na wʊ lɩɛb kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ pɔ.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ ba ir waan a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃, ka ba dɔɔl ba nuur sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr yaar, ka a poturbo mɩ na be a Jerusalɛm pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo yɔɔ jie mɩ sɔɔn de a 'yɛr.

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ ben a Sɩteven sɛ̃ ka wʊ tara kpɛ̃ɔ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ a nɩbɛ sɛ̃.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie a ba bʊɔl ba, Mãɛ Sorbo, nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Sirenɩ nɩ a Aleksandiriya, ka bamɩne mɩ yi a Sɩlɩsɩya nɩ Asɩya. A nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ piel a nɛchɩrɛ na ba chɩrɛ nɩ a Sɩteven.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ baa tʊɔrɔ wʊ ɛ, a yɛ̃ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na kʊ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: A ba pãa sɔɔl tʊ̃ nɩbɛ bamɩne ka ba ŋmaa jir 'yɛr ɩka, “Sɩn lɛ wõ a Sɩteven na fere 'yɛrɛ 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn.”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Tɩ tɔ a nɩbɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba nyɔɔ a Sɩteven chiin a ba 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ sɛ̃.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba waan jir ŋmarba ka ba wa 'yɛr, “A daba nyãna ba gala 'yɛr dɛbɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir chɛchɛ nyãna ɛ, nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wʊn bin ɛ.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Sɩn mãɛ lɛ wõ wʊn 'yɛr ɩka a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ na ŋmɛr a jie nyãna, tɩ na wʊ lɩɛb a binfʊ ala a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ bin kʊ sɩ.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Ka a bala jaa na jãnɛ a be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie, kãan a Sɩteven nie ka wʊ kaara fɩka a malaka nie.

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: “A wʊ yi a Chaldea wɛr pɔ na wʊ chen tɩ jãnɛ a Haran wɛr pɔ. A wʊ sãa na wa kpi baar lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn waan wʊ a kana yɩn ben kpɩɛrɛ.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, ɩka wʊn yaa naa a sãn a paal 'lɔ na maal ba a gbãgbaar. A anyã puor lɛ ban wa puor ma a jie nyãna.’ (A Pielfʊ Gãn

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn tɩr nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham a yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ yele. Abraham na wa dɔɔ a Isaakɩ, wʊ ŋmaa wʊn a yʊɔr a ber aniin daar. Ka a Isaakɩ mɩ dɔɔ a Jekɔb, ka a Jekɔb mɩ dɔɔ a sɩ sãamɩne pie nɩ ayi.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: “A sɩ sãamɩne banyãna na tara nɩ a ba yɛbile Josefʊ nɩ nyuur jũu, na ba nyɔɔ wʊ da kʊ a Ijipitɩ dẽme. Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn be a wʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: “Ka a kɔ̃ lo Ijipitɩ nɩ a Kanaan wɛr wʊ jaa waan dɔɔyɛ, ka a sɩ sãamɩne ba nyɛrɛ bʊ̃diir ɛ.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Jekɔb na wa wõ ɩka bʊ̃diir ben a be Ijipitɩ, wʊ tʊ̃n a sɩ sãamɩne ka ba chen.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: A gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ ban chen lɛ a Josefʊ wiil a wʊ mãɛ kʊ ba, ka a Faaro mɩ pãa bɔ̃ɔ a Josefʊ yir nɩbɛ.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Jekɔb chen a be a Ijipitɩ nɩ a sɩ sãamɩne be lɛ ba jaa tɩ kpi.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Ba tuo naa a ba ãgãma waan a Sekem wa ũu ba a yaa 'lɔ pɔ a Abraham na da a Hamor bibiir sɛ̃ a be a Sekem.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “A sɔ̃ɔ na wa gbʊr ka a Nãaŋmɩn na maal a lɛ wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham, a sɩ nɩbɛ na be a Ijipitɩ dɔɔn paal.

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ ka a nãyuo wa di na wʊ kaara a Ijipitɩ na waa bɔ̃ɔ a Josefʊ yele ɛ.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Wʊ maal naa a sɩ nɩbɛ putuyele na wʊ ge ba, na wʊ ma fɩr a pɔɔ pʊʊr ka bala wa dɔɔ bipɩlɛ ba lɔb ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ a ba kpi. (A Yifʊ Gãn

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: “A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ ba dɔɔ a Mosesɩ, ka wʊ ɩ bivɩla a Nãaŋmɩn niem. A ba guol wʊ ŋmɛrsɩ ata be a wʊ sãa yir.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Ban wa yin wʊ tɩ bar a yõo, Faaro bipɔɔ de wʊn guol ka wʊ ɩ a wʊ mãɛ bidaba.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Ba jãn naa Mosesɩ a Ijipitɩ tẽe yɛ̃ a jaa, ka wʊ kpɛ̃mɛ a wʊ 'yɛr nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ pɔ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Wʊ tɩ nyɛn a ba ãsʊɔ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na gere ka wʊ ŋmaa de na wʊ kʊ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar, wʊ tɩ nyɛn a Isɩral nɩbɛ ayi mɩ na jɛɛrɛ taa. Ka wʊ 'maal ba tɩ sʊrɔ ba, ‘Yɩn yɛɛr nara, bo lɛ so a yɩ jɛɛrɛ taa?’

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ mɩ kʊ ma nɩtãa lɛ fʊn kʊ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ a jãa wɛ?’ (A Yifʊ Gãn

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Mosesɩ na wõ a 'yɛr nyãna ka wʊ jo chen tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a Midiyan, be lɛ wʊ be na wʊ wa dɔɔ dabil ayi.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Wʊn wa nyɛ a lɛ ka a nɛ'maa wʊ, a wʊ tɔɔ gbʊr ɩka wʊ kaa. Be lɛ wʊ wõ a Soro kɔkɔr na 'yɛr,

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: “Ka a sɩ Soro pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Yaa a fʊ nafɔɔr bar, a jie fʊn ara ɩ naa jichɛchɛ.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: N nyɛ naa a n nɩbɛ be a Ijipitɩ pɔ ban gere, n wõ naa ba hʊ̃ʊnfʊ naa suu ɩka n wa faa ba. Wa, ka n tʊ̃ fʊ a be a Ijipitɩ.’ (A Yifʊ Gãn

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “A Mosesɩ nyãna nɩ wʊ ju ban tɔr bar tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, ‘An lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɩka fʊ kaara sɩ na fʊ tɔɔrɔ a sɩ 'yɛr?’ 'Lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ tʊ̃ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a malaka na wa dũu wʊ 'lɩrɛ a bũu a tɩbile pɔ, ɩka wʊ tɩ ɩ a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a ba fafaara.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: 'Lɔ lɛ tɩ de ba nie na wʊ tʊ̃ tʊ̃ yɔɔ be a Ijipitɩ tẽe pɔ, nɩ a Man Jɩɛ par nɩ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ban be a yuom lɩjaayi (

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: A Mosesɩ nyãna nɩ wʊ ju lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ɩka, ‘Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ naa a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ a maa kaar ka wʊ yi a yɩ mãɛ nɩbɛ pɔ.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, 'matãan a sɩ sãakpãmɩne ka a malaka 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ wʊ be a Sinai Tɔ̃ɔ par na ɩ nyɛvʊr 'yɛr ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ sɩ.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: “Tɩchɛ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne tɔr wʊ naa, na baa tu a wʊ nɛɛ ɛ. Ba tɔr wʊn bar ka a ba tɩɛr lɩɛb chen be a Ijipitɩ.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: A ba mɛ bʊtɩɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ nyɛtãan nele, na ba waan bomo wa lon bɔɔr a jie na ba dãɛn 'yɔ̃ɔn a ba nũu bʊ̃maalaa nyãna.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ ba, na wʊ bar ba ka ba puoro a saaju bomo. Ka anyãna tɩ tuor a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb, wʊ sʊʊr naa, ‘A yɩn Isɩral nɩbɛ, yɩn be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ a yuom lɩjaayi (

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Kai, tɩ a bʊtɩbɛ ala yɩn kaara lɛ a Molokɩ nɩ ŋmɛrbir ŋmɩn Arefan, a tɩbɛ anyãna lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ maal puoro. A lɛ jũu, ɩn vɛ̃ naa ka ba nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a Babilon puor ka yɩ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be.’ (Amosɩ

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “A sɩ sãakpãmɩne dɔ̃ɔ maal gãma yir be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ka a ɩ dãasɩɛ, ba maal wʊ naa nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na man wiil a Mosesɩ ka wʊ nyɛ.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: A gãma yir nyã lɛ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne tara ka a Josua de ba a nie ka ba waan, a ba wa faa a wɛr nyã pɔ a nɩyor na dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn dii ba bar. A gãn yir nyã ben be lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ tãn a Nãa David sɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn maal vɩla kʊ wʊ ka wʊ ɩka wʊn mɛn yir kʊ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ Solomon lɛ wa mɛ a yir kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: “Fʊ̃ʊ kaa, a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ɩ a saaju sʊɔ, ba kpɩɛrɛ a yie anyã nɩbɛ na mɛ ɛ. Nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: ‘Saaju lɛ ɩ a maa nakɔɔ, ka a tẽe ɩ a n gbɛɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie. Ka a sɩ Soro sʊʊr, yir bo tɔ lɛ yɩn mɛ kʊ ma? Bɩɩ nyɩnɛ lɛ na ɩ a n pɛ̃nfʊ jie?

english_verse: You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did.
translation_verse: “Yɩn nyãkpãɛn dẽme na ɩ nɩbɛ na ba wõnõ 'yɛr ɛ, yɩ kaara a yɩ sãamɩne kaar. Yɩ maa sɔɔ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɔr ɛ.

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ jaa ba be be ka a yɩ sãamɩne ba ge wʊ ɛ. Ba gba kʊ naa bala na de nie mʊɔl 'yɛr a Torfʊ Sʊɔ 'lɔ waa 'yɛr. A pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn gba lɛ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ na yɩ kʊ wʊ.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Yɩn lɛ de a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ wʊn kʊ a malaka a wʊ wa kʊ yɩ ka yɩ de tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ, tɩchɛ a yɩ tɔr bar.”

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: A 'yɛr nyãna ban wa wõ, ka ba suur ir a ba kpar kparaa jur 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a Sɩteven a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na sɛɛ a wʊ dʊl kaa saaju na wʊ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr, ka a Yesu ara a Nãaŋmɩn nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Yɩ nyɛ ka, n nyɛ naa saaju na yuo ka a Nɩsaal Bie ara a Nãaŋmɩn nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: A anyãna ban wõ ba pɔɔn a tobo tɩ ŋme chɛlma na ba tɩ mɩl wʊ,

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: na ba tɔɔ wʊ yin a tẽe chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ tɩ piel lɔɔrɔ wʊ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ. Ka a bala na di dãasɩɛ yaar a ba fuusɩ bin pɔlbile kɔ̃ɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Sɔɔl sɛ̃.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Ban lɔɔrɔ wʊ a lɛ nɛ na kʊ ka wʊ sʊɔr Yesu, “N Soro Yesu de a n sãɛ.”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Wʊ lon gbur dumo tɩ pãa ŋme chɛlma 'yɛr, “N Soro taa ɩrɛ wa yaara ba sãn ɛ.” A anyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr baar tɩ ŋmaa vʊʊr.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Sɔɔl mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir bur buro a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ bara, na wʊ yʊɔrɔ kpɩɛrɛ a yie nyɔɔrɔ dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: A bala na jo ŋma a dẽdẽme chɛnɛ na mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr jie jaa ban chen.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Filip chen Samariya tẽe pɔ, na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka Yesu lɛ a Nɩ-iraa a be.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: A nɩyɔɔ na wa wõ a lɛ a Filip na 'yɛr tɩ mɩ nyɛ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ wʊn tʊ̃, a ba yuo toor chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ.

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Daba kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be ban bʊɔlɔ Simon na wʊ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃ tãsɔɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a 'maa a Samariya nɩbɛ nɛɛ, na wʊ ma wile a wʊ mãɛ ɩka nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ ba jaa, a bɛrɛ nɩ a bil ba jaa ma yuon toor chɛlɛ wʊ tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “A daba nyã 'lɔ kpɛ̃ɔ ɩ naa a gãdaa sʊɔ kpɛ̃ɔ.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Ba turo wʊ naa bojũu a kor naa wʊn tʊ̃nɔ̃ a tãsɔɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a 'maa ba nɛɛ.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a lɛ ban wa sɔɔ de a lɛ a Filip na 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr, nɩ a Yesu na ɩ a Nɩ-iraa yuor, dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ ba jaa son a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: A Simon mɩ sɔɔ naa ka ba so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ a wʊ turo a Filip yʊɔrɔ, a nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ wʊn nyɛ wʊn tʊ̃.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na be a Jerusalɛm na wa wõ ɩka a Samariya nɩbɛ mɩ sɔɔn de a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, ba tʊ̃n Pita nɩ Jɔɔn a ba sɛ̃.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ban tɩ ta, ba sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ a nɩbɛ ka ban nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ba kpɛ ãsʊɔ jaa sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ lɛ ba so ba, nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn pãa dɔɔl ba a nuur a ba nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Simon na nyɛ a lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na dɔɔl ba a nũu ka ba nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, a wʊ de libie kʊ ba,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: tɩ 'yɛr, “Yɩ mɩ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ nyãna ka maa wa dɔɔl nɩrɛ jaa a n nũu, a wʊ mɩ ma nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ libie jaa yɩ wɛ, bojũu fʊ tɩɛr ɩka fʊn tʊ̃ɔ da a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ nɩ libie.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Faa tara sɔr na lɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃n sɩ ɛ, bojũu a fʊ nyãa ba tor a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

english_verse: Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you.
translation_verse: Lɩɛb a fʊ pututɩɛr nyãna na fʊ sʊɔr a sɩ Soro amɩne sɛ̃ wʊn vɛ̃n kʊ fʊ a fʊ tɩɛr dɛɛr nyãna fʊn tara.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: N nyɛn bɔ̃ɔn fʊn ɩ pubier sʊɔ ka a yelbier nyɔɔ fʊ pɔɔ.”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: A Simon pãa 'yɛr, “Yɩ sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ma ka ala yɩn 'yɛr taa waara a n sɛ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: A lɛ Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn na wa man 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr baar, ba lɩɛb kulo a Jerusalɛm ba 'yɛrɛ naa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna a tẽbil ala na chɛɛl a Samariya.

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn malaka 'yɛr kʊ a Filip, “Ir suu de a sɔr 'lɔ na yi a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ tu a Jerusalɛm chen a Gaja.”

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ wʊ ir chiine, na wʊ tɩ tuor a Etiyopiya daba ãsʊɔ ban maal ka wʊ ab na wʊ ɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kaara a Etiyopiya nãpɔɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Kandasɩ libie. Jerusalɛm lɛ wʊ chen tɩ puor,

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: na wʊ lɩɛb kulo jãnɛ a wʊ wii torko pɔ na wʊ kara a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya gãn.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr a Filip, “Chen tɩ gbʊr a torko par.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: A Filip jo chen a be a torko par na wʊ wõ a daba na kara a Isaiya gãn a wʊ sʊʊr wʊ, “Fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa lɛ par fʊn kara ɔ?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Ka a daba 'yɛr, “Ŋmɩnɛ ɩn ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ a par, ka nɩrɛ baa wiil ma ɛ?” Tɩ na wʊ bʊɔl a Filip ka wʊ do jãnɛ a torko pɔ.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: A anyãna lɛ a da-abbaa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kara a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, “Ba nyɔɔ wʊn chiine fɩka pɛr na tɩ kʊ, Wʊ ɩn jaa gbul fɩka pele ban põnõ a kɔɔl na waa haa nɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Wʊ siir a wʊ mãɛ a lɛ ban ba tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr kaa tor ɛ. An lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a wʊ bal nɩbɛ 'yɛr? A wʊ nyɛvʊr ba tal tɛɛr a ka a wɛr pɔ ɛ.” (Isaiya

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Ka a da-abbaa nyã sʊɔr a Filip, “Yãayãabɩlɛ, 'yɛr kʊ ma, an 'yɛr lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ nyã 'yɛrɛ? Wʊ mãɛ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: A Filip pãa piel na wʊ de a Nãaŋmɩn gãn 'yɛr nyãna man a Yesu 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ wʊ.

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Tɩ na wʊ tɔɔ a torko a wʊ ara, ka a 'lɔ nɩ a Filip 'mataa kpɛ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, ka a Filip so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Ban wa yi a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa jaa naa a Filip chiin ka a da-abbaa 'lɔ ba lɛ nyɛ wʊ go ɛ. Tɩ de wʊ sɔr chɩlɛ kulo.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Filip tɩ yi naa Ajotusɩ na wʊ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a tẽn ala a jaa tɩ tãn Sisariya.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Sɔɔl kpɛ̃ɔ chɛnɛ pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr tʊ̃ɔ a Juu nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ a Damaskus na wʊ wiil ba ɩka a Yesu lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã, ir na fʊ kpɛ be a tẽe pɔ, ban wiil fʊ naa a lɛ fʊn na ɩ.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Ɩ̃n wiil wʊ naa a lɛ wʊn na di dɔɔyɛ a n yuor jũu.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Wʊn tɩ ta Jerusalɛm, wʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a poturbo tɩchɛ a ba joro wʊ dãbãɛ, baa sɔɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ naa a poturo ãsʊɔ ɛ.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: A wʊ jãna pɔ, wʊ nyɛn daba ban bʊɔlɔ Ananias na dɔɔl wʊ nũu ka wʊ lɛ nyɛrɛ.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Ajienaa ka bʊ̃nyɩnsɩ 'lɔɔr a Sɔɔl nɩbie pɔ lo a wʊ lɛ nyɛrɛ go. Wʊ ir naa a ba tɔ̃ɔ so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Sɔɔl chɛnɛ pʊrɔ a sɩ Soro poturbo ju, na wʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ ba. Wʊ chen naa a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃,

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: na wʊ tɩ sʊɔr sɔr de gãn na chiin a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir ala na be a Damaskus ɩka 'lɔ wa tɩ nyɛ nɩrɛ na turo a Sɔr nyã, ka pɔɔ lɛ bɩɩ daba lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ɔ nyɔɔr ba waan a Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ wʊ de a sɔr chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a Damaskus ka chãa yi be a saaju ajienaa wa nyɩɩr jil wʊ.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Ka wʊ lo tẽe tɩ wõ kɔkɔr na bʊɔlɔ wʊ sʊrɔ, “Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bo lɛ so a fʊ gere ma a lɛ?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Ka a Sɔɔl sʊʊr, “An lɛ fʊ ɩ Soro?” Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Maa lɛ ɩ a Yesu fʊn gere.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Ka a dɔɔr bala nɩ a Sɔɔl na 'mataa chiine gĩe ara, na ba wõ a dɔɔn, tɩchɛ baa nyɛ nɩrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Ka a Sɔɔl ir a tẽe ara na wʊ yuo a nɩbie tɩ waa lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ. A ba nyɔɔ wʊ a nũu tɔɔ kpɛ̃n be a Damaskus.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Wʊ maal ber ata waa lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ, waa di bʊ̃diir bɩɩ nyũ kʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a poturo ãsʊɔ ben a be Damaskus a ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Ananias ka a sɩ Soro wa bʊɔl wʊ a jãna pɔ, “Ananias.” A wʊ sɔɔ, “Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro.”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ir de a Sɔr Dẽdẽe chiine na fʊ tɩ ta a Judas yir sʊʊr daba na yi Tasusɩ a ba bʊɔlɔ Sɔɔl, be lɛ wʊ be sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: A Ananias 'yɛr, “N Soro, n wõn a daba nyã yelyɔɔ a lɛ wʊn ge a fʊ nɩsʊ̃n a be a Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: A ka mɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ mɩ kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ wa nyɔɔr a bala jaa na bʊɔlɔ a fʊ yuor.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ a Ananias, “Chen, a daba nyãna lɛ ɩ a n bʊ̃-iraa ka wʊn tuo a maa yuor chiin a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ, nɩ a ba nãmɩne sɛ̃, lɔ̃ɔn a Isɩral nɩbɛ.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a Ananias ir chen tɩ kpɛ be a yir, na wʊ tɩ dɔɔl wʊ a nũu tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “N yɛbɛ Sɔɔl, a sɩ Soro Yesu na yi fʊ be a sɔr pɔ fʊn waara lɛ tʊ̃ ma a fʊ sɛ̃ ka fʊn lɛ nyɛrɛ go, na fʊ sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Wʊn wa di a bʊ̃diir baar wʊ lɛ nyɛn kpɛ̃ɔ, na wʊ jãnɛ a poturbo bala sɛ̃ a be a Damaskus bãlãa.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Ajienaa, wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir, na wʊ 'yɛrɛ ɩka a Yesu ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Bala jaa na wõ a wʊ 'yɛr ka a 'maa ba nɛɛ a ba sʊrɔ, “A ba ɩ a daba nyãna lɛ ir gere a bala a be a Jerusalɛm na bʊɔlɔ a yuor nyãna ɛ? Na wʊ wa a ka mɩ ɩka wʊ wa nyɔɔr a bala mɩ chiin tɩ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Ber yɔɔ na wa po, a Juu nɩbɛ guor taa ɩka ba kʊ a Sɔɔl,

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: tɩchɛ kaa a Sɔɔl bɔ̃ɔn a ba guorfʊ. Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ jaa lɛ ba mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir guro wʊ a tẽe dɔ̃dɔɩ nɛɛr, bʊɔrɔ a wʊ kʊ̃ fʊ.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a bala na turo wʊ 'mɔɔ wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ pɩɛ pɔ tãsɔɔ na ba tur wʊ bar ka wʊ tu a dãpuor dachin pɔ suu.

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Banabasɩ tara wʊ chiin be a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃. 'Lɔ lɛ pãa man kʊ ba a lɛ a Sɔɔl na nyɛ a sɩ Soro a sɔr pɔ, ka a Soro 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ wʊ. Wʊ 'yɛr ɩka a be a Damaskus wʊ mʊɔl 'yɛr a Yesu yuor 'yɛr, na wʊ ta jo dãbãɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: A Sɔɔl jãnɛ a be a ba sɛ̃ na wʊ so wʊ mãɛ yʊɔrɔ be a Jerusalɛm na wʊ mʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor 'yɛrɛ ba joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: A poturbo na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ba tara wʊn chiin a Sisariya tɩ bar ka wʊ chen Tasusɩ.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Damfʊ ba lɛ be a bala na puoro be a Judiya, Galilee nɩ a Samariya, ka a nyã'maar. Ba kpɛ̃mɛ naa ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpɛ̃ɔ ka ba nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ na ba joro a sɩ Soro dãbãɛ.

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita yʊɔrɔ a tẽn na wʊ wa chen Lida ɩka wʊ tɩ piir a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo kaa.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ tɩ nyɛ daba ban bʊɔlɔ Ainɩyas na gbɛr gã yuomo aniin.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita bʊɔl wʊ, “Ainɩyas, Yesu yuor pɔ, sa. Ir saaju na fʊ pilʊ̃ a fʊ bʊ̃gãnaa.” Ajienaa lɛ a Ainɩyas ir saaju.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Ka a Lida nɩbɛ ba jaa nɩ a Saron nɩbɛ na nyɛ a lɛ, a ba lɩɛb puoro a sɩ Soro.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ na dɔ̃ɔ puoro ben a be Jopa, ka ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Tabita, a yo bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ba lɩɛb bʊɔlɔ Dɔɔkas. Wʊ ma maala na vɩla na wʊ sʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɔ̃n dẽme.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊ ɩ baal na wʊ kpi, a ba so wʊ a kʊ̃ɔ don gaal be a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Lida nɩ a Jopa mɩ ba jãa ɛ, kaa a pupuorbo wõ ɩka a Pita ben a Lida, a ba tʊ̃ nɩbɛ ayi ka ba tɩ sʊɔr wʊ, “Yãayãabɩlɛ a wʊ wa fɔɔ.”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Ka bala naa Pita jaa 'mataa chen, wʊn wa ta ba de wʊn don be a bɔɔpɩɛ die pɔ. A pɔɔkɔɛ ba jaa ara na jilʊ̃ wʊ kõnõ na ba wile a Pita a fuusɩ nɩ a fɔɔr a Dɔɔkas na maal wʊn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ be a ba sɛ̃.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Pita diin ba jaa a ba yi a be a die pɔ, tɩ na wʊ gbur dumo sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn, na wʊ lɩɛb tor a kũu tɩ 'yɛr, “Tabita ir.” A wʊ yuo a nɩbie nyɛ a Pita na wʊ ir jãnɛ.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita nyɔɔ wʊ a nũu sʊ̃ʊ wʊ a wʊ ir ara, tɩ pãa bʊɔl a bala na sɔɔ nɩ a pɔɔkɔɛ ka ba kpɛ wa nyɛ a wʊ ɩ nɩ nɩbir.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka a yele nyãna yaar a Jopa wʊ jaa ka a nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie wa sɔɔ a sɩ Soro.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: A Pita jɛ̃n be a Jopa bãlãa a daba kɔ̃ɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Simon na ma nɔ̃nɔ̃ gãma yir.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Be a Sisariya, daba kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be a ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Konɩlɩyɔs, wʊ be naa a sojasɩ gbul 'lɔ pɔ a Itali nɩbɛ na bʊɔlɔ a sojasɩ kɔbaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: 'Lɔ naa wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa puoro naa na ba joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ, na wʊ ma kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ bala an wõnõ pɔrɔ, na wʊ mɩ ma sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn ba gala ɛ.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ lɛ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa bal, a wʊ gã jãn jaa chɛchɛ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn malaka a wʊ wa ara a wʊ sɛ̃ tɩ bʊɔl wʊ, “Konɩlɩyɔs.”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Ka wʊ chaal a lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ tɩ sʊʊr, “Bo lɛ N Soro?” Ka a malaka 'yɛr, “A fʊ puorfʊ nɩ a kʊfʊ fʊn kʊrɔ a nɔ̃n dẽme don tɩ pɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn ka wʊ tara a fʊ tɩɛr.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã, tʊ̃ nɩbɛ ka ba chen a Jopa tɩ tara daba kɔ̃ɔ na di Simon tɩ ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Pita a wʊ wa.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Wʊ kpɩɛrɛ naa a daba na nɔ̃nɔ̃ gãma mɩ na di a Simon yir, ka a wʊ yir gbʊr a man kpɛ̃ɛ gogoro.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: A malaka na wa chen baar, Konɩlɩyɔs bʊɔl naa a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ayi nɩ a soja ãsʊɔ mɩ na puoro na ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Wʊ man a lɛ jaa na ɩ tɩ tʊ̃ ba a Jopa.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sʊ̃ɔ, ka ba be a sɔr pɔ chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a tẽe, a Pita mɩ don a gar ju Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ wʊ a wʊ bʊɔrɔ bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ na di, ban chɛnɛ maala a bʊ̃diir ka a dedee kpɛ wʊ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: A wʊ nyɛ saaju na yuo a wʊ nyɛ bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ fɩka fɔkpɛ̃ɛ na gbũ jiir anaar ka wʊ suro wa gbʊrɔ a tẽe.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Ka a dʊ̃gbɛɛ anaar a jaa bʊrɔ be be nɩ a tẽe bʊ̃vʊʊrsɩ nɩ a lubilɔɔrsɩ a jaa.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a kɔkɔr pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Pita, ir kʊ 'wɔb.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'yɛr, “Sɩrɛ jaa, kai. N Soro, n ba 'wɔb a dʊ̃dɛbɛ anyãna nɩ a dʊ̃chiirsɩ anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka a kɔkɔr lɛ 'yɛr go, “Ta bʊɔl bon jaa bʊ̃dɛɛr 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na maal a wʊ ɩ bʊ̃sʊ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Gbɛɛ ata lɛ a ɩ, tɩchɛ ka a fɔbaa lɛ jaa a lɛ nɛ don a saaju ajienaa.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pita na chɛnɛ pɛrɛ a nyɛfʊ nyã par, ka a dɔɔr bala a Konɩlɩyɔs na tʊ̃ mɩ nyɛn a Simon yir na ba ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: Ba bʊɔl sʊʊr, “Daba na di Simon tɩ ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Pita ka lɛ wʊ kpɩɛrɛ ɔ?”

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pita na chɛnɛ tɩɛrɛ a nyɛfʊ nyãna ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Simon, dɔɔr ata lɛ bʊɔrɔ fʊ sʊrɔ.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Ir suu a ba sɛ̃, taa ɩrɛ wa baala fʊn na lɔ̃ɔ chiin ba ɛ, maa lɛ tʊ̃ ba.”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Ka a Pita ir suu tɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ na wʊ 'yɛr, “Maa lɛ ɩ a nɩrɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ wa?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: A ba 'yɛr, “Konɩlɩyɔs na ɩ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ lɛ sɩ yi. A wʊ yele tor naa ka wʊ ɩ nɩrɛ na joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ. A Nãaŋmɩn malaka wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka wʊ bʊɔl fʊ a fʊ wa be a wʊ yir a wʊ wõ lɛ jaa fʊn na man.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: A Pita pãa tara ba kpɛ̃n tɩ gaal. Ka a wɛr chaar, a bala nɩ a Pita nɩ a pupuorbiir bamɩne na yi a Jopa pʊɔ ba a ba 'mataa chen.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Ba chen ka a wɛr tɩ chaar a ba ta a Sisariya, ka a ɩ a Konɩlɩyɔs mɩ chɛlɛ ba naa na wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ yir dẽme nɩ a wʊ barmɩne ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa a ba jɛ̃ gu.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pita na ta kpɩɛrɛ a Konɩlɩyɔs yir wʊ ir tuor a Pita gbur dumo a wʊ niem na wʊ puor wʊ nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita nyɔɔ wʊ a wʊ ir, ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Ara, a maa mɩ nɩsaal lɛ n mɩ ɩ.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Wʊ 'yɛr wʊ naa lɛ tɩ kpɛ a die pɔ tɩ nyɛ nɩyɔɔ na duo.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa an ba tara sɔr ɩka a sɩn Juu nɩbɛ ba lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a yɩn bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn wiil ma naa ɩka n taa bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa nɩdɛɛr ɛ, bɩɩ nɩsɔ̃ɔ naa ɛ.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so yɩn wa bʊɔl ma n ba baala ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr ɩka n kʊ̃ wa ɛ, bo 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ bʊɔl ma?”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Ka a Konɩlɩyɔs 'yɛr, “Ber anaar nɩ dɩna ɩn be a die pɔ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na bal baar. Ajienaa lɛ ka a ɩ daba na su fuu ka wʊ nyɩlɛ wa ara a n sɛ̃,

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: tɩ na wʊ bʊɔl ma, ‘Konɩlɩyɔs, Nãaŋmɩn wõn a fʊ sʊɔrfʊ na wʊ tɩɛr a fʊ kʊfʊ fʊn kʊrɔ a nɔ̃n dẽme.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Tʊ̃ nɩrɛ a wʊ chen a Jopa tɩ bʊɔl Simon ban lɛ bʊɔlɔ Pita, wʊ be naa a daba ban bʊɔlɔ a Simon na nɔ̃nɔ̃ gãma ka a yir gbʊr man gogoro.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so ka n mɩ tʊ̃ a fɔɔ a lɛ nɛ bʊɔl fʊ, a fʊ mɩ pɛl pʊɔ tɩ wa. Pʊ̃pãanyã, Nãaŋmɩn niem lɛ sɩ jaa jãnɛ tɩ na chɛl bon jaa a sɩ Soro na tʊ̃n fʊ ɩka fʊ wa 'yɛr kʊ sɩ.”

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita pãa piel na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, “Pʊ̃pãanyã n pãa bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn ba ooro ɛ.

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma den nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa na joro wʊ dãbãɛ tɩ maal a lɛ na tor.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: A 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩr kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ nɩ a nyã'maar 'yɛr na tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a bon jaa Soro sɛ̃.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ala na ɩ a Judiya paal wʊ jaa na piel a Galilee a lɛ a Jɔɔn na wa mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ 'yɛr baar.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ir a Yesu na yi Najaretɩ na wʊ de a wʊ Sɩɛ nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ wʊ. Wʊ yɔn a ka wʊ jaa jilʊ̃ na wʊ maala vɩla sãana nɩ baalsɩ nɩ a bala jaa a dɛɛr sʊɔ na tara, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn ben a wʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Sɩn lɛ nyɛn nɩbir, a ala a jaa wʊn tʊ̃ a Juu nɩbɛ tẽn pɔ, nɩ a Jerusalɛm. Ba nyɔɔ wʊn kpa yɔɔl a daa ju ka wʊ kpi.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ wʊ naa a kũu pɔ, a ber ata daar a wʊ ir ɩ vʊr na wʊ vɛ̃ a ba nyɛ wʊ.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ ba jaa ba lɛ nyɛ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩn na ɩ a dãasɩɛ dẽme a Nãaŋmɩn na de nie ir, sɩn nɩ 'lɔ 'mantaa di tɩ nyũ wʊn wa ir a kũu pɔ baar.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: 'Lɔ lɛ kpãkpãan sɩ ɩka sɩ mʊɔl 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ir ka wʊ ɩ a vʊɛ nɩ a kpĩisɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: 'Lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, nɩrɛ jaa wa sɔɔ de wʊ, wʊn nyɛn yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ tu a wʊ yuor pɔ.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pita na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a anyãna, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa kpɛ a bala jaa na wõnõ a 'yɛr nyãna.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bala na sɔɔ de na tu a Pita wa, nɛɛ 'maa ba naa a lɛ ban nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na pʊr a wʊ Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: A ba wõ ban 'yɛrɛ kɔkɔyor na ba pɛɛrɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn. Ka a Pita pãa 'yɛr,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Nɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaa a nɩbɛ banyãna sɔr ka ba ta so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ ɛ? Ba mɩ nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩtãa a sɩn.”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba naa ka ba so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ nɩ Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor, a ba pãa sʊɔr a Pita ka wʊ be a ba sɛ̃ bãlãa.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a yɛɛr na be a Judiya wõ naa ɩka a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, mɩ sɔɔn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pita na wa lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm a bala na ŋmaa a yʊɔr tɩ sɔɔl mii 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ 'yɛrɛ,

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: “Fʊ ben chen a bala na ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr yir tɩ lɔ̃ɔ din ba wɛ?”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Ka a Pita pãa piel mana a lɛ jaa na ɩ na wʊ kʊ ba,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “N ben a Jopa tẽe pɔ naa sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a wa ɩ ma jaa de dee, ka n nyɛ nyɛfʊ kɔ̃ɔ. N nyɛn bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ fɩka fɔkpɛ̃ɛ ban tur bar ka wʊ yi saaju suu na wʊ gbũ jiir anaar ka wʊ suu wa ara a be ɩn be.

english_verse: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Be lɛ n muul kaa naa nyɛ dʊ̃gbɛɛ anaar jaa bʊrɔ na be ka a wɛr pɔ, mɔdʊ̃n, bʊ̃vʊʊrsɩ lɔ̃ɔn a lubil.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ka n wõ kɔkɔr na 'yɛr, ‘Pita ir na fʊ kʊ 'wɔb.’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka n haa 'yɛr, ‘Kai, n Soro bon jaa na ɩ dɛɛr na ba vɩɛlɛ ɛ, ba kpɛ n nɛɛ pɔ jaa ɛ.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka a kɔkɔr lɛ 'yɛr yi a saaju go, ‘Ta bʊɔl bon jaa Nãaŋmɩn na maal a wʊ vɩɛlɛ bʊ̃dɛɛr ɛ.’

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: A anyãna ɩ naa nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ gbɛɛ ata tɩ lɛ tɔɔ a jaa don be a saaju go.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: “Ajienaa, ka a ɩ a nɩbɛ ata ban tʊ̃ a n sɛ̃ na yi Sisariya mɩ tãn a yir 'lɔ pɔ ɩn kpɩɛrɛ dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma ka n tu ba chen naa taa baala ɛ. Maa nɩ a yɛɛr ayʊɔb banyãna lɛ 'mataa chen na sɩ tɩ kpɛ a daba yir.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: A wʊ man kʊ sɩ a lɛ a malaka na wa a wʊ yir wa 'yɛr, ‘Tʊ̃ nɩbɛ a Jopa ka ba tɩ bʊɔl daba na di Simon ban lɛ bʊɔlɔ Pita.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: 'Lɔ lɛ na waan 'yɛr wa 'yɛr kʊ fʊ ka a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yir dẽme ba jaa tu a 'yɛr 'lɔ pɔ nyɛ faafʊ.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ ka n piel a 'yɛr naa 'yɛrɛ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa a ba sɛ̃ a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ wa a sɩ sɛ̃ a pielfʊ.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Ka n pãa tɩɛrtɔ a lɛ a sɩ Soro na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, ‘Jɔɔn den kʊ̃ɔ son, tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ Nãaŋmɩn na den a wʊ Sɩɛ son yɩ.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ ba, a kʊfʊ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ kʊ sɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ sɩn sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto, maa mɩ nɩ ana, na ŋmaa Nãaŋmɩn sɔr?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Ban wõ a anyãna, baa lɛ haa nɛɛ ɛ, tɩ pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ, “Nãaŋmɩn pãa yuon sɔr kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ban lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr nyɛ nyɛvʊr.”

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ban kʊ a Sɩteven ka a pupuorbo bamɩne yɛ̃yɛl tɩ yi Fonisiya, Sipurusɩ nɩ Antiok, na ba mana a 'yɛr kʊrɔ a Juu nɩbɛ tɛɛ.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka a pupuorbo bamɩne na yi a Sipurusɩ nɩ a Sirenɩ, chen a Antiok na ba mɩ tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊrɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: A sɩ Soro nũu dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ ba ka a nɩyɔɔ dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔ de na ba lɩɛb turo a sɩ Soro.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Ka a 'yɛr nyã lo a pupuorbo na be a Jerusalɛm toor pɔ, a ba ir Banabasɩ tʊ̃ a be Antiok.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Wʊn tɩ ta nyɛ a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na wãɛ ba, nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpɛ̃ɔ tɩ 'yɛr, ka ba ara nɩ yelmãɛ nɩ a ba nyãa wʊ jaa kʊ a sɩ Soro.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Banabasɩ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ nɩsʊ̃ na sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wʊ tara sɔɔfʊ na wʊ mɩ tɔɔ nɩyɔɔ waan a ba wa turo a sɩ Soro.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Ala puor lɛ ka a Banabasɩ pãa yɩ a be tu Tasusɩ tɩ bʊɔrɔ a Sɔɔl.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Wʊn tɩ nyɛ wʊ, ba lɩɛb wa Antiok na ba be be yuon gbul, ka a Banabasɩ naa Sɔɔl wile a nɩyɔɔ bala. Be a Antiok lɛ a pupuorbo nyɛ a yuor, “Kɩrɩsɩto turbo.”

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ yi be a Jerusalɛm wa Antiok.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: A ba ãsʊɔ yuor na di Agabus ir ara, ka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛr, “Kɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na lon a Worom wɛr wʊ jaa.” A kɔ̃ nyãna wa lo naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Kɩlaudiyus na di a nãa.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: A poturbo banyãna mɔ̃ naa na ba ir bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ lɛ a ba fɔ̃ɔ na jã na ba sʊ̃ʊn a yɛɛr bala na be a Judiya.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ban wa ir a kʊfʊ anyãna, Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl lɛ ba tʊ̃n a ba tɩ kʊ a nɩbɛrɛ.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a Nãa Hɛrɔd nyɔɔr a pupuorbo bamɩne na wʊn ge.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka ba ŋmaa Jemesɩ na ɩ a Jɔɔn yɛbɛ ju.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: A Hɛrɔd nyɛn a lɛ ka a pɛl a Juu nɩbɛ pʊɔ, ka wʊ nyɔɔ a Pita mɩ. A anyãna ɩ naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban tara a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ.

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
translation_verse: Wʊn nyɔɔ a Pita a lɛ, a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ lɛ wʊ tɩ pɔɔ wʊ, tɩ na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ sojasɩ jiir anaar ka ba jʊrɔ taa kaara nɩ wʊ. Bojũu wʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a Gõl Bar Difʊ wʊ po baar tɩchɛ ka wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ka nɩrɛ jaa nyɛ.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ ba gu a Pita be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a pupuorbiir mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ wʊ.

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: A tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a wɛr na chaar ka a Hɛrɔd na vɛ̃ ka ba waan a Pita ka wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, bɔ̃jɔrsɩ ayi lɛ ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Pita gbɛb sojasɩ ayi ka wʊ be a ba sãlsɔɔ gã guro, ka a sojasɩ bamɩne ara a pɔɔfʊ die dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ gu.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Ajienaa lɛ a sɩ Soro malaka wa kpɛ ara a wʊ sɛ̃ be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka a die wʊ jaa chaar. Ka a malaka ŋme a Pita jaa 'mɛ 'mɛ 'mɛ, sãɩ wʊ 'yɛr, “Pɔɔ fɔɔ jaa ir,” ka a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ for a Pita nũ'yɔ̃ɔ pɔ lo.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a malaka 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, “Su a fuu nɩ a nafɔbɔ na fʊ 'mɔɔ a fɔbaa vaar na fʊ turo nɩ ma.” Ka a Pita ɩ a lɛ a malaka na 'yɛr.

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Ba tɩ yin a dɔ̃dɔr dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ pɔ, na ba lɛ tɩ yi a dɔ̃dɔr ayi sʊɔ pɔ, tɩ pãa ta a yõo dɔ̃dɔr a kur pãn na pɔɔ. Ban wa ta a wʊ yuo wʊ yõ a ba yi. A malaka tara na a Pita den a sɔr dẽdẽe ãsʊɔ chiin bãlãa tɩ bar wʊ tɩ chen ajienaa.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Ka Pita tɩɛr pãa yuo a wʊ 'yɛr, “Pʊ̃pãanyã n bɔ̃ɔn naa an ba ɩ baal ɛ, ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ a wʊ malaka a wʊ wa 'loo ma faa a Hɛrɔd nũu pɔ, nɩ a lɛ jaa a Juu nɩbɛ na tɩɛr ɩka ba ge ma.”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: A anyãna wʊn wa tɩɛr yuo, wʊ chen a Mɛɛr na ɩ a Jɔɔn Maakɩ ma yir, be lɛ a pupuorbiir yɔɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ wʊ.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Pita wa kpoor a yõo dɔ̃dɔr pãn, ka a gbãgbaa pɔɔbile ban bʊɔlɔ Woroda chen a pãn yuofʊ jie.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Wʊn wa wõ ka a ɩ Pita kɔkɔr lɛ ka a nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ wʊ tɩ põn bar, waa yuo a pãn ɛ, tɩ lɩɛb jo kpɛ a die pɔ tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Pita lɛ be a yõo dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ!”

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: A ba tãn wʊ pĩpĩi bar tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Gelfʊ baal lɛ kpɛ fʊ!” Tɩ wʊ chɛnɛ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir 'yɛrɛ ɩka sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, ka ba 'yɛr, “A wʊ malaka tɔ̃ɔ lɛ.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita chɛnɛ kpooro a pãn, ka ba tɩ yuo nyɛ ka a ɩ 'lɔ lɛ ka a nɛɛ 'maa ba.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Ka a Pita wɩlwɩl ba a nũu a ba bar gɔ̃n tɩchɛ ka wʊ man a lɛ a sɩ Soro na yin wʊ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ. Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Jemesɩ nɩ a yɛɛr, na wʊ bar ba tɩ yi chen jiyuo.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: A wɛr na chaar bibio, ka a sojasɩ ɩ chĩcham na ba sʊrɔ taa, “Bo lɛ na tara a Pita?”

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Hɛrɔd vɛ̃n ka ba yɔbɔ wʊ faa na baa nyɛ wʊ ɛ, na wʊ kpãkpãan a sojasɩ mɩ faa tɩ 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ ba. Ka a Hɛrɔd pãa yi a Judiya na wʊ chen a Sisariya na wʊ tɩ jãnɛ a be bãlãa.

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: A Hɛrɔd dɔ̃ɔ tara a Tire nɩbɛ nɩ a Sidon nɩbɛ nɩ jɛɛr, ka ba nyɛtaa nɛɛ na wa a nãa 'maalfʊ jie. Blastus na ɩ a nãa gbãgbaa wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sɛ̃ lɛ ba tʊ̃ ɩka wʊ 'maal a nãa kʊ ba bojũu a wʊ wɛr pɔ lɛ ba nyɛrɛ a ba bʊ̃diir.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Bɩbir kɔ̃ɔ, a Nãa Hɛrɔd sun a wʊ nafɔɔr jãnɛ nɩ a nãa dakɔɔ ju, na wʊn 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Ka a nɩbɛ ŋmiere chɛlsɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Ŋmɩn kɔkɔr lɛ a nyãna na 'yɛrɛ, nɩrɛ ba lɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Ajienaa lɛ ka a sɩ Soro malaka wa 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ baal bojũu waa de a 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, ka a bufulo 'wɔb a nyaba ŋma ka wʊ kpi.

english_verse: God's message kept spreading.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr chɛnɛ yaara.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl na wa tʊ̃ a ba tʊ̃mɔ̃ baar a Jerusalɛm, ba tara naa Jɔɔn Maakɩ 'mataa chiin.

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ ayi banyãna den a sɔr ka a Nãaŋmɩn de ba nie suun be a Selusiya na ba tɩ kpɛ a gboro chiine a Sipurusɩ.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a Elimasɩ na ɩ a jɩbʊrɔ a wʊ yuor par lɛ a lɛ nɛ, ŋmaan a Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl sɔr ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka a tẽkaara nyãna wʊ sɔɔ a Yesu ɛ.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Ka a Sɔɔl nyãna ban bʊɔlɔ a Pɔɔl na sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kaara a Elimasɩ jaa tãɩɩ tɩ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɩ Soro na ŋme fʊ naa ka fʊ jɔ̃ɔ tɩ tãn sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ, fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ chãa ɛ.” Ajienaa lɛ a Elimasɩ nɩbie de, ka a libe pɔɔ wʊ, ka wʊ bɔɔlɔ bʊɔrɔ nɩrɛ na nyɔɔ wʊ nũu.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: A lɛ a tẽkaara na nyɛ a lɛ na ɩ, wʊ sɔɔn de, bojũu nɛɛ 'maa wʊ naa a lɛ ban wile a sɩ Soro yele.

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl nɩ a wʊ taaba kpɛ̃n a gboro yi a Pafos chen be a Perga na be a Pamfiliya be lɛ a Jɔɔn Maakɩ tɩ bar ba tɩ lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Ka ba yi a Perga chen Antiok na gbʊr a Pisidiya, a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir daar ba kpɛ̃n a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie na ba tɩ jãnɛ.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Ban wa kar a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ nɩ a ala a Nãaŋmɩn 'Yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb baar, a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie nɩbɛrɛ sʊʊr naa Pɔɔl nɩ a wʊ taaba, “Yɛɛr, yɩn tara 'yɛr na sʊ̃ʊn a nɩbɛ sɩ kʊ yɩn sɔr, yɩ 'yɛr.”

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: A Nãaŋmɩn nyã a sɩn Isɩral nɩbɛ na puoro lɛ dɔ̃ɔ chɛl ir a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na wʊ vɛ̃ ka ba dɔɔ paal be a Ijipitɩ pɔ. Kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ de faan ba yin a paal 'lɔ pɔ,

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr taa wa a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Paal bʊrsɩ ayopõi lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn dii bar be a Kanaan tɩ de a wɛr 'lɔ kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ ka a ɩ ba sofʊ.

english_verse: years. Then God gave our people judges until the time of the prophet Samuel,
translation_verse: ). A anyã puor, Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ban 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ tɩ tãn a Samuwɛl na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ sɔ̃ɔ.
