english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Wʊn wa ir a Sɔɔl bar, wʊ den a David ka wʊ ɩ a ba nãa. Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, ‘N nyɛn a David na ɩ a Jesɩ bidaba ka a n ãgãn 'maa, wʊn maal yele a jaa ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: “A Nãa David nyãna bal par pɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ir a Fafaara Yesu kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ nɩtãa lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a Yesu wa, Jɔɔn mʊɔlɔ naa a tɩɛr lɩɛbfʊ 'yɛr na wʊ suoro a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Lɛ a Jɔɔn na wa baaraa a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘An lɛ yɩ tɩɛr ɩka n ɩ naa? N ba ɩ a 'lɔ yɩn tɩɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ben a n puor waara, n ba sɛɛ ɩka n for a wʊ nafɔbɔ ɛ.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Maa yɛɛr na ɩ Abraham yɔn nɩ a yɩn na joro a Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ na ɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a faafʊ nyãna wa.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Bojũu, a nɩbɛ na be a Jerusalɛm nɩ a ba nɩbɛrɛ ba chɛl a Yesu ɛ, na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ 'yɛr ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb a ba ma kara a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir jaa ɩ.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Ban wa maal a lɛ jaa ban sɛb a wʊ yele baar, a wʊ poturbo yɔɔ wʊn a daa ju na ba tɩ ũu a yaa pɔ.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn ir wʊ a kũu pɔ.

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: A ber yɔɔ na wa po, a bala wʊn dɔ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔ yɔn yin a Galilee tɩ tãn a Jerusalɛm, ba nyɛ wʊ naa, bala lɛ ɩ a dãasɩɛ dẽme a pʊ̃pãanyã kʊ a sɩ nɩbɛ.

english_verse: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
translation_verse: “Sɩn 'yɛrɛ naa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna kʊrɔ yɩ, a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne.

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn maal kʊ a sɩn na ɩ a yɔn a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne, na wʊ sãɩ a Yesu yin a kũu pɔ. Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Yiel Gãma ayi pɔ, ‘Fʊ ɩ naa a maa bie, dɩna n lɩɛb a fʊ sãa.’ (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ nɩ nyã, wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ kpi pʊ̃ɔ go ɛ, a 'yɛr nyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr, ‘Ɩn kʊ yɩ naa a yelsʊ̃n nɩ a sʊ̃ʊfʊ 'lɔ ɩn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a David.’ (Isaiya

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: A jie ãsʊɔ lɛ 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Fʊ kʊ̃ vɛ̃ ka a fʊ Chɛchɛ Sʊɔ kpɛ pʊ̃ɔfʊ jie ɛ.’ (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: “A Nãa David na wa tʊ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ a wʊ sɔ̃ɔ, wʊ kpi naa ka ba ũu wʊ be a wʊ sãamɩne yar par, ka a wʊ ãgãn pʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na sãɩ a kũu pɔ, ba kpɛ pʊ̃ɔfʊ jie ɛ.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: “A lɛ jũu n yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ka a tu naa a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba mʊɔl yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ kʊ yɩ.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: ‘Nyɛ a yɩn na kpɛrɛ, yɩ bʊ̃bʊr na yɩ wɛ, bojũu ɩn maal yele kɔ̃ɔ a yɩ sɔ̃ɔ yɩn ba be na sɔɔ de ɛ, ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ gba 'yɛr kʊ yɩ.’ ” (Habakuk

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ na wa yire a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, a nɩbɛ lɛ sʊɔr ba naa ɩka ba lɛ wa a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar wa 'yɛr a 'yɛr nyãna go.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Lɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ dẽme na wa yaar, a Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie nɩ a bala na lɩɛb a Juu nɩbɛ turo naa Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ na ba bʊrɔ nɩ ba kpãkpãana ba 'yɛrɛ ka ba mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ nyã pɔ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir na lɛ wa ta, a tẽdẽme yɔɔ jie tɩ lɔ̃ɔn taa na wõ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ ba nyãna, nyuur kpɛ ba naa ka ba tʊrɔ chɩrɛ nɩ lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ ba jo ba dãbãɛ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Sɩn 'lɔ, sɩn den nie 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ. Tɩchɛ yɩn ben tɔr bar na yaa kaa a yɩ mãɛ ɩka yɩ sɛɛn naa a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ sɩn lɩɛb chen a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ sɛ̃.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a sɩ Soro kpãkpãan sɩ ɩka sɩ ɩ, ‘Ɩn maal fʊ naa ka fʊ ɩ chãa kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka fʊn chiin a faafʊ 'yɛr tɩ tãn a tẽe baaraa.’ ” (Isaiya

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: A lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na wõ a 'yɛr ba pʊɔ pɛl naa ka ba de 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr, ka a bala jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na ir ɩka ba nyɛ a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, sɔɔ de.

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Ka a sɩ Soro 'yɛr yaar a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ dam a pɔbɔ bala na turo a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ tɩ ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, nɩ a tẽe nɩdierbe 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba dii a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ bar a paal 'lɔ pɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Ka ba pãpã a ba gbɛɛ tã-uul 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ka a wiil ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na ge ba naa tɩ na ba chen a Ikoniyum.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ka a nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a poturbo bala ka ba sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Be a Ikoniyum lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ ma chen a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, be lɛ ba man a 'yɛr jaa nɛ nɛ nɛ ka a nɩyɔɔ bamɩne na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɔɔ de a 'yɛr.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bala na ba sɔɔ de ɛ, dam a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɔ̃ɔ a ba tɩɛr ka baa bara a yɛɛr ɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ jɛ̃n a be na ba kor bãlãa, na ba 'yɛrɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr ba joro dãbãɛ ɛ, ka a sɩ Soro mãɛ wiil ɩka a wʊ wãɛfʊ 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ na wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ba a ba tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ yelwiilsɩ nɩ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Ka a tẽdẽme ŋma jiir ayi, a bamɩne pʊɔ a Juu nɩbɛ, ka a bamɩne mɩ pʊɔ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Nɛchɩrɛ lon a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ nɩ a ba nɩbɛrɛ chara pɔ, ka ban maal Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ dɛɛr lɔb ba nɩ kʊsɩbɛ.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: A be a Lɩstɩra, dagbɛrɛ kɔ̃ɔ ben be ban lɔ̃ɔ dɔɔn ka waa chen kaa jaa ɛ.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Wʊ chɛlɛ naa lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ ka a Pɔɔl kaa wʊ jaa tãɩɩ nyɛ na wʊ bɔ̃ɔn wʊn tara sɔɔfʊ na sa.

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: A wʊ bʊɔl a daba 'yɛr, “Ir ara naa fʊ gbɛɛ.” Ka a daba vaa ir na wʊ piel chiine.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Lɛ a nɩbɛ na wa nyɛ a lɛ a Pɔɔl na ɩ, ka ba ŋme chɛlma nɩ a Likoniya kɔkɔr pɔ, “A ŋmɩmɛ lɩɛb nɩbɛ suu wa a sɩ sɛ̃!”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Ba bʊɔlɔ naa a Banabasɩ “Jeyusɩ,” na ba bʊɔlɔ a Pɔɔl mɩ “Hɛmɩsɩ,” bojũu 'lɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Ka a Jeyusɩ nyã puorfʊ yir na dɔ̃ɔ ara a tẽe kõkoor bɔɔrloro waan nɩ nadai nɩ a vapuulsɩ bojũu 'lɔ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ bala bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba lo bɔɔr kʊ ba.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ, Banabasɩ nɩ a Pɔɔl na wa wõ a lɛ, ba tɔɔn a ba fuusɩ chɩɛr tɩ jo kpɛ be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ tɩ tãna,

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: “Nɩbɛ, bãa lɛ yɩ maala a lɛ? Sɩn mɩ ɩn nɩbɛ, nɩsaalba a yɩ kaar. Sɩn waan naa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã wa kʊ yɩ, na sɩ 'yɛrɛ yɩ ɩka, yɩ lɩɛb yi a yelpɔrsɩ anyãna pɔ, tɩ turo a Nãaŋmɩn vʊr. 'Lɔ lɛ maal a saaju, nɩ a tẽe, nɩ a man kpɛ̃ɛ, nɩ bon jaa na be be.

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: A kõr jaa wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bar ka nɩrɛ jaa turo a wʊ sɔr.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma wiil a lɛ wʊn ɩ maal vɩla sʊɔ, na wʊ ma wiil a wʊ maal vɩla vɛ̃ ka a saa yi saaju waara, ka a bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ sɔ̃ɔ mɩ be be. Wʊ ma kʊ yɩn a bʊ̃diir yɔɔ ka yɩ pʊɔ pɛl.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: A 'yɛr nyãna gba ban 'yɛr, tɩchɛ a chɛnɛ na ɩ tuo ka ban ŋmaa a nɩbɛ sɔr ka ban bar a bɔɔr lofʊ.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na pãa yi a Antiok nɩ a Ikoniyum wa tɔ̃n a nɩyɔɔ bala 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba lɔb a Pɔɔl nɩ kʊsɩbɛ vu yin bar a tẽe kõkoor tɩɛr man tãa wʊ kpi naa.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo tɩ ŋmaa jilʊ̃ wʊ ara nɩ ka wʊ ir kpɛ a tẽe pɔ. A wɛr na wa chaar ka 'lɔ naa Banabasɩ chen a Debi.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba tɩ mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ, ka a nɩyɔɔ jaa sɔɔ turo a Yesu. A ba pãa lɩɛb chen a Lɩstɩra, Ikoniyum nɩ a Antiok,

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: tɩ mʊlɔ a pupuorbiir 'yɔ̃ɔn ba ãkpãɛn ka ba ara nɩ yelmãɛ a sɔɔfʊ pɔ, na ba 'yɛrɛ, “Dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ pɔ lɛ sɩn tu tɩ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ ir nɩdierbe kʊ ba a ba kaara a puorfʊ dẽme ãsʊɔ jaa. Ba gal kɔ̃ na ba sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn tɩ de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro ban sɔɔ nũu pɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Ba kpɛ̃n tala a Pisidiya, ba wa ta naa Pamfiliya,

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: ban wa mʊɔl a 'yɛr a Perga, ba suun chen be a Ataliya.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Be a Ataliya lɛ ba yi tɩ kpɛ a gboro chen a Antiok. Be lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nũu pɔ ka wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ba, a ba tʊ̃ɔ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ a be tʊ̃ baar.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Ban tɩ tara jaa lɛ ba bʊɔl a pupuorbiir lɔ̃ɔ taa na ba man a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na de ba tʊ̃n nɩ a lɛ mɩ wʊn yuo sɔr kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ de.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Ba jɛ̃n a be kor bãlãa nɩ a poturbo.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Nɩbɛ bamɩne yin be a Judiya suun wa a Antiok wa wile a yɛɛr 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩn baa sɔɔ ka ba ŋmaa yɩ a yʊɔr nɩtãa lɛ a Mosesɩ na bin ɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ faafʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: A anyãna vɛ̃ naa ka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ nɩ a nɩbɛ mĩi 'yɔ̃ɔ taa ka a ɩ nɛchɩrɛ. Be lɛ a ba ir Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ nɩ a pupuorbiir bamɩne 'yɔ̃ɔ ɩka ba chen a Jerusalɛm tɩ nyɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr nyãna.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: A pupuorbiir na wa bar ba a sɔr, ba chen tɩ yi a Fonisiya nɩ a Samariya na ba man a lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na lɩɛb puoro a Nãaŋmɩn. Ka a yɛɛr ba jaa pʊɔ pɛl nɩ a 'yɛr nyã.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Ban wa ta Jerusalɛm, a pupuorbiir nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ de ban nuur ayi, ka ba man a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na de ba tʊ̃n.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Ka a pupuorbiir bamɩne na chɛnɛ tara a Farasee wiilfʊ ir ara tɩ 'yɛr, “A nɩyor banyãna, a sɛɛ naa ka ban ŋma a ba yɔɩ na ba tu a lɛ a Mosesɩ na bin.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɔ̃ɔn taa na ban guor a 'yɛr nyãna.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: A lɛ ban mana taa tɩ ta sɔ̃ɔ, Pita ir 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɛɛr, yɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn ir man a yɩ pɔ, ka a bala na ba sɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, mɩ na wõ a faafʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr yi a n nɛɛ pɔ, na ba sɔɔ de.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn na bɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa nyãa lɛ wiil ɩka wʊ sɔɔn de ba na wʊ kʊ ba a wʊ Sɩɛ nɩtãa lɛ wʊn kʊ sɩ.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ mʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ bɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn kaa, na yɩ tuo a poturbo tuor a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na ba dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ɔ tuo ɛ?

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Ban wa 'yɛr baar ka a Jemesɩ mɩ 'yɛr, “Yɛɛr, yɩ chɛlɛ ka.

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon man kʊ sɩ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩɛrtɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ ɩka wʊn ir nɩbɛ a ba pɔ, kʊ a wʊ mãɛ.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘A anyã puor, ɩn lɩɛb tɩ mɛ a Nãa David yir 'lɔ na lo. Ɩn mɛn a 'lɔ na ŋmɛr, naa lɛ maal wʊ.

english_verse: I promised it long ago.”
translation_verse: ka ba bɔ̃ɔn a kõr jaa jaa.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: “Maa 'yɛr nɩ nyã ɩka sɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a ɩ tuo kʊ a nɩyor banyãna na lɩɛrɛ waara a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ yɩ ɩ a sɩ sɛb kʊ ba ɩka yaa, ba taa dire tɩɩ bon ɛ, na ba taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ, dʊ̃ɔ na var kpi ba taa ɔɔrɔ ɛ, na ba taa ɔɔrɔ jãɩ ɛ.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Bojũu, lɛ lɛ a Mosesɩ bin a nɛɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ ma mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nyãna a tẽn a jaa ka ba mɩ ma kar naa a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar jaa.”

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɔ̃ɔn a pupuorbiir ba jaa pãa kaa ir nɩbɛ a ba pɔ, ka ban 'matãan a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ tʊ̃ ka ba chen a Antiok. Judas ban bʊɔlɔ Basabasɩ nɩ Silasɩ lɛ ba ir, a nɩbɛ ayi banyãna mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ nɩbɛrɛ pʊɔ a yɛɛr.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Bala lɛ ba tʊ̃n a gãn nyãna, “A sɩn nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na ɩ a yɩ yɛɛr puor naa a yɩn na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na ɩ a sɩ yɛɛr be a Antiok nɩ a Siriya nɩ a Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Sɩ wõn a bamɩne na yi a sɩ sɛ̃ sɩn ba kʊ ba sɔr ɛ, tɩ vuro yɩ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ yɩ 'yɛrɛ.

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so ka sɩ jaa sɔɔ ir nɩbɛ bamɩne tʊ̃ ka ban wa a yɩ sɛ̃, 'matãan a sɩ barmɩne Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ,

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: a nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ sɔɔ kũu a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor jũu.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu lɛ sɩ tʊ̃n a Judas nɩ a Silasɩ ka ban tɩ man a ba nɛɛ a lɛ sɩn sɛb.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: A vɩɛlɛ na kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩ a sɩn mɩ sɛ̃, ka sɩ kʊ̃ lɛ tuo yɩ tuor dɔɔl a anyãna ju ɛ.

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: Yɩ taa dire tɩɩ bon ɛ, yɩ taa ɔɔrɔ jãɩ ɛ, na yɩ taa ɔɔrɔ dʊ̃ɔ na var kpi nʊ̃n ɛ, bɩɩ sɛ̃nɛ ɛ. Yɩ mɔ̃ na yɩ nyɔɔ yɩ mãɛ a yelsɩ anyãna pɔ. Yɩ jɛ̃ vɩla.”

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Ban wa bar a Pɔɔl, nɩ a Banabasɩ, nɩ a Judas nɩ a Silasɩ sɔr, ka ba suu chen be a Antiok, be lɛ ba tɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa a bala na puoro ba jaa na ba de a gãn kʊ ba.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ na wa kar a gãn a 'yɔ̃ɔ ban ãkpãɛn ka ba pʊɔ pɛl.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Judas nɩ a Silasɩ mɩ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr yɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔn a yɛɛr ãkpãɛn.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Ban wa jɛ̃ a be kor bãlãa, ka a yɛɛr lɛ bar ba sɔr nɩ nyã'maar ka ba lɩɛb chen a bala na tʊ̃ ba sɛ̃.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ jɛ̃ be a Antiok, be lɛ a bala nɩ a ba taaba bamɩne wile tɩ mʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr.

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: A mɩn lɛ wa kor bãlãa ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ a Banabasɩ, “Vɛ̃ ka sɩ lɛ chen tɩ piir a yɛɛr kaa a tẽn ala pɔ sɩn mʊɔl a sɩ Soro 'yɛr na sɩ kaa ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ba irɛ.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Ka a Banabasɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ de a Jɔɔn Maakɩ ka ba 'mataa chen.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl ba tɩɛr ɩka a ba tara sɔr ɛ, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bar ba be a Pamfiliya ba lɛ pɔɔ ba chen a tʊ̃mɔ̃ jie ɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba tãn tãn taa na ba ŋma dẽdẽme, ka a Banabasɩ de a Jɔɔn Maakɩ na wʊ kpɛ̃n a gboro chiin be a Sipurusɩ.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl mɩ ir a Silasɩ ka a yɛɛr de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro nũu pɔ, ka wʊn sʊ̃ʊ ba.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Wʊ tun a Siriya nɩ a Sɩlɩsɩya 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bala na puoro ãkpãɛn.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl tɩ chen a Debi na wʊ tɩ po chen a Lɩstɩra, be lɛ wʊ tɩ nyɛ a pupuoro ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Timoti ka a wʊ ma ɩ Juu nɩrɛ na wʊ puoro, tɩchɛ ka a wʊ sãa ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Ka a yɛɛr na be a Lɩstɩra nɩ a Ikoniyum pʊr a Timoti yovɩla.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ tara wʊ 'mataa chiine na wʊ ŋmaa wʊ a yʊɔr, bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ a be bɔ̃ɔn naa a Timoti sãa na ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: A lɛ ban lʊɔrɔ a tẽn, lɛ mɩ lɛ ba mana kʊrɔ ba lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩdierbe na be a Jerusalɛm na 'yɛr bin ka nɩbɛ ba turo.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Ba 'yɔ̃ɔn a pupuorbiir ãkpãɛn ka a nɩbɛ mɩ waara pʊɔrɔ bɩbir jaa.

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl nɩ a wʊ taaba tun a Fɩrgɩya nɩ a Galatiya paal pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ŋmaa ba sɔr a ba taa mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr a be a Asɩya ɛ.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Ban wa gbʊr a Misiya wɛr pɔ, ba ta mʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba chen a Bitiniya tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ba kʊ ba sɔr ɛ.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: A ba vɩɛ a Misiya bar tɩ suu chen be a Toroyas.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: A tãsɔɔ lɛ a Pɔɔl nyɛ jãna pɔ, daba na yi Masedonɩya ara sʊɔrɔ wʊ 'yɛrɛ, “Gɔ̃ɔ wa ka a Masedonɩya wa sʊ̃ʊ sɩ.”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na nyɛ a nyɛfʊ nyãna, sɩ ir chɔɔr a lɛ nɛ na sɩ chen a Masedonɩya, na sɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ bʊɔl sɩ a sɩ tɩ mʊɔl a wʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ ba.

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: A Toroyas sɩn yi tɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a gboro a dẽdẽe a lɛ nɛ chen a Samotɩrasɩ, ka a wɛr na wa chaar, sɩ tãn a Neapolis.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Be lɛ sɩ yin gbɛɛ chiin a Filipai, Worom dẽme lɛ dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ a be na wʊ mɩ ɩ a Masedonɩya jikpɛ̃ɛ, be lɛ sɩ jãnɛ maal ber aŋmɩnɛ jaa.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar, sɩ yin a tẽe pɔ suu a põn pɔ, be lɛ sɩ tɩɛr ɩka sɩn nyɛn jie ban ma sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn. Sɩ jãnɛ naa be na sɩ piel 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊrɔ pɔbɔ na lɔ̃ɔ a be.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: A pɔbɔ banyãna na chɛlɛ ãsʊɔ yuor din Lidiya na yara fɔjɩɩr, na wʊ yi a Tayatira tẽe pɔ, wʊ mɩ puoro naa Nãaŋmɩn. A sɩ Soro yuon a wʊ tɩɛr ka wʊ sɔɔ de a lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛr.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: 'Lɔ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa na wa so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, wʊ bʊɔl sɩ naa a sɩ wa a wʊ yir a wʊ wa 'yɛr, “Ala ɩ yɩ sɔɔ naa ɩka n sɔɔn de a sɩ Soro, sɩrɛ jaa yɩ wa jɛ̃ a n yir.” Lɛ lɛ wʊ mʊlɔ sɩ.

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Daar kɔ̃ɔ sɩn lɛ chiine a jie 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie, sɩ tuor gbãgbapɔɔbile na tara a sɩdɛɛr na wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ yele na ɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ. A lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ na wʊ nyɛrɛ libiyɔɔ kʊrɔ a bala na so wʊ.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Ka a pɔɔbile nyãna turo a Pɔɔl na wʊ turo sɩ tãna 'yɛrɛ, “A nɩbɛ banyãna ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be saaju tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na ba wile yɩ a faafʊ sɔr.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ a pɔɔbile mar bɩbir jaa ka a Pɔɔl wa vʊʊr faa tɩ lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a sɩɛ 'lɔ na be a pɔɔbile pɔ ɩka, “Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor pɔ, n 'yɛr kʊ fʊ ɩka fʊ wa yi a pɔɔbile pɔ.” Ajienaa ka a sɩɛ yi tɩchɛ wʊ bar.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: A pɔɔbile sorbo na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka baa lɛ nyɛrɛ tɔ̃nɔ̃ a pɔɔbile sɛ̃ ɛ, ba nyɔɔn a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ tɔɔ chiin be a daa pɔ a nɩbɛrɛ niem.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: Ba tara na yelsɩ kɔ̃ɔ an ba tara sɔr kʊ a sɩn Worom nɩbɛ a sɩn sɔɔ bɩɩ maal ɛ.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala ir pʊɔ na baa bara a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ ɛ, ka a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ vɛ̃ ka ba yaar a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ fuusɩ bar tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba fɔb ba.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: A lɛ ban wa fɔb ba sɛɛ, ba nyɔɔr ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na guro a pɔɔfʊ die, a wʊ bɔ̃ɔ kaara ba.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Wʊn wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ tɩɛ ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a die 'lɔ na be a sãlsɔɔ tɩ lũ a ba gbɛɛ mar daa.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: A tãsɔɔ bãɩn lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn, na ba yiele nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yiel, ka a ba taaba ban lɔ̃ɔ pɔɔn ba chɛlɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Ajienaa ka a tẽe dam kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, ka a pɔɔfʊ yir par kparaa mɩ dam. Ka a pɔɔfʊ der dɔ̃dɔɩ yuo yuor a jaa, ka nɩrɛ jaa bɔ̃jɔrma ban lũn wʊ for for.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Ka a 'lɔ na guro a pɔɔfʊ die jɛɔ ir na wʊ nyɛ a lɛ a pɔɔfʊ der dɔ̃dɔɩ na yuor, ka wʊ vʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ̃n wʊ mãɛ, bojũu wʊ man tãa a pɔɔfʊ dẽme yin jo.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl tãn, “Ta ge fʊ mãɛ ɛ, sɩ jaa ben ka.”

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: A wʊ bʊɔl a ba waan fãtɩn wa kʊ wʊ a wʊ pɔɔ kpɛ̃n be a die pɔ, na wʊ tɩ lo gbur dumo mɩrɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ niem.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Tɩ pãa ir tara ba yin tɩ sʊʊr, “Nɩbɛrɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn ɩrɛ wa nyɛ faafʊ?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: A ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro Yesu, fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yir dẽme na nyɛn faafʊ.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba pãa 'yɛr a sɩ Soro 'yɛr kʊ wʊ nɩ a bala jaa na be a wʊ yir.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a pɔɔfʊ die guguro de ba yin a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ tɩ pɛɛ a ba natɩɛ tɩchɛ a ba mɩ tɔ̃ɔ so a 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Ka a pɔɔfʊ die guguro tara ba kpɛ̃n a wʊ yir tɩ maal bʊ̃diir kʊ ba, ka nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a wʊ yir dẽme, bojũu 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa sɔɔn de a Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Wɛr na wa chaar a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ ir a ba tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ tʊ̃ na ba 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na guro a pɔɔfʊ die ɩka, a wʊ yuo a dɔɔr bala bar.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Ka a pɔɔfʊ die guguro 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, “A 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ tʊ̃n ɩka n yuo a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a Silasɩ bar. A lɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn tʊ̃ɔ chiine, yɩ chiine nɩ nyã'maar.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ a bala ban tʊ̃, “Chãa pɔ jaa lɛ ba vɛ̃ a ba fɔb sɩ, baa tɔɔr a sɩ 'yɛr ɛ, sɩ gba ɩ naa a Worom dẽme tɩ ba 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ. Pʊ̃pãanyã a ba lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sɔɔl yuo sɩ bar? Bala mãɛ ba wa yuo sɩ bar.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na tɩ man a lɛ kʊ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ, ban wa wõ ɩka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ ɩ naa Worom dẽme, dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Ka a bala mãɛ pãa chen tɩ 'maal ba yin a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩ sʊɔr ba ɩka ba yi a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ na wa yi a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ba chen a Lidiya yir, be lɛ ba tɩ tuor a yɛɛr ka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpãɛn tɩ pãa yi a be chen.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Ba tun a Amfipolis nɩ Apoloniya na ba wa a Tɛsalonika, be lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir be.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pɔɔl na ma irɛ lɛ wʊ chen be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir, Pɛ̃nfʊsɩ gbɛɛ ata jaa lɛ wʊ kpɛ a be ka a 'lɔ nɩ a bala vɩɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma.

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: Wʊ mana na wile a lɛ an sɛɛ a Nɩ-iraa na di dɔɔyɛ tɩ na wʊ ir a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr, “A Yesu nyãna ɩn ben 'yɛrɛ a wʊ 'yɛr, lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne sɔɔ a lɛ na ba pʊɔ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ, 'matãan a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ puoro a Nãaŋmɩn lɔ̃ɔn pɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a nyuur kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ, ka ba bɔ a ba taaba nɩbomo be a daa pɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa, na ba piel gɔ̃mɔ̃ be a tẽe pɔ. Ba tɩ sɛɛn a Jason yir bʊɔrɔ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ ɩka ba yin chiin ba a nɩyɔɔ bala niem.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ban ba wa tɩ nyɛ ba ɛ, ba nyɔɔn a Jason nɩ a yɛɛr bamɩne chiin a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ niem tɩ tãna 'yɛrɛ. “A dɔɔr banyãna lɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wɛr wʊ jaa, ba wa tãn ka pʊ̃pãanyã

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: ka a Jason tuor de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ yir. Ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa a Nãa Siisa chire na ba 'yɛrɛ ɩka nãa ãsʊɔ ben be ban bʊɔlɔ Yesu.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: A lɛ a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ bala na wõ a 'yɛr nyãna, a dam ba naa.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: A ba pãa 'yɔ̃ɔ a Jason nɩ a yɛɛr bala a ba yab libie tɩ ba bar ba sɔr.

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: A lɛ a wɛr na wa tɔ̃ɔ sɔb, ka a yɛɛr bar a Pɔɔl nɩ Silasɩ sɔr ka ba chen be a Beriya. Ban tɩ ta, ba chen be a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: A Juu nɩbɛ bala na be a Beriya ɩn chɛchɛlbɛ gar a bala na be a Tɛsalonika, bojũu ba den a 'yɛr nɩ chɩlfʊ na ba vɩɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma kaara bɩbir jaa ɩka ba kaa lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ ɩn yelmãɛ bɩɩ.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: A Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie sɔɔn de a 'yɛr lɔ̃ɔn a bala na ba ɩ Juu nɩbɛ dɔɔr yɔɔ jie, nɩ a pɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bamɩne yɔɔ jie.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: A Juu nɩbɛ na be a Tɛsalonika na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Pɔɔl ben a Beriya mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, ba chen a be tɩ chãgbala a nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ ba.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Ka a yɛɛr mɩ tɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Pɔɔl a man sɔr a wʊ chen, tɩchɛ ka a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti 'lɔ jɛ̃ be a Beriya.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ bala na bɩɛl a Pɔɔl ba tɩ tãn wʊn a Atensɩ, bala lɛ wʊ lɩɛb tʊ̃ ɩka a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti a ba wa fɔɔ a wʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: A lɛ wʊn chɛnɛ chɛlɛ ba be a Atensɩ, suur wa tɩ kpɛ wʊ naa a lɛ wʊn nyɛ a tẽe na sɛɛn a tɩbɛ.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ 'lɔ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a Greek bala na puoro a Nãaŋmɩn ma mana taa be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir, nɩ a bala wʊn ma pɔɔ a daa pɔ bɩbir jaa nɩ nɩrɛ jaa wʊn tuor.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Ka a bɔ̃bɔ̃ɔnbɔ bamɩne na yi a Epikuriya nɩ a Sɩtoyik gbul pɔ piel chɩrɛ nɩ a Pɔɔl. Ka a bamɩne sʊrɔ, “Bo lɛ a dabʊlɔbʊlɔ nyãna ara 'yɛrɛ?” Ka a bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Ŋmɩn sãansɩ kɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.” Ba 'yɛrɛ naa nɩtɔ̃ bojũu, Pɔɔl mʊɔlɔ naa a Yesu 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nɩ a kũu pɔ irfʊ 'yɛr.

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba pãa bʊɔl wʊ chiin be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie a Areyopagusɩ na ba 'yɛr, “Fʊn tʊ̃ɔ wiil sɩ a fʊ wiil paalaa nyãna fʊn wile a sɩ bɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ?

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: 'Yɛr sãan lɛ a nyã fʊn waan a sɩ wõnõ na sɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Atensɩ dẽme ba jaa lɔ̃ɔn a sãanba na kpɩɛrɛ a be, baa tara tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ pʊɔ a 'yɛr paalaa ban ma 'yɛrɛ na ba chɛlɛ nɩ yelpaalaa na ɩrɛ.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ a Pɔɔl pãa ir a nɩbɛ na lɔ̃ɔ a Areyopagusɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, “Atensɩ dɔɔr, n nyɛn bɔ̃ɔn yɩn nʊ̃ ŋmɩmɛ puorfʊ.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Bojũu ɩn yʊɔrɔ kaara a yɩ tɩbɛ jiir, n tɩ nyɛn a bɔɔr lofʊ jie ãsʊɔ yɩn sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ, ŊMƖN 'LƆ SƖN BA BƆ̃Ɔ Ɛ. A Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ gba yɩn mãɛ na puoro tɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, 'lɔ lɛ n waan ɩka n wa mʊɔl kʊ yɩ.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: “A Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ na maal a wɛr nɩ a bomo a jaa na be a pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a saaju nɩ a tẽe jaa Soro, na waa gãna a yie ala a nɩbɛ na mɛn a ba nuur kʊ wʊ ɛ.

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: Waa dɩɛl nɩrɛ nũu tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala ɩ wʊ bʊɔrɔ na bon kɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bojũu 'lɔ mãɛ lɛ kʊ nɩrɛ nyɛvʊr, ka wʊ vʊrɔ nɩ bon jaa.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Dabʊ̃'yen pɔ lɛ wʊ tu na wʊ maal a nɩbʊrsɩ, ka ba kpɩɛrɛ a wɛr wʊ jaa. A wʊ man a sɔ̃ɔsɩ kʊ ba, nɩ a jiir ala ban na kpɩɛrɛ.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn maal naa nɩtɔ̃ ka nɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔ yɔbɔ wʊ, amɩne sɛ̃ ban nyɛ wʊ naa, bojũu waa jãan a sɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: ‘Bojũu, a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ chɛnɛ na sɩ be ka a tẽe pɔ,’ na sɩ yʊɔrɔ nɩtãa lɛ a yɩn mãɛ nɩbɛ bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, ‘A wʊ bibiir lɛ sɩ ɩ.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: “Sɩn ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir jũu, yɩ taa ɩ a sɩ tɩɛr ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nyã kaara naa fɩka bon nɩrɛ na de wʊ yɛ̃ bɩɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ mɛn, sɛlma, bɩɩ libipɩla, bɩɩ kʊsɩr kaar ɛ.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: A kõr jaa 'lɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ ba bɔ̃ɔfʊ, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, wʊ kpãkpãana na nɩrɛ jaa jie jaa ɩka, ba lɩɛb ba tɩɛr.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Bojũu wʊ ir bɩbir bin wʊn na de a daba 'lɔ wʊn ir, na wʊ tɔɔr a wɛr 'yɛr nɩ yeltorsɩ pɔ. Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a chaar kʊ nɩrɛ jaa wʊn sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Ban wa wõ a kũu pɔ irfʊ 'yɛr, bamɩne lan wuol, tɩchɛ ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr, “Sɩn bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ lɛ wõ a 'yɛr nyã nɩdaar.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: A lɛ, Pɔɔl bar ba naa a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bãlãa turo a Pɔɔl na ba sɔɔ de a 'yɛr, a ba ãsʊɔ lɛ Diyonɩsɩs na ɩ a Areyopagusɩ lɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩrɛ, ka a pɔɔ ãsʊɔ mɩ na pʊɔ yuor di Damarisɩ nɩ bamɩne mɩ na pʊɔ.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: A anyã puor, Pɔɔl yin a Atensɩ na wʊ chen be a Korɩntɩ.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ tuor a Juu nɩrɛ na di Akuila na wʊ ɩ Pontus bal nɩrɛ, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ Pɩrɩsɩla na yi a Itali wa a ba kor ɛ, bojũu a Nãa Kɩlaudiyus 'yɛr naa ɩka a Juu nɩrɛ jaa wʊ yi a Worom. Ka a Pɔɔl chen ɩka wʊ piir ba kaa.

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: Wʊ tɩ jɛ̃n a be a ba 'mataa tʊ̃nɔ̃ bojũu ba jaa bɔ̃ɔn a gãma tʊ̃mɔ̃.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar jaa Pɔɔl ma chen a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir ma tɩ 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛr bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ lɩɛb a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ tɩɛr.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti na wʊ yi a Masedonɩya wa a Korɩntɩ, Pɔɔl den a wʊ sɔ̃ɔ wʊ jaa na wʊ mʊɔlɔ mana kʊrɔ a Juu nɩbɛ ɩka a Yesu lɛ a Nɩ-iraa.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka Juu nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ a Pɔɔl kpõkpoor 'yɛr, a wʊ pãpã a fɔbaa 'yɔ̃ɔ ba tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A yɩ jãɩ a lɩɛb dɔɔl a yɩ ju, a ba lɛ ɩ maa 'yɛr ɛ, an yi dɩna chiine, n chiine naa a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ jie.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl yin be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir na wʊ gʊɔr a daba ãsʊɔ na di Titiyusɩ Jusɩtusɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ na puoro ka a wʊ yir gbʊr a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Ka a Kɩrɩsɩpusɩ na ɩ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir nɩdiere nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro 'yɛr nɩ a Korɩntɩ dẽme yɔɔ na wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, a ba sɔɔ a wʊ so ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: A tãsɔɔ ãsʊɔ, a sɩ Soro 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl jãna pɔ ɩka, “Taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ, mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr ta pɔɔ nɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: Bojũu, ɩn pʊɔ fʊ naa, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ nyɔɔ fʊ bɩɩ maal fʊ dɛɛr ɛ, bojũu n chɛnɛ na tara nɩyɔɔ a tẽe nyã pɔ.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a Pɔɔl jɛ̃ a be a Korɩntɩ yuon gbul nɩ bʊ̃ŋmaa na wʊ wile ba a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Galio ɩ a Akaya nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ka a Juu nɩbɛ guor taa na ba nyɔɔ a Pɔɔl chiin be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Be lɛ ba tɩ 'yɛr, “A daba nyãna lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ lɩɛb a nɩbɛ tɩɛr ka ba puoro Nãaŋmɩn na ba tu a lɛ a sɩ nɛbinsɩ na 'yɛr ɛ.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl haa nɛɛ ɩka wʊ 'yɛr 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka a Galio 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ, “A yɩn, ala ta ɩ yeldɛɛr bɩɩ yelbier kɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ, ɩn taa 'maan ãgãn chɛlɛ.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ an ben wa lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ a yɩ 'yɛr bie nɩ yoe 'matãan a yɩ nɛbinãa yɩn mãɛ yɩ maal, maa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ a 'yɛr nyãna tɔɔr ɛ.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ wʊ vɛ̃ a ba dii ba bar be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: A ba lɩɛb nyɔɔ a Sosetenesɩ na ɩ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ba ŋme be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ yir dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, tɩchɛ a Galio baka ba be be ɛ.

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl jɛ̃n be a Korɩntɩ tẽe kor bãlãa, tɩ pãa bar a yɛɛr na wʊ kpɛ a gboro chiine a Siriya ka a Pɩrɩsɩla nɩ a Akuila pɔɔ wʊ. Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka wʊ chen, wʊ vɛ̃n ka ba põ wʊ a ju be a Senkereya, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ mãɛ.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Ba tãn be a Efesus tẽe pɔ, ka a Pɔɔl bar a Pɩrɩsɩla nɩ a Akuila tɩ na wʊ mãɛ chen be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie ka a 'lɔ naa Juu nɩbɛ tɩ mana taa.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: A ba sʊɔr wʊ ka wʊ jãnɛ a be bãlãa tɩ waa sɔɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Wʊn tɩ ta a Sisariya wʊ don be a Jerusalɛm na wʊ tɩ puor a bala na puoro, tɩ pãa suu chen a Antiok.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na tɩ jɛ̃ a be a Antiok kor bãlãa, wʊ pãa yin yʊɔrɔ a Galatiya paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa nɩ a Fɩrgɩya na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a poturbo ãkpãɛn.

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ yuor na di Apolosɩ yin be a Aleksandiriya na wʊ wa a Efesus tẽe pɔ. Wʊ ɩ naa nɩrɛ na jãn na wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma tɩ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr sʊ̃.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Apolosɩ nyã dɔ̃ɔ jãn a sɩ Soro sɔr defʊ, na wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ nɩbijɩɛ na wʊ wile nɩ Yesu yele ka a tor, tɩchɛ ka a ɩ Jɔɔn Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ tɛɛ lɛ wʊ bɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Wʊ piel na wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ nyãa be a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir. A Pɩrɩsɩla nɩ a Akuila na wa wõ a wʊ 'yɛr, ba bʊɔl wʊn be a ba yir na ba pãa wiil wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn sɔr jaa dẽ dẽ.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Apolosɩ wa bʊɔrɔ naa Akaya chenfʊ, ka a yɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ ãkpɛ̃ɔ tɩ sɛb gãn kʊ a bala na puoro a be a ba de wʊ. Wʊn tɩ ta a be a ɩn sʊ̃ʊfʊ kpɛ̃ɛ kʊ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ na vɛ̃ ka ba sɔɔ de a Yesu.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Wʊ 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa lɔb a Juu nɩbɛ chãa pɔ, na wʊ chɩrɛ ba yuoro nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma wile ba ɩka a Yesu lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Apolosɩ na chɛnɛ be a Korɩntɩ, Pɔɔl den a mɔgɔ̃ɔ sɔr na wʊ tɩ ta a Efesus tẽe pɔ. Be lɛ wʊ nyɛ a poturbo bamɩne,

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: na wʊ sʊʊr ba, “A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn sɔɔ de a Yesu, yɩ nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ bɩɩ?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “Kai, saa gba wõ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ben be ɛ.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl sʊʊr ba, “Tɩ kɔ̃buor lɛ yɩ so?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “Jɔɔn kʊ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ so.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A bala na lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr lɛ a Jɔɔn so ba Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka ba sɔɔ de a 'lɔ na waara a wʊ puor, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Yesu.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ na wa wõ a anyãna ba sɔɔ naa ka wʊ so ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl dɔɔl ba a wʊ nuur ka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa kpɛ ba a ba 'yɛrɛ kɔkɔyor, na ba ma piel 'yɛrɛ lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na wiil ba.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Ban ɩn dɔɔr pie nɩ ayi.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Ŋmɛrsɩ ata lɛ wʊ be a be na wʊ ma kpɩɛrɛ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie na wʊ ma kpɩrkpɩrɛ ba wile ba a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a bamɩne kpɛ̃mɛ naa a ba nyãa na ba tɔr baa sɔɔ ɛ, tɩ na ba kpɛrɛ a Sɔr. Ka a Pɔɔl bar ba, tɩ na wʊ ma tara a bala na sɔɔ de, ma kpɛ̃n be a Tiranusɩ jãnfʊ diyal 'lɔ pɔ, a ba ma tɩ mana taa.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ wʊ tara tɩ tãn yuom ayi, ka a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, bala jaa na be a Asɩya paal pɔ wõn a sɩ Soro 'yɛr.

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn den a Pɔɔl tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl fɔbile wʊn ma fɩɛlɛ nɩ nyĩe nɩ a wʊ pẽpele ala wa sɩɩr baalaa, a baalsɩ ba jaa ma sa naa, ka a sɩdɛbɛ ma yire a nɩbɛ pɔ.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ yi yʊɔrɔ na dii a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi na ba fere de a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a bala na tara a sɩdɛbɛ, “A Yesu 'lɔ a Pɔɔl na mʊɔlɔ, a wʊ yuor pɔ lɛ sɩ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ wa yi.”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na di Sekeva dabil ayopõi lɛ dɔ̃ɔ maala a lɛ nɛ.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Daar kɔ̃ɔ a sɩdɛɛr sʊʊr ba naa, “N bɔ̃ɔn Yesu naa bɔ̃ɔ Pɔɔl, tɩchɛ a yɩn mɩ ba boo yɩ ɩ?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Ka a daba 'lɔ na tara a sɩ dɛɛr pãa ɔɔ nyɔɔr ba jaa, na wʊ ŋme ba jaa pɩrpɩr tɔɔr a ba bonsɩ chɩɛr ka ba jo yi a die pɔ nɩ sal'mɔi.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: A Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, na kpɩɛrɛ a be Efesus tẽe pɔ, ba jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa a yele nyãna, ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba a baa lɛ tara a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor dãɛnɛ nɩ ɛ.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na sɔɔ de, yi chãa pɔ, na ba 'yɛrɛ a ba maal dɛbɛ.

english_verse: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
translation_verse: A jɩbʊʊrbɔ bala mɩ na sɔɔ, waan naa a ba jɩbʊʊrgãma wa chʊm a nɩbɛ ba jaa niem, ban wa sɔr a gãma ala ban chʊm libie ka a ɩ libipɩla turo ayi dɔɔl lɩjaayi nɩ pie (

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: A sɔr nyãna lɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr yaar de jie wʊ jaa nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: A anyãna a jaa na wa ɩ, Pɔɔl ŋmen nyãa ɩka wʊn chen a Jerusalɛm na wʊn tu a Masedonɩya nɩ Akaya, “Maa wa ta a be baar, ɩn chen a Worom mɩ.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Wʊ tʊ̃ naa a nɩbɛ ayi a bala na sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ Timoti nɩ Erastus ka ba chen a Masedonɩya, tɩchɛ ka a 'lɔ jãnɛ be a Asɩya paal pɔ kor bãlãa.

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a damfʊ ir a Sɔr tʊfʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Sãa kuro kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Demetirus, wʊ ma den a kpãna na wʊ kur a Atemisɩ puorfʊ yirbil ka a bala na ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ nyɛrɛ libie.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: A Demetirus bʊɔl a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ a bala jaa mɩ na bɔ̃ɔ a maalfʊ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɛɛr, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa tɔ̃nɔ̃ sɩn nyɛrɛ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã pɔ.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Yɩn mãɛ nyɛ naa na yɩ wõ a lɛ a Pɔɔl nyãna na 'yɛrɛ na wʊ lɩɛrɛ a nɩyɔɔ tɩɛrsɩ a ka Efesus tẽe pɔ, nɩ a Asɩya wɛr wʊ jaa. Wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka a ŋmɩmɛ ala ban maal nuur ba ɩ ŋmɩmɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: A ba ɩ a sɩ yara na lo tɛɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba kʊ̃ lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Atemisɩ puorfʊ yir kpɛ̃ɛ go ɛ. Ka a Asɩya nɩ a wɛr wʊ jaa kʊ̃ lɛ puoro a ŋmɩn pɔɔ nyã go ɛ.”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Ban wõ lɛ a Demetirus na 'yɛr, ka a suur nyɔɔ ba a ba tãna 'yɛrɛ, “A sɩn Efesus ŋmɩn Atemisɩ bɛrma naa.”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Ajienaa lɛ a tẽe wʊ jaa pɔɔn gɔ̃mɔ̃, ka a nɩbɛ nyɔɔ a Gayus nɩ Aristakus, Pɔɔl tʊ̃tʊ̃taaba lɛ a ba 'mataa yi a Masedonɩya na ba ŋmʊr ba a lɛ chiin a jie 'lɔ a tẽe nɩbɛ na ma lɔ̃ɔ dãɛnɛ.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl mɩ ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ daa yi a be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a poturbo ba sɔɔ ɛ.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne gba na yi a paal 'lɔ pɔ na ɩ a Pɔɔl barmɩne, tʊ̃n nɩbɛ ka ba sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ taa ɩrɛ wa yire be a dãɛn jie ɛ.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Burbur kpɛ̃n a nɩyɔɔ bala na duo a be, ka bamɩne 'yɛrɛ turo nɩ ka sɛn, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ turo nɩ jã yuo, ka bamɩne mɩ gba ba bɔ̃ɔ bo 'yɔ̃ɔ ban be be ɛ.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ daa Aleksanda 'yɔ̃ɔ be a niem, ka a nɩyɔɔ bala tãna mana wʊ lɛ wʊn na 'yɛr. Wʊ wɩlwɩl ban nuur ɩka, ba bar gɔ̃n tɩ wʊ 'yɛr a ba kaa.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ban wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Juu nɩrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ, ba jaa ŋmen chɛl bʊ̃'yen lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ na ba 'yɛrɛ, “A sɩn Efesus ŋmɩn Atemisɩ bɛrma naa.”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: A tẽe gãn sɛɛrɛ vɛ̃n ka a nɩyɔɔ bala ŋmaa vʊʊr tɩ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Efesus dẽme woɩ, a wɛr wʊ jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Efesus tẽe lɛ kaara a Atemisɩ kpɛ̃ɛ nyã puorfʊ yir a wʊ kʊsɩr 'lɔ na yi a saaju lo.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: A nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ jũu, nɩrɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chɩɩr ɛ, yɩ ɩ jaa gbul na yɩ ta pɔɔrɔ yelsɩ ɛ.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ banyãna yɩn waan a kana ba ju bon jaa a puorfʊ yir ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ ŋmɩn pɔɔ nyã ɛ.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ a Demetirus nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ wa tara nɩrɛ nɩ 'yɛr, a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ bɩbie ben be nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ban tʊ̃ɔ chen a be tɩ bʊɔl taa.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ yele kɔ̃ɔ mɩ ben be a yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ 'yɛr, ban tʊ̃ɔ maal a be a nɩyɔɔ ba jaa niem.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: A lɛ an sɩ ben 'yɛr pɔ, a damfʊ nyã na dam a dɩna jũu. A 'yɛr nyãna jũu, saa be jaa na tʊ̃ɔ wiil a par ɛ, bojũu a ba tara par ɛ.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: A anyãna wʊn 'yɛr baar tɩ na wʊ wɛl a nɩyɔɔ bala bar.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Lɛ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ na wa baar, Pɔɔl tʊ̃n bʊɔl a poturbo ka ba wa, a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpãɛn tɩ bar ba tɩ chen a Masedonɩya.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ tu a tẽn ala pɔ na wʊ 'yɛr 'yɛr yɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔn a bala na puoro ãkpãɛn na wʊ tɩ tãn a Greek wɛr pɔ,

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: wʊ jɛ̃n a be ŋmɛrsɩ ata. Bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔ guoro taa a wʊ yele, ala lɛ so waa lɛ kpɛ a gboro chen a Siriya ɛ, tɩ na wʊ lɩɛb tu a Masedonɩya sɔr nɩ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Ka a bala na pʊɔ wʊ a ba 'mataa chen lɛ, Sopater a wʊ sãa lɛ Pirihusɩ na yi Beriya, Aristakus nɩ Sekundusɩ mɩ na yi Tɛsalonika, Gayus na yi Debi nɩ Timoti nɩ Tɩchɩkusɩ nɩ a Torofimusɩ na yi a Asɩya paal pɔ.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ de nie tɩ chɛlɛ sɩ be a Toroyas.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ di na wa baar, sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro yi be a Filipai tẽe pɔ na sɩ maal ber anũu tɩ na sɩ pɔɔ ba a be Toroyas jãnɛ a be ber ayopõi.

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: A Pɛ̃nfʊ divɩɛ bio daar jãanʊɔr, sɩ jaa lɔ̃ɔn taa na sɩn 'mataa di. Pɔɔl 'yɛrɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ bojũu wɛr wa chaar, wʊn chen naa. Wʊ 'yɛr kor jaa ka wɛr tɩ põ.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: A be a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ sɩn lɔ̃ɔ ba chʊm fãtɩnsɩ yɔɔ bin.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Pɔlbile kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Yutikus jãnɛ naa a tokoro nɛɛ ka a gʊ̃ɔ kpɛ wʊ. Lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛr kor a wʊ gur jaa chãɩɩ na wʊ yi a be a saaju faa lo a be tẽe, bojũu ba mɛn a bɔɔpɩɛ dɔɔl taa ata ka ba suu 'mɔɔ ka ɩ wʊ kpi naa.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl suun tɩ lo bib a kũu tɩ kõr wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ, “Yɩ ta kõnõ ɛ, wʊ chɛnɛ na vʊrɔ.”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Wʊ pãa don be a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ, ka wʊ tɩ ŋma a paanʊ̃ɔ ka ba 'mataa di tɩ wʊ lɛ 'yɛr lɛ̃ɛɛ ka wɛr tɩ chaar ka wʊ yi a be chen.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka pɔlbile ir kpɛ̃mɛ ka ba ãgãn 'maa a ba tara wʊ kul a yir.

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Sɩn den nie kpɛ a gboro na sɩ chiine a Asosɩ, be lɛ sɩn tɩ 'mɔɔ a Pɔɔl, lɛ lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ man sɩ tɩ chiine nɩ gbɛɛ a be.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Lɛ wʊn tɩ tuor sɩ a be Asosɩ, sɩ 'mɔɔ wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a gboro pɔ na sɩ chiine be a Mitilene.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Wɛr na wa chaar, sɩ yin a be tɩ ta Kiyosɩ ka a wɛr lɛ bɔ̃ɔ chaar sɩ go kaa ɩ sɩ vɩɛn Samosɩ bar. A ber ata daar lɛ sɩ pãa ta a Miletusɩ.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Pɔɔl bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ po a Efesus tẽe bar na wʊ ta gã be a Asɩya paal pɔ ɛ, bojũu wʊ bʊɔrɔ na fɔɔ ɩka 'lɔ tʊ̃ɔ, wʊ ta a Jerusalɛm a Pentikosɩ bɩbir daar.

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na chɛnɛ be a Miletusɩ lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ bʊɔl a bala na puoro a be a Efesus, a ba nɩdierbe ka ba wa.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Ban wa ta, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɩn be dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ a yɩ sɛ̃, yi a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa ɩn wa a Asɩya paal pɔ.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: Mãɛ siirfʊ nɩ nɩbikʊ̃ɔ lɛ n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a sɩ Soro tʊ̃mɔ̃, bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ ba maal ma vɩla ɛ.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Yɩn mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka n ba sɔɔl yɩ yele jaa na sʊ̃ʊ yɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ n wile yɩn chãa pɔ, nɩ a yɩ yie pɔ.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: “Pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr man ka n chiine a Jerusalɛm, n ba bɔ̃ɔ lɛn na tɩ pɔɔ ma a be ɛ.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yelbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ n bɔ̃ɔ ɩka, tẽe jaa pɔ, a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ma vɛ̃n ka n bɔ̃ɔn ɩka die pɔ pɔɔfʊ, nɩ wõm lɛ ɩn tɩ tuor.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a anyãna maa bã ma ɛ, ala ɩ ɩn tʊ̃ɔ jo ta a baaraa naa tʊ̃ a lɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu na kʊ ma a tʊ̃mɔ̃, ɩka n 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Yɩ nyɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã a yɩn bala jie ɩn yɔ naa mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr, yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ a dɩna ɩka ãsʊɔ jaa wa bɔr, n nũu ba pʊɔ be ɛ.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Bojũu n ba dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔl yɩ yele jaa ɛ, tɩ man a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ kʊ yɩ.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ nɩ a nɩbɛ bala 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na maal yɩ a yɩ ɩ a nɩdierbe. Yɩ kaara a Nãaŋmɩn pupuorbiir bala wʊn dãn a wʊ mãɛ jãɩ.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka maa wa chen baar, nɩbɛ na lɩɛb gbõgboi kpɛ a yɩ pɔ, ba kʊ̃ bar yɩ ɛ.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Bamɩne na yin a yɩn mãɛ pɔ gba, na ba jɛr a yelmãɛ bɛl nɩbɛ ka ba turo ba.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu yɩ guro yɩ mãɛ na yɩ tɩɛr kaa, yuom ata nɩ nyã ɩn ba vɛ̃ a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wiilfʊ ɛ, nɩbikʊ̃ɔ lɛ n wile nɩ yɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: “Pʊ̃pãanyã, n de yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nũu pɔ, nɩ a wʊ wãɛfʊ 'yɛr bie pɔ, a yɩ na tʊ̃ɔ nʊ̃ɔ a wʊ kʊ yɩ a bʊ̃sʊ̃n ala wʊn tara kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: N ba tara nyuur nɩ ãsʊɔ jaa libir, bɩɩ sɛlma, bɩɩ a fɔɔr ɛ.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Yɩn mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a n bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ, a n nũu pɔ lɛ a yi, naa sʊ̃ʊ a bala ɩn lɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Bon jaa ɩn maal, n wiil yɩ naa ɩka a tʊ̃kpãɛn a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ, sɩn tu na sɩ sʊ̃ʊ a nɩbaalsɩ, yɩ tɩɛrɛ a lɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, ‘An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a 'lɔ na de bon kʊ nɩrɛ, gar a 'lɔ na de.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Wʊn 'yɛr a anyãna baar, wʊ gbur dumo ka ba jaa 'mataa sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Ba jaa ŋman nɩbikʊ̃ɔ tɩ na ba kõr kõr wʊ.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: A lɛ na maal sɔ̃ɔ a ba pʊɔ nɩ nyã, a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ wʊ go ɛ, tɩ na ba bɩɛl wʊ suun a gboro jie.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Sɩn wa bar taa baar, sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro na sɩ tor a Kos a dẽdẽe a lɛ nɛ, a wɛr na wa chaar ka a ɩ sɩ tãn a Worodesɩ yi a be chen a Patara.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Sɩ nyɛn a gboro ãsʊɔ na chiine a Fonisiya ka sɩ kpɛ chiine.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Sɩn wa nyɛrɛ a Sipurusɩ tẽe sɩ lan wʊn bar a guba jie tɩ ta a Siriya tẽe pɔ. Tire lɛ sɩ ta ara an ɩ be lɛ a gboro na wɛl a tuor wʊn tuo.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a sɩ bɩbie na wa ta, sɩ yin de a sɩ sɔr. Ka a poturbo bala ba jaa nɩ a ba pɔbɔ nɩ a ba bibiir ba jaa bɩɛl sɩ yin a tẽe pɔ, tɩ tãn a man gogoro nɛɛ, ka sɩ gbur dumo sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: Sɩn wa bar taa baar, sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro pɔ, tɩ ba mɩ lɩɛb kulo a yir.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: A Tire sɩn be yi, a man pɔ lɛ sɩ chen bãlãa tɩ ta a Tolemaisɩ be lɛ sɩ puor a yɛɛr bamɩne na be be na sɩ gã a be bɩbir bʊ̃'yen.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Sɩn ir a be chiine sɩ tãn be a Sisariya na sɩ chen a Filip na mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ na wʊ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ ayopõi bala ãsʊɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ ir be a Jerusalɛm yir.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Sɩ jɛ̃n a be kor bãlãa ka a Agabus yi be a Judiya na,

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: wʊn wa a sɩ sɛ̃. Wʊ den a Pɔɔl sɩɛ gãn na wʊ lũn a wʊ mãɛ nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr. “A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr naa, ‘Nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na be a Jerusalɛm na lũ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na so a sɩɛ gãn nyãna, na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nuur pɔ.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: A lɛ sɩn wõ a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ ka a sɩn nɩ a nɩbɛ bala na be a be 'mataa sʊɔr a Pɔɔl ɩka wʊ taa chiine a Jerusalɛm ɛ.
