english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ kõnõ na yɩ chɩrɛ ma a ãgãn a lɛ? N sɔɔ naa, a ta ɩ a lũfu tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ n kpi be a Jerusalɛm a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor jũu.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: A lɛ sɩn sʊɔr wʊ faa sɩ mɩ bar naa tɩ 'yɛr “A sɩ Soro bɔfʊ a ɩ.”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: A anyã puor lɛ sɩ chɔɔr na sɩ do be a Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Ka a poturbo bala na be a Sisariya bɩɛl sɩ na ba waan sɩ a daba 'lɔ na di a Minason yir be lɛ sɩn wa kpɩɛrɛ, Sipurusɩ tẽe pɔ lɛ wʊ yi tɩ na wʊ ɩ a poturo kor.

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: A lɛ sɩn wa ta a Jerusalɛm, a yɛɛr de sɩn nuur ayi.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Ka a wɛr na wa chaar, sɩn nɩ a Pɔɔl jaa 'mantaa chen ɩka sɩ tɩ nyɛ a Jemesɩ ka a ɩ be lɛ a nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: A lɛ ban wõ anyãna ba jaa pɛɛn Nãaŋmɩn tɩ na ba pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, “Yɛbɛ faa nyɛ, a Juu nɩbɛ turo turo na sɔɔ de, tɩchɛ ba chɛnɛ na tara nɩbijɩɛ nɩ a nɛbinãa.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Ba man kʊ ba ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ wile a Juu nɩbɛ bala na kpɩɛrɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ, ka ba taa turo a Mosesɩ ɛ, na fʊ lɛ wile ɩka ba taa ŋmara a ba bibiir yɔɩ ɛ, na ba taa ɩrɛ a lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na ma ɩrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Ban wõn ɩka fʊ wan a ka, ŋmɩnɛ sɩn ɩ?

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Fʊ̃ʊ bʊɔrɔ fʊ tu a lɛ sɩn na 'yɛr. Dɔɔr anaar ben ka a sɩ sɛ̃ na ba põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba mãɛ.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: De a nɩbɛ banyãna na fʊ pʊɔ ba, a yɩ tɩ pɔl a fʊ yab a ba jur põfʊ sãma. Ka nɩrɛ jaa na pãa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a lɛ ban man 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ba ɩ yelmãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ mɩ turo naa a nɛbinãa.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a nɩyor bala na sɔɔ de, sɩ sɛb gãn kʊ ba a lɛ sɩn 'yɛr ɩka, ba taa dire tɩbɛ bomo ɛ, bɩɩ ɔɔrɔ jãɩ ɛ, bɩɩ 'wɔb dʊɔ na var kpi ɛ, na ba taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Wɛr na wa chaar, Pɔɔl den a dɔɔr anaar bala ka ba 'mataa tɩ pɔl a ba mãɛ. Tɩ na wʊ pãa chen be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir na tɩ 'yɛr a bɩbie ala a ba pɔlfʊ na baar nɩ a bɔɔrlo bomo ala a ba ãsʊɔ jaa na ir.

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: A lɛ a ber ayopõi na wa gbʊrɔ a baarfʊ, a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Asɩya paal pɔ nyɛn a Pɔɔl a be Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, na ba dam a nɩyɔɔ bala a ba nyɔɔ wʊ.

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Ba gboolo naa, “Isɩral nɩbɛ woɩ, yɩ wa sʊ̃ʊ sɩ yooo. A daba lɛ anyãna na wile a nɩbɛ jie jaa ka ba kpɛrɛ a sɩ nɩbɛ nɩ a nɛbinãa lɔ̃ɔn a jie nyãna. Lɛ tɛɛ gba ba lɛ ɛ, wʊ tara na a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, kpɛ̃n ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a jivɩla nyãna.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Ba dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ a Torofimusɩ na yi a Efesus nɩ a Pɔɔl a ba tɩɛr man tãa a Pɔɔl tara wʊn kpɛ̃n be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: A tẽdaa wʊ jaa kab, ka a nɩbɛ yire a tẽe wʊ jaa joro waara na ba wa nyɔɔ a Pɔɔl. Ba nyɔɔ wʊn a lɛ tɔɔ yin a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir jie tɩ pɔɔ a pãma ajienaa.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Ka ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kʊ a Pɔɔl ka a yele tɩ ta a Worom sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɩka a Jerusalɛm wʊ jaa dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: A wʊ vaa ir a lɛ na wʊ tara a nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne nɩ a sojasɩ na wʊ suu chiin be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ. Lɛ ban wa nyɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a wʊ sojasɩ a ba bar a Pɔɔl ŋmefʊ.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Lɛ a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa ta wʊ tɩ nyɔɔn a Pɔɔl na wʊ vɛ̃ ka ba gbãr wʊ nɩ bɔ̃jɔrsɩ ayi. Tɩ pãa sʊʊr ɩka nɩ ana lɛ, nɩ bo lɛ wʊ maal.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Bamɩne na be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ 'yɛrɛ na turo nɩ jiyuo ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ turo nɩ jiyuo a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ba nyɛ a yelmãɛ ɛ, a gɔ̃mɔ̃ jũu, wʊ vɛ̃n ka ba tara a Pɔɔl gʊɔr be a sojasɩ kpɛfʊ jie.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Lɛ a Pɔɔl na wa ta a dofʊ jie a nɩbɛ pir pirfʊ jaa waa naa ka a sojasɩ 'mɔɔ a Pɔɔl.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na turo tãna 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ kʊ wʊ.”

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Lɛ a sojasɩ na wa ɩka ba kpɛ̃n a Pɔɔl a be a ba kpɛfʊ jie wʊ sʊɔr naa a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɩka, “N 'yɛr 'yɛr kɔ̃ɔ kʊ fʊ bɩɩ?” Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sʊʊr wʊ, “Fʊn tʊ̃ɔn 'yɛr a Greek kɔkɔr bɩɩ?

english_verse: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
translation_verse: A ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ŋmaa a jɛɛr na fʊ tɔɔ dɔɔr turo anaar (

english_verse: terrorists into the desert?”
translation_verse: ) kpɛ̃n be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ɛ?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “N ɩ naa Juu nɩrɛ naa yi a Tasusɩ na be a Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ, naa yi a tẽe nyãna pɔ nɩrɛ jaa na bɔ̃ɔn, yãayãabɩlɛ sɔɔ ka n 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: A sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kʊn a Pɔɔl sɔr, ka a wʊ ir ara a dofʊ jie, na wʊ wɩlwɩl a nɩyɔɔ bala a nuur ka ba bar gɔ̃mɔ̃. A lɛ ban wa ŋmaa a vʊʊr wʊ den a ba Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr na 'yɛrɛ nɩ.

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “N yɛɛr nɩ n sãamɩne yɩ chɛlɛ a n 'yɛr ɩn 'yɛrɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: A lɛ ban wa wõ wʊn 'yɛrɛ nɩ a ba mãɛ Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr ka ba jaa ŋma vʊrɔ, tɩ a Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛrɛ,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Maa ɩn Juu nɩrɛ ka ba dɔɔ ma a Tasusɩ na be a Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ, tɩchɛ a kana a Jerusalɛm lɛ ba guol ma. A wiwile Gamaliel pĩlem lɛ n jãnɛ ka wʊ wiil ma a nɛɛr ala a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ turo ka n mɩ turo a Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a n nyãa wʊ jaa, nɩtãa a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa na be turo a dɩna.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: N gen a nɩbɛ bala na turo a Sɔr nyãna naa kʊ, n nyɔɔr dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: A bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na tʊ̃ɔn di ma dãasɩɛ, ba sɛ̃ lɛ n de gãma na chiin a ba yɛɛr sɛ̃ be a Damaskus, n chen ɩka n tɩ nyɔɔr a nɩbɛ banyãna waan a Jerusalɛm ka ba kpãa ba.

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “Mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sɔ̃ɔ lɛ n chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a Damaskus, ajienaa ka ɩ chãa yin be a saaju na wʊ wa chaar jilʊ̃ ma.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Ka a n tɔ̃ɔ lo a tẽe a lɛ nɛ tɩ naa wõ kɔkɔr na sʊrɔ ma, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bo lɛ fʊ gere ma a lɛ?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Ka n sʊʊr, ‘Soro, an lɛ fʊ ɩ?’ Ka a kɔkɔr 'yɛr, ‘Maa lɛ a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ a 'lɔ fʊn gere.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: A maa nɩ a bala na lɔ̃ɔ chiine ba nyɛn a chãa, tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ a nɩnyɩnɛ kɔkɔr lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ nɩ ma par ɛ.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Ka n sʊʊr, ‘Soro, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn ɩ?’ Ka a Soro 'yɛr, ‘Ir na fʊ kpɛ be a Damaskus tẽe pɔ, be lɛ ban tɩ man fʊ a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ ɩ.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Ka n lɔ̃ɔturbo bala nyɔɔ ma a nũu tɔɔ kpɛ̃n be a Damaskus tẽe pɔ, bojũu a chãa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na chaar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma a lɛ nɛ n ba lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Daba kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Ananias wan ɩka wʊ wa nyɛ ma, wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn puoro na wʊ turo a nɛbinãa ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa na kpɩɛrɛ a be 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Wʊ wa ara na gbɛb ma tɩ 'yɛr, ‘N yɛbɛ Sɔɔl, lɛ nyɛrɛ,’ ka a ɩ ajienaa ka a ɩ n nyɛ wʊ naa.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: Be lɛ wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ma, ‘A sɩ sãakpãmɩne Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ir fʊ ɩka fʊ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ, na fʊ lɛ nyɛ a chɛchɛ sʊɔ 'lɔ, na fʊ wõ a 'yɛr bie yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ.

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã, bo lɛ fʊ lɛ chɛlɛ go? Ir a ba so fʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka a na pɛɛ a fʊ yeldɛbɛ bar a fʊ bʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro yuor.’

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “A lɛ ɩn lɩɛb wa a Jerusalɛm naa tɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn be a puorfʊ yir, ka a wa kaara ma jãna kaar.

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: Ka n nyɛ a sɩ Soro na 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊrɔ ma. Wʊ 'yɛr naa, ‘Pɔɔ fɔɔ na fʊ yi ka a Jerusalɛm ajienaa, bojũu nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ sɔɔ a lɛ fʊn na 'yɛr a n 'yɛr ɛ.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: Ka n 'yɛr sɔɔ wʊ, ‘N Soro a nɩbɛ banyãna bɔ̃ɔn naa a lɛ ɩn yʊɔrɔ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir naa nyɔɔrɔ ba pɔɔrɔ tɩ ŋmiere a bala na sɔɔ de fʊ.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ban kʊrɔ a fʊ dãasɩ dire Sɩteven maa nɩ bala jaa ɩn nɛbʊ̃'yen ka n kaara a ba fuusɩ tɩ ba kʊ wʊ.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ka a sɩ Soro pãa 'yɛr kʊ ma, ‘Chen, ɩn tʊ̃ fʊn tɛɛr jaa be a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ.’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: A nɩyɔɔ bala chɛlɛ naa lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ wa tãn a jie wʊn 'yɛr a nɩyor pɔ chenfʊ 'yɛr, a ba pãa tãna 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ ŋmaa wʊ ju, a ba sɛɛ ɩka wʊ lɛ chɛnɛ vʊrɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ ba pɔɔrɔ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ tɩ lɔɔrɔ a ba fɔɔr ɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a tãna lɔɔrɔ a saaju.

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Ka a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ vɛ̃ ka ba tara a Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n be a sojasɩ yir. Wʊ 'yɛr ɩka ba fɔb a Pɔɔl a wʊ wiil ba bãa lɛ so a nɩbɛ pɔɔrɔ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ a lɛ.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: A lɛ ban lũ a Pɔɔl ɩka ba fɔb, ka wʊ sʊʊr a sojasɩ nɩdier na ara a be, “A tara na sɔr ka yɩn fɔb a Worom nɩrɛ na yaa sʊʊr kaa a yelnyɩnɛ wʊn ɩ ɛ?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Lɛ a sojasɩ nɩdier na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ wʊ chen a ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ fʊn ɩ? A daba nyã ɩn Worom nɩrɛ.”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Ka a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ chen a Pɔɔl sɛ̃ tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, “'Yɛr kʊ ma, Worom nɩrɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ ɔ?” Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr ɩka, “Ɔ̃ɔ.”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: A sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ pãa 'yɛr, “Maa 'lɔ, libiyɔɔ lɛ n yab tɩ jãnɛ a ka.” Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “Maa 'lɔ, ka lɛ ba dɔɔ ma.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Ka a bala na ara gu a wʊ fɔbfʊ mɩ tɔɔ yaar ajienaa. Dãbãɛ mɩ kpɛ̃n a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a lɛ wʊn wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Pɔɔl ɩn Worom nɩrɛ tɩchɛ ka wʊ vɛ̃ a ba lũ wʊ.

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar, a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ ba bara a Pɔɔl ɛ. Wʊ for wʊn bar tɩ bʊɔl a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ nɩbɛrɛ ka ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa. Tɩ wʊ pãa suu waan a Pɔɔl a wʊ wa ara a ba niem.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kãan a nɩbɛrɛ a lɛ a dẽdẽe tɩ 'yɛr, “N yɛɛr, n tʊ̃n a lɛ na sɛɛ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn wa ta a dɩna tɩ n nyãa ba dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: A anyãna wʊn 'yɛr ka a Ananias na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na ara gbʊr a Pɔɔl ɩka ba 'ma wʊ a nɛɛ pɔ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Nãaŋmɩn na 'ma fʊ naa a fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ a tãpɩɛl dachin nyã. Fʊ jɛ̃n a be ɩka fʊ tɔɔr a n 'yɛr a lɛ a nɛɛ na bin tɩ na fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ lɛ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɛɛ, na fʊ 'yɛr ɩka ba 'ma ma.”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: A bala na ara gbʊr a Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ, “Aa! Bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ fʊ tʊrɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “Yɛɛr n ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ ɛ, bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ‘Taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ nɩbɛ nɩdier ɛ.’ ” (A Yifʊ Gãn

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Saduseemɩne jɛr naa Faraseemɩne pɔ ka wʊ tãn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa be a nɩbɛrɛ pɔ na wʊ 'yɛr, “N yɛɛr, Farasee lɛ n ɩ, ka a Farasee nɩrɛ dɔɔ ma. N ara naa kana a ban tɔɔr a n 'yɛr, bojũu n sɔɔ naa ɩka kũu pɔ irfʊ ben be.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: A lɛ wʊn wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ ka a nɛchɩrɛ lo a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne chara pɔ, ka a lɔ̃ɔfʊ ŋma jiir ayi.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Saduseemɩne 'yɛr ɩka kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, malakasɩ nɩ a nɩsɩɩr mɩ ba be be ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a Faraseemɩne 'lɔ sɔɔn ɩka a jaa ben be.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: A gɔ̃mɔ̃ pɔɔfʊ tɩ gar naa ka a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne na ɩ a Faraseemɩne ir ara tɩ chɩɩr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa ɩka, “Sɩn 'lɔ ba nyɛ a daba nyã chɛfʊ jie ɛ, tɩ ala wa ɩ sɩɛ kɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ malaka lɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ wʊ?”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: A nɛchɩrɛ wa tɩ gar naa, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a wʊ man tãa ban wa tɔɔr a Pɔɔl 'mɛl, ala lɛ so ka wʊ vɛ̃ a sojasɩ suu tɩ faa a Pɔɔl don be a sojasɩ yir.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: A tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a sɩ Soro wa ara na gbʊr a Pɔɔl tɩ 'yɛr, “Nyɔɔ nyãa, a lɛ fʊn man a n yelmãɛ a ka a Jerusalɛm mɩ tɩ man a lɛ nɛ a be a Worom tẽe pɔ.”

english_verse: The next morning more than
translation_verse: Ba gar dɔɔr lɩjaayi na guor a guorfʊ nyãna.

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Ba chen a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃, na ba tɩ 'yɛr, “Sɩ bin nɛɛ ɩka sɩ kʊ̃ di bon jaa ɛ, ka sɩn baa kʊ a Pɔɔl baar ɛ.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ nɩbɛrɛ yɩ tʊ̃ 'yɛr a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a wʊ waan a Pɔɔl a yɩ sʊʊr yele kɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par. Sɩn na var gu be a sɔr pɔ kʊ wʊ ka waa ta a be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl arbile wõ a guorfʊ nyã na wʊ chen be a sojasɩ yir na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl bʊɔl a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ na wʊ 'yɛr, “Tara a pɔlbile nyãna chiin a yɩ jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ wʊ tara na 'yɛr na 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ wʊ tara a pɔlbile chiin a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, “A Pɔɔl na be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ lɛ tʊ̃ bʊɔl ma na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka n tara a pɔlbile nyãna waan a fʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ tara na 'yɛr na 'yɛr kʊ fʊ.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Ka a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyɔɔ a pɔlbile nũu tɔɔ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, “Baa lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: A wʊ 'yɛr, “A Juu nɩbɛ guor taa na ban sʊɔr fʊ ka fʊn waan a Pɔɔl a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ sɛ̃ a bio ka a kaara fɩka ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba sʊʊr wʊ yele kɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bar a pɔlbile sɔr tɩ kpãa wʊ 'yɛr, “Taa ɩrɛ wa vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ jaa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka fʊ wan a n sɛ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: The commander called in two of his officers and told them, “By nine o'clock tonight have
translation_verse: Tɩ pãa bʊɔl a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ ayi na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ chɔɔr sojasɩ kɔɔr ayi (

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Yɩ bɔ a wii ãsʊɔ kʊ a Pɔɔl a wʊ do jãnɛ a yɩ chiin wʊ vɩla tɩ kʊ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Feliksɩ.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: A gãn wʊn sɛb 'lɔ nɩ nyã,

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: A daba nyãna lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ nyɔɔ na ba ta ɩka ba kʊ, tɩchɛ ɩn wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Worom nɩrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ, n chiin naa a n sojasɩ tɩ faan wʊ.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: N ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka n bɔ̃ɔ a yele 'lɔ jũu ban ba bara wʊ ɛ, naa de wʊ chiin be a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Naa tɩ nyɛ ka ɩ a yele ɩ naa a ba nɛbinãa yele lɛ. Tɩchɛ saa lɛ nyɛ yele jaa wʊn maal na wʊ sɛɛn kũu bɩɩ die pɔ pɔɔfʊ ɛ.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Lɛ ban wa man kʊ ma ban guoro a daba nyã yele, ala lɛ so ka n vɛ̃ ka ba tara wʊ a fɔɔ a lɛ nɛ waan a fʊ sɛ̃. N mɩ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na tuoro wʊ a yele ɩka ba wa a fʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr a fʊ mɩ kaa.”

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: A sojasɩ tun a lɛ a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɛr na ba de a Pɔɔl a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa na ba tɩ tãn Antɩpatɩrsɩ tẽe pɔ.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Wɛr na wa chaar, ba vɛ̃n ka a wiir dẽme tara a Pɔɔl chiine, tɩchɛ ka a gbɛɛ dẽme lɩɛb wa a sojasɩ yir.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Lɛ ban tɩ ta a Sisariya tẽe pɔ ba den a gãn na ba kʊ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ tɩ de a Pɔɔl mɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ nũu pɔ.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Ka a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kar a gãn tɩ na wʊ sʊʊr a Pɔɔl a tẽbuor wʊn yi, wʊn wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Sɩlɩsɩya lɛ wʊ yi.

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr, “A bala na tuo fʊ a yele wa ta ala lɛ ɩn wõ a fʊ 'yɛr.” Wʊ vɛ̃n ka ba tara a Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n be a yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ a Nãa Hɛrɔd na dɔ̃ɔ mɛ tɩ guro wʊ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: A ber anũu puor lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Ananias suun chen be a Sisariya nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne, ka a Tɛtulusɩ na ɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ pʊɔ ba a ba 'mataa chen na tɩ 'yɛr a Pɔɔl 'yɛr a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ niem.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Ban bʊɔl a Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n ka a Tɛtulusɩ na man a wʊ yele kʊ a Feliksɩ na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, “Sɩ din nʊ̃ɔ kor a fʊ jũu, ka a fʊ yɛ̃ lɩɛr yelvɩɛlsɩ kʊ a fʊ paal.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Jie jaa nɩ sɔr jaa pɔ fʊ ɩn nɩkpɛ̃vɩla, a sɩ sɔɔ de fʊ nɩ nuur ayi nɩ pʊpɛl.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n dam fʊ yɔɔ ɛ, yelbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n sʊɔr jaa bãlãa ka fʊ di kãnyir kʊ sɩ.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: Sɩ nyɛn a daba nyãna ka wʊ ɩ gɔ̃bʊɔrɔ, a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa lɛ wʊ ma tɔ a ba jur gbõtaa a wɛr wʊ jaa, na wʊ ɩ a nɩbɛ bala na turo a Najaretɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩdiere.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ fʊ sʊʊr wʊ kaa fʊn nyɛn bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a ala sɩn 'yɛrɛ dɔɔlɔ wʊ ɩn yelmãɛ.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa na be be 'yɛr ɩka a lɛ a Tɛtulusɩ na 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ.

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: A paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kʊn a Pɔɔl sɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “N bɔ̃ɔn naa an ɩ yuom yɔɔ lɛ fʊ kaa a paal nyãna, a nʊ̃mɔ̃ ma naa ka n na 'yɛr a n 'yɛr.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Fʊ̃ʊ sʊʊr ban 'yɛr ɩka a ba gar ber pie nɩ ayi ɩn chen be a Jerusalɛm ɩka n tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn ɛ.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Ba ãsʊɔ jaa ba nyɛ ma ka n chɩrɛ nɩ nɩrɛ jaa be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɛ, bɩɩ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩyɔɔ be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir ɛ, bɩɩ a tẽe pɔ jie jaa ɛ.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: A anyãna ban 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wiil ma a yelmãɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ n sɔɔ naa ɩka n puoro naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ puoro naa lɛ turo a Sɔr 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a sɔr yuo. N sɔɔn de a ala a jaa, na a sɔɔ lɛ a nɛɛ na sɔɔ nɩ a ala a jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: N mɩ tara naa a tɩɛrtɔ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩtãa a ba kaar ɩka nɩsʊ̃n nɩ a putudẽme na ir ɩ vʊɛ.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so ka n mʊɔrɔ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ɩka a n pʊtɩɛr wʊ chaar Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a nɩrɛ niem.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: “N yin kor, tɩ naa lɛ lɩɛb wa a Jerusalɛm nɩ libie ɩka n wa kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme tɩ naa mɩ lo bɔɔr kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: N dɔ̃ɔ pɔl a n mãɛ baar a lɛ ban nyɛ ma a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ. Nɩyɔɔ ba dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ ma ɛ, n mɩ ba dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ damfʊ jaa ɛ.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Asɩya paal pɔ wa, bala mɩ lɛ a ta sɛɛ ɩka ba be ka na ba 'yɛr a n 'yɛr.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Bɩɩ a banyãna na be ka a ba wiil a yelnyɩnɛ ban nyɛ ɩn maal, a lɛ ɩn ara a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ niem.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Ala ba ɩ a 'yɛr bir bʊ̃'yen nyãna tɛɛ ɩn 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa a lɛ ɩn ara a ba niem naa 'yɛr, ‘A kũn na ir ɩ vʊɛ, naa be ara a yɩ niem a yɩn tɔɔr a n 'yɛr a dɩna.’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: A Feliksɩ na wa pãa bɔ̃ɔn a Sɔr yele wʊ wɛl a nɩbɛ bar tɩ 'yɛr, “A Lisiyasɩ na ɩ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ wa wa ala lɛ ɩn 'yɛr a fʊ 'yɛr.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Wʊ den a Pɔɔl kʊ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka a wʊ ma kʊ wʊ sɔr tɩ sɔɔ ka a Pɔɔl barmɩne ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ nɩ a wʊ bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: A maal ber aŋmɩnɛ jaa tɩchɛ ka a Feliksɩ tara a wʊ pɔɔ Durusila na ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ waan. Wʊ tʊ̃n ka ba waan a Pɔɔl a wʊ chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn mana a lɛ fʊn na sɔɔ de a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl 'yɛr naa a lɛ nɛ wa pʊr yeltorsɩ yuor nɩ mãɛ nyɔɔfʊ nɩ lɛ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ na waara. Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a Feliksɩ a wʊ 'yɛr, “Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɛɛn lɛ, chiine maa wa tara jaa ɩn tʊ̃n bʊɔl fʊ.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Wʊ tɩɛr man taa Pɔɔl na kʊ wʊn libie, na wʊ ma bʊɔlɔ a Pɔɔl jaa tɔ̃tɔ̃ ma 'yɛrɛ nɩ wʊ.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Yuom ayi na wa po Pɔsɩyusɩ Festus den a Feliksɩ jie, tɩchɛ a Feliksɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ pɛl a Juu nɩbɛ pʊɔ jũu, wʊ bar a Pɔɔl a be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Festus na de a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ maal ber ata wʊ yin be a Sisariya chen a Jerusalɛm.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Be lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a ba nɩdierbe man a 'yɛr ala ban tara nɩ, a Pɔɔl kʊ wʊ.

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: Ba sʊɔr a Festus nɩbijɩɛ jaa ɩka a lɛ ban sʊɔrɔ wʊ a wʊ sɔɔ na wʊ vɛ̃ a ba tara a Pɔɔl waan be a Jerusalɛm an ɩ ba chɔɔr ũu gu na kʊ a Pɔɔl a be a sɔr pɔ.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Ka a Festus 'yɛr, “A Pɔɔl ben a be a Sisariya a maa mãɛ a kʊ̃ kor ɛ, tɩchɛ ka n chen a be.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne pʊɔ ma a sɩ 'mataa chen, ala ɩ wʊ maal yeldɛɛr a yɩ 'yɛr n kaa.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Wʊn wa maal a be ber aniin bɩɩ pie wʊ chen a Sisariya, wʊn tɩ gã ir wʊ jãnɛ naa a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka ba waan a Pɔɔl.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Ban wa waan a Pɔɔl a Juu nɩbɛ bala na yi a Jerusalɛm wa ara na jilʊ̃ wʊ ŋmara jir kara dɔɔlɔ wʊ tɩchɛ ba maa tʊ̃ɔ wiil a par ɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛr, “Maa ba maal dɛɛr jaa ɛ, naa sɔ̃ɔ a Juu nɩbɛ nɛbinãa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa ɛ.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Festus bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ pɛl a Juu nɩbɛ pʊɔ na wʊ sʊʊr a Pɔɔl, “Fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ do a Jerusalɛm ka a 'yɛr anyãna ban 'yɛr dɔɔl fʊ ka n tɩ tɔɔr a be bɩɩ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “Pʊ̃pãanyã, n ben ara a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie a be an sɛɛ ɩka ba tɔɔr a n 'yɛr. N mɩ ba maal yeldɛɛr jaa a Juu nɩbɛ sɛ̃ ɛ, a lɛ a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ na bɔ̃ɔn.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Maa ta maal dɛɛr naa sɛɛn kũu n kʊ̃ naa tɔr ɩka n kpi ɛ. Tɩchɛ a 'yɛr anyãna a Juu nɩbɛ na 'yɛr dɔɔl ma na ba ɩ yelmãɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa ba tara sɔr na fɩr ma kʊ ba ɛ. Siisa sɛ̃ lɛ ɩn chen ka wʊ tɩ tɔɔr a n 'yɛr.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Lɛ a Festus nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa man taa baar wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, “Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ sʊɔr ɩka a Siisa sɛ̃ lɛ fʊn chen, be lɛ fʊn chen.”

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: A kor bãlãa ka a Nãa Agripa nɩ a wʊ yɛpɔɔ Bernisɩ wa be a Sisariya ɩka ba wa puor a Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Festus.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: An ɩ ban jɛ̃n a be kor jũu, Festus mana naa a Pɔɔl yele kʊrɔ a Nãa Agripa 'yɛrɛ, “Daba kɔ̃ɔ lɛ be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ a Feliksɩ na pɔɔ.

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: A lɛ ɩn chen a Jerusalɛm, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a ba nɩdierbe den yelyɔɔ dɔɔl wʊ na ba sʊɔr ma ɩka n tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ge wʊ.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: “Ka n 'yɛr kʊ ba a Worom nɩbɛ maa tɔɔr nɩrɛ 'yɛr ala ɩ a nɩnyɩnɛ ban 'yɛr a 'yɛr dɔɔl ba wa ara a bala na tara nɩ wʊ a 'yɛr niem na wʊ 'yɛr a wʊ nɛɛ 'yɛr ɛ.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so ka ba pɔɔ ma a sɩ 'mataa wa a ka, n ba wa jɛ̃n a 'yɛr ɛ, a wɛr na wa chaar n jɛ̃ naa na tɔɔr a 'yɛr naa vɛ̃ ka ba waan a daba.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: A bala na tara nɩ wʊ a 'yɛr na wa ir ara na 'yɛr, baa 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa na ɩ dɛɛr nɩtãa lɛ ɩn tɩɛr ban taa 'yɛr ɛ.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a ba 'yɛr tɩ ɩ naa a ba puorfʊ yele lɛ nɩ daba kɔ̃ɔ na di Yesu na kpi ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ir naa na wʊ ɩ vʊr.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Ka a yele nyã sɔb ma nɩbir, n ba bɔ̃ɔ ŋmɩnɛ ɩn na ɩ vɩɛr a yele nyã ɛ, ala lɛ so ka n sʊʊr wʊ ala ɩ wʊn bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ chen a Jerusalɛm ka ba tɩ tɔɔr a 'yɛr anyãna a be.

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl 'lɔ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, n vɛ̃n a ba tara wʊ tɩ tãn a lɛ ɩn na bar sɔr ka ba tara wʊ chiin a Siisa sɛ̃.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Ka a Nãa Agripa pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Festus, “Maa mãɛ naa bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n wõ a daba mɩ kɔkɔr.” Ka a Festus 'yɛr, “Bio fʊn wõn a wʊ kɔkɔr.”

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar, a Nãa Agripa nɩ a Bernisɩ wa naa ka a nɩbɛ de 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ ba ka ba tɩ kpɛ̃n be a dikpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ ban ma lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a sojasɩ nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ. Ka a Festus vɛ̃ a sojasɩ tara a Pɔɔl waan.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Ka a Festus 'yɛr, “Nãa Agripa nɩ a yɩn bala jaa na be ka a sɩ sɛ̃, yɩ nyɛ a daba nyãna, a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma be a Jerusalɛm nɩ a ka a Sisariya tãna 'yɛrɛ ɩka a ba sɛɛ ka wʊ lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: N mɩ ba nyɛ yele jaa wʊn ɩ ka a tara sɔr ka sɩn kʊ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ bojũu wʊ sʊɔr sɔr ɩka wʊ chen a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sɛ̃, n mɩ na bar wʊn sɔr ka wʊ chen a Worom.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ n ba tara a wʊ yele ala na sɛɛ ka n na sɛb kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ ɛ. A lɛ jũu n waan wʊ naa a yɩ jaa niem nɩ a fʊ̃ʊ Nãa Agripa niem a fʊ mɩ jʊʊr a wʊ yele kaa, ka n bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ɩn na sɛb.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Bojũu, a ba sɛɛn ma ɛ, ka n na vɛ̃ ka ba tara nɩrɛ ban pɔɔ chiine naa ba wiil a yelnyɩnɛ wʊn maal ɛ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Kaa Agripa pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, “Fʊ tara na sɔr na 'yɛr a fʊ nɛɛ 'yɛr.” Pɔɔl tur naa a nũu tɩ piel mana a wʊ mãɛ yele,

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Nãa Agripa, a ɩ man bɔɔsʊ̃n ɩn ara a fʊ niem a dɩna naa na man a n yele ala a jaa a Juu nɩbɛ na 'yɛr dɔɔl ma.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ, a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa a Juu nɩbɛ yelbinsɩ a jaa nɩ a ba nɛchɩrɛ, a lɛ jũu n sʊɔr fʊ naa ka fʊ di kãnyir chɛlɛ ma.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Ba bɔ̃ɔ man kor na ban tʊ̃ɔ di a lɛ dãasɩɛ ala ɩ ba bʊɔrɔ naa. Ba bɔ̃ɔn ma naa ɩn turo yele jaa nɛ nɛ nɛ a sɩ puorfʊ pɔ, naa ɩ a Farasee nɩrɛ.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: A pʊ̃pãanyã a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne ka n tara a tɩɛr jũu lɛ so ka n 'yɛr be tɔɔrɔ a dɩna.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: A nɛ tɩra anyãna jũu lɛ a sɩn bʊrɔ pie nɩ ayi ɩ a sɩ tɩɛrtɔ fʊ jie, ka a na wa ɩ a sɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir puoro a Nãaŋmɩn mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ. A fʊ̃ʊ nãa a tɩɛrtɔ nyãna jũu, a Juu nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma a 'yɛr.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Bo lɛ so ka yɩ tɩɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sãɩ kũu ɛ?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “A maa mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ŋme nyãa naa ɩka ɩn ɩ naa lɛ jaa ɩn na tʊ̃ɔ naa sɔ̃ɔ a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ yuor.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ n ɩ be a Jerusalɛm. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ kʊ man sɔr ka n nyɔɔr a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo yɔɔ naa pɔɔ, bala wa kʊrɔ ba n ma sɔɔ naa.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa, n ma chen a be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir naa tɩ ge ge ba, naa ma fɩrɛ ba 'yɛr 'yɛr dɛbɛ tɩchɛ a maa tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a lɛ ɛ. A lɛ a ba yele na dire ma a suur n ma chen a wɛr yuo pɔ tɩ ge ba a be.

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Ala jũu lɛ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ dɔ̃ɔ kʊ ma a sɔr ka n chiine a Damaskus.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sɔ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ̃ʊ nãa, a lɛ ɩn be a sɔr pɔ, n nyɛn chãa na yi saaju chaar mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wʊ jilʊ̃ ma nɩ a bala ɩn lɔ̃ɔ chiine.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Sɩ jaa lon tẽe tɩchɛ ka n wõ nɩrɛ na sʊrɔ ma nɩ a Heburu kɔkɔr, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl bo lɛ fʊ gere ma a lɛ? Fʊ wõn tuo fʊn tɛb a dakʊra.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Be lɛ n pãa sʊʊr, ‘Fʊ̃ʊ nɩ ana, Soro?’ A wʊ 'yɛr, ‘Maa lɛ a Yesu 'lɔ fʊn gere.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã ir ara, n yi fʊ naa, naa ir fʊ a fʊn ɩ a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ na fʊ mʊɔlɔ a ala fʊn nyɛ a dɩna nɩ a ala ɩn na wiil fʊ.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Ɩn faa fʊ naa a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ nɩbɛ nuur pɔ, nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nuur pɔ. Maa lɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ fʊ,

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: tɩ yuo a ba nɩbie na fʊ tɔɔ ba yin a libe pɔ, waan a chãa pɔ, na fʊ yin a Satan kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ, waan a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, a wʊ vɛ̃ a ba yelbier kʊ ba ka ba mɩ nyɛ jie nɩ a bala na sɔɔ de ma, ka n faa ba.’

english_verse: King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven.
translation_verse: “A lɛ jũu, Nãa Agripa, n ba tɔr a nyɛfʊ nyã na yi a saaju ɛ.

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: N den nie man kʊ a Damaskus dẽme, nɩ a Jerusalɛm dẽme, nɩ a Judiya wɛr wʊ jaa, nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ. N mʊɔl kʊ ba ɩka ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr, na ba lɩɛb wa Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, na ba vɛ̃ ka ba tʊ̃mɔ̃ wiil ɩka ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so a Juu nɩbɛ nyɔɔ ma a ba puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kʊ ma.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ sʊ̃ʊ ma wa tãn a dɩna, ka n be ara na man kʊ nɩrɛ jaa. N ba 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa tɩ põn a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ na 'yɛr ɩka an ɩ naa ɛ,

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: ɩka a Nɩ-iraa na din dɔɔyɛ na wʊ mɩ ɩ nɩrɛ na de nie ir a kũu pɔ, na wʊ man a chãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Kana lɛ a Pɔɔl ta ka a Festus kpɛ tãn wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, “Pɔɔl a tɩɛr bɔr fʊ naa, a jãn yɔɔ vɛ̃n ka fʊ gele.”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Festus n ba gele ɛ, ala ɩn 'yɛrɛ ɩn yelmãɛ nɩ yɛ̃ 'yɛr.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: A nãa bɔ̃ɔn a yele anyãna ala lɛ so ka n lɔb yãan bar tɩ 'yɛrɛ nɩ wʊ. N sɔɔ naa ɩka a yele anyãna ba sɔɔl wʊ ɛ, bojũu a yele nyã ba ɩ libe pɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Nãa Agripa, fʊ sɔɔn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bɩɩ? N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka fʊ sɔɔ naa.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Ka a Nãa Agripa pãa sʊʊr a Pɔɔl, “Fʊ tɩɛr man tãa a dɩna tɛɛ lɛ fʊn lɩɛb ma ka n ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto turo bɩɩ?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl 'yɛr, “Ka dɩna tɛɛ lɛ bɩɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ lɛ yo, n sʊɔrɔ naa Nãaŋmɩn ɩka a ta ɩ a fʊ̃ʊ tɛɛ ɛ, tɩ nɩrɛ jaa na chɛlɛ a 'yɛr nyãna a dɩna ka yɩ kaara a n kaar tɩ na ba taa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ yɩ a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ anyãna ɛ.”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Ka a nãa vaa ir nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Bernisɩ lɔ̃ɔn a bala wʊn 'mataa jãnɛ nɩ.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Ba yin a die pɔ, pãa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊrɔ taa na ba 'yɛrɛ, “A daba nyãna ba maal yele jaa na sɛɛn kũu bɩɩ pɔɔfʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Ka a Agripa 'yɛr kʊ a Festus, “Fʊn taa tʊ̃ɔ bar a daba nyãna ala ɩ waa ta sʊɔr sɔr ɩka wʊn chen a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sɛ̃ ɛ.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: An wa ta ɩka sɩn chen a Itali, ba den a Pɔɔl nɩ a bamɩne ban pɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ban bʊɔlɔ Julius, wʊ ɩ naa a nãkpɛ̃ɛ soja kpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro kpɛ̃ɛ na yi a Adɩramatiyum ka wʊ ir an ɩ Asɩya kõkoor lɛ wʊn lana. Aristakus na ɩ a Masedonɩya paal nɩrɛ na wʊ yi a Tɛsalonika pʊɔ sɩ naa.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: A wɛr na wa chaar, sɩ tãn a Sidon tẽe pɔ, ka a Julius maal a Pɔɔl vɩla na wʊ bar wʊ sɔr ka wʊ chen a wʊ barmɩne sɛ̃ ka ba tɩ sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Be lɛ sɩ lɛ yin a gboro lana a Sipurusɩ bojũu a sɛsɛbɛ furo na tuoro sɩ.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: A lɛ sɩn wa gɔ̃ɔ a man sãlsɔɔ Sɩlɩsɩya nɩ a Pamfiliya kõkoor lɛ sɩ wa ara nɩ a Mira na be a Lisiya wɛr pɔ.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a soja nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyɛ a Aleksandiriya gboro kpɛ̃ɛ na chiine a Itali ka wʊ wɛl sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: A ber waa naa sɩn maal a sɔr pɔ, jaa vɛ̃ nɛ nɛ nɛ lɛ sɩ chiine ka a wõ sɩ tɩ sɩ tɩ ta a Sɩnidusɩ tẽe pɔ. Lɛ a sɛsɛbɛ na ba sɔɔrɔ a sɩ chiine a dẽdẽe ɛ, a gboro tun sɩ naa a Kɩrete a Salmone tẽe puor.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: A man kõkoor lɛ sɩ lana ka a wõ sɩ tɩ tãn a jie ban bʊɔlɔ a Gboro Juvɩla Ara A Jie na gbʊr a Lasea tẽe.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Sɩ sɔ̃ɔn sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ a sɔr pɔ a sɛsɛbɛ na furo jũu, ka a gboro chenfʊ mɩ kʊrɔ dãbãɛ bojũu Kɔ̃ Galfʊ sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ mɩ po naa, ka a Pɔɔl kpãana ba 'yɛrɛ,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Nɩbɛ, n kãan nyɛ ka a dãbãɛ be a sɩ chenfʊ pɔ, yelyɔɔ na sɔ̃ɔ naa, a gboro tuor nɩ a sɩn mãɛ mɩ.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ba chɛlɛ a lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ sɔɔ a lɛ a gboro mɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩ lɛ a gboro sʊɔ na 'yɛr.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: A gboro ara a jie ba sɛɛn ban na ara a be a ɔɔr sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a ba yɔɔ jaa 'yɛr naa ɩka ba po chiine, tɩɛrɛ ɩka ban tãn a Fonɩksɩ na ba ara a be. A Kɩrete gboro ara a jie lɛ na tor a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie guba nɩ a nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Lɛ a sɛsɛbaalʊ̃ʊ na wa furo yire a guba jie, ba tɩɛr ɩka ba nyɛn a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ, ba for a kur nɛbie bara tɩ lana nɩ a gboro a Kɩrete gogoro.

english_verse: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
translation_verse: An wa kor bãlãa, ka a sɛsɛbɛ kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie guba sɛsɛbɛ fun yi a be wa.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: A sɛsɛbɛ tʊ̃ɔn a gboro a waa lɛ tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ chiine a niem ɛ, ka a sɛsɛbɛ pãa tara sɩ chiine.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: A lɛ sɩn wa pã a dõdolbile ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Kawuda, a ɩn tuo kʊ sɩ ka sɩn 'mɔɔ a gborbile.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Lɛ a nɩbɛ na wa 'mɔɔ a gborbile ka wʊ do, ba den miir lũ gbɛb a gborkpɛ̃ɛ. Sɩ joro na dãbãɛ ɩka sɩ taa ɩrɛ wa tɩ mʊr a Sirtisɩ bire pɔ ɛ, ka ba bar a kur nɛbie ka a gboro pãa tɔɔrɔ.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: A sɛsɛkpɛ̃ɛ nyã mɔ̃n sɩn jaa ka wɛr wa chaar ka ba piel wɛlɛ a tuor lɔɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: A ber ata daar a gboro dẽme mãɛ foro naa a gboro bomo nɩ a ba mãɛ nuur lɔɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Lɛ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ a ŋmarbie na ba lɛ wa nyɩr ɛ, a ber waa naa tɩchɛ ka a sɛkpɛ̃ɛ chɛnɛ ire ka a sɩ chãɛfʊ tɩɛrtɔ jie baar.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: A ber wa po naa tɩ baa di bon jaa ɛ, ka a Pɔɔl ir ara a ba sãlsɔɔ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Nɩbɛ, yɩn ta sɔɔ tu a n nɛɛ ka sɩ ta chɛnɛ be a be Kɩrete a wõm nyãna kʊ̃ naa pɔɔ sɩ ɛ, ka a bonsɩ be wɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa, yɩ nyɔɔ nyãa bojũu ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ bɔr ɛ, a gboro tɛɛ lɛ na wɛ.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Jãa tãsɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na so ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tʊ̃n a wʊ malaka ka wʊ wa ara a n lõboor,

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, ‘Pɔɔl taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ, fʊn nyɛn a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa nɩ nɩbir ka wʊ tɔɔr a fʊ 'yɛr. Ka a bala jaa fʊn lɔ̃ɔ chiin Nãaŋmɩn den a ba nyɛvʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ a fʊ nũu pɔ.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu yɩ nyɔɔ nyãa nɩbɛ, n sɔɔn de Nãaŋmɩn ɩka wʊn ɩ naa a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr kʊ ma.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ sɩn don dõdol kɔ̃ɔ ju ara.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Ba ŋman a mibe ala na nyɔɔr a nɛbinyɔɔrsɩ ala bar, na ba mɩ lɛ for a vuulsɩ ala na ma dɛnɛ a gboro tɩ pãa yɛr a fɔbaa bar a gboro niem ka a sɛsɛbɛ pãa tɔɔrɔ ba chiine a bire pɔ.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Ka a gboro dẽme bʊɔrɔ sɔr ban na yi a gboro pɔ jo na ba for a gborbile bar ka wʊ suu a man pɔ tɩ maala fɩka a nɛbinyɔɔrsɩ ala lɛ ba foro bara a niem.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Ba joro na dãbãɛ ɩka a gboro wʊ taa tɔ̃n sɩ kʊsɩr ɛ, a ba wɛl a nɛbie ala anaar na ma nyɔɔ a gboro lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ tɩ pãa sʊɔrɔ ɩka wɛr wʊ chaar fɔɔ.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: A Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛr kʊ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a sojasɩ, “Yɩn baa vɛ̃ ka a gboro dẽme banyãna be a gboro pɔ ɛ, sɩ wɛ naa a lɛ nɛ.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Ka a sojasɩ ŋmaa a miir 'lɔ na nyɔɔ a gborbile taa lɛ ka wʊ lo.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka yɩ di a bʊ̃diir na yɩ tara kpɛ̃ɔ, a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa jukɔɔlbir kʊ̃ vʊ̃ɔ a wʊ ju pɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: A anyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr baar tɩ na wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔ amɩne puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan a ba jaa niem, tɩ pãa ŋmaa na wʊ ɔɔrɔ.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ ba jaa ãkpɛ̃ɛ ka a ba mãɛ di a bʊ̃diir amɩne.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Lɛ ban wa di tɩɩ baar, ba wɛl a bʊ̃diir ala na chɛ lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ tɩchɛ ka a gboro ɩ dẽfere.

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Lɛ a wɛr na wa chaar, baa bɔ̃ɔ a jie ban be ɛ, tɩchɛ na ba nyɛ a jie na gɔr ka a bire be be a ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka ala ɩ ba tʊ̃ɔ naa a ba tɩ ara.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a gboro tɩ tɔ a bire na duo a jie ãsʊɔ, ka a gboro niem mʊr wʊ jaa na wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chen ɛ. Ka a kɔ̃sɛbɛ ŋmiere a gboro pãa wɛlɛ.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Ka a sojasɩ ɩ nɛbʊ̃'yen bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kʊ a bala ban pɔɔ ka a ãsʊɔ jaa taa dũu yi jo ɛ.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a Pɔɔl jũu, waa sɔɔ ɩka ba ɩ a lɛ ɛ. Wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ naa a bala na bɔ̃ɔ a dũufʊ ka ba ɔɔ lo a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ de nie dũu do a gogoro ju.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Ka a bala na chɛ mɩ nyɔɔr a gboro daar bɩɩ a gboro da'mɛlɛ ala, nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ba ɩ ka ba ãsʊɔ tɩ jaa do a gogoro ju ka yele ba tara ba ɛ.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Sɩ jaa na wa do baar, ba 'yɛr ɩka a dõdol yuor din Malta.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ bala na be a be maal sɩn vɩla, ba gbɔɔn bũu na ba bʊɔl sɩ a sɩ tɩ uoro, bojũu saa lɛ waara ka ɔɔr kʊrɔ.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Ka a Pɔɔl mɩ vaar delsɩ wa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bũu pɔ, ka a dʊ̃pãn na be a delsɩ pɔ, a bũu tʊl kpɛ ka wʊ yi dʊ̃ a Pɔɔl nũu mar taalɛ.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Lɛ a gogoro dẽme bala na wa nyɛ a lɛ a waa na mar a Pɔɔl nũu pɔ jɔ̃jɔlɔ, ka ba 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, “A daba nyãna na ɩn nɩkʊrɔ, a man na ba di wʊ gba ɛ, tɩchɛ yeltorsɩ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ka wʊ be be ɛ.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl mɩr a waa 'yɔ̃ɔ a bũu pɔ tɩ yele jaa ba tara wʊ ɛ.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: A dõdol 'lɔ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ban bʊɔlɔ Pubilusɩ tara na jie na gbʊr a be a wʊ de sɩ sãan, na wʊ kʊ sɩ bʊ̃diir ber ata.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: A Pubilusɩ sãa bɩɛrɛ na gã, ãgãn tʊl nɩ masʊmɔ̃ lɛ wʊ nyɩɛrɛ. Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n tɩ nyɛ wʊ, wʊn wa sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn baar wʊ dɔɔl wʊ naa nũu sãa wʊ.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Ba den 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ sɩ sɔɩ jaa pɔ, a lɛ sɩn wa chɔɔr na yi, ba kʊ sɩn bon jaa sɩn na bʊɔrɔ.

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Sɩ maal a be ŋmɛrsɩ ata tɩ na sɩ lɛ kpɛ a gboro ãsʊɔ na ara a be a sãɛ. A gboro yin Aleksandiriya na wʊ tara ŋmɩn yɩbɛ Kasɩtor nɩ Poluksɩ.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Sɩ tɩ ara naa Sɩrakusɩ maal ber ata a be.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a gboro yin sɩ tãn a be a Wuregiyum. Wɛr na wa chaar ka a ɩ a guba jie sɛsɛbɛ furo naa, a ber ayi daar ka sɩ ta a be Puteyoli na sɩ suu a be.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Be lɛ sɩ pɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo bamɩne ka ba bʊɔl sɩ ka sɩ tɩ maal daa gbul a ba sɛ̃. Lɛ lɛ a ɩ tɩ sɩ pãa wa ta a Worom.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: A Nãaŋmɩn puorbo na be a Worom na wa wõ ɩka sɩ waara naa, ba yin tuor sɩ tɩ ta Apiyusɩ Daa pɔ na ba pɔɔ sɩ a Sãanba Kpɛfʊ Jiir Ata. A lɛ a Pɔɔl na wa nyɛ a nɩbɛ banyãna wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ ãkpɛ̃ɔ.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Lɛ sɩn wa ta a Worom, ba sɔɔ naa ka a Pɔɔl kpɩɛrɛ a wʊ yõ tɩchɛ ka a soja kaara wʊ.

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na wa maal ber ata, wʊ bʊɔl naa a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe lɔ̃ɔ taa. Lɛ ban wa duo ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N yɛɛr, n gba na ba maal yele jaa bɔr a sɩ nɩbɛ sɛ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ 'mab a sɩ sãakpãmɩne yel-ɩrsɩ ɛ, tɩ ba nyɔɔ ma naa be a Jerusalɛm na ba kʊ a Worom nɩbɛ.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Ba chɛ̃pɛ̃n a yele faa na ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bar ma bojũu n ba maal yele jaa na sɛɛn kũu ɛ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ na ba sɔɔ ɛ, ka n faa tɩ sʊɔr sɔr ka ba chiin ma a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sɛ̃. A ba ɩ n tara na yele kɔ̃ɔ na de dɔɔl a n nɩbɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu lɛ so ka n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n nyɛ yɩ naa 'yɛr yɩ. Bojũu a Isɩral nɩbɛ tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie jũu lɛ ba gbar ma nɩ a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ anyãna.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Ka ba 'yɛr, “Saa nyɛ gãn jaa yi a Judiya dẽme sɛ̃ ɛ, ka a sɩ yɛɛr bala mɩ na yi a be wa ba 'yɛr a fʊ 'yɛrdɛɛr jaa kʊ sɩ ɛ.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ sɩn bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ fʊn nyɔɔ, bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka jie jaa nɩbɛ 'laar naa a yele nyãna.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba 'yɔ̃ɔn bɩbir ban lɛ wa a Pɔɔl sɛ̃, a bɩbir na wa vɩɛ ba wan yɔɔ jaa a be wʊn kpɩɛrɛ. An yi a bibio tɩ tãn a jãanʊɔr na wʊ mana a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kʊrɔ ba, na wʊ de a Mosesɩ nɛɛ nɩ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr ka ba sɔɔ a Yesu.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Bamɩne lɩɛb ba tɩɛr a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne ba sɔɔ de ɛ.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Ba chɩrɛ nɩ taa na ba piel yire lɛ a Pɔɔl na wa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr baaraa na wʊ 'yɛr, “A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr yelmãɛ kʊ a yɩ sãakpãmɩne a lɛ wʊn de a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya nɛɛ

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: 'yɛr, ‘Chen a nɩbɛ banyãna sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn wõn lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ yɩ kʊ̃ wa bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, yɩn fur nɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩ yɩ kʊ wa nyɛ bon jaa ɛ.”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Bojũu a nɩbɛ banyãna nyãn kpɛ̃mɛ naa, baa wõnõ ɛ, ba ma ũun a ba nɩbie, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, ban taa nyɛn naa ba nɩbie na ba wõn a ba tobo bɔ̃ɔ a par na ba lɩɛb wa a n sɛ̃ ka n sãa ba.’ (Isaiya

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl yab a yir 'lɔ pɔ wʊn kpɩɛrɛ yuom ayi, tɩ ma de nɩrɛ jaa na ma wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Yele jaa ba ŋmaa wʊ sɔr ɛ, ka wʊ 'lara na wʊ mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr na wʊ wile ba nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Maa Pɔɔl na ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto gbãgbaa, Nãaŋmɩn na kaa ir bʊɔl ka n ɩ a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nãa naa ɩ a wʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ, lɛ sɛɛrɛ kʊrɔ a yɩn na be a Worom.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: A tun a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩ a wʊ yuor jũu, sɩ nyɛn wãɛfʊ nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ na sɩn bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa, ka ba wa wõnõ a wʊ toor nɩ sɔɔfʊ.

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Yɩn mɩ pʊɔn a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔl ka yɩ ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sofʊ.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: N sɛɛrɛ na kʊrɔ a yɩn bala jaa na be a Worom a Nãaŋmɩn na nʊ̃, na wʊ bʊɔl yɩ a yɩ ɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ. Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na yi a sɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jie a be a yɩ sɛ̃.

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: N den nie puor a maa Nãaŋmɩn yãan a yɩ jaa jũu tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, bojũu a yɩ sɔɔfʊ ŋmen yaar a wɛr wʊ jaa.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: A Nãaŋmɩn nyã ɩn tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ nɩ a n nyãa wʊ jaa, naa mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na ɩ a wʊ Bie 'yɛr, di man dãasɩɛ lɛ ɩn ma tɩɛrɛ a yɩ yele.

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: N ma sʊɔrɔ naa Nãaŋmɩn ba gala ɛ, naa sʊɔrɔ ɩka a wʊ bɔfʊ a ɩ, ka wʊ yuo sɔr a pʊ̃pãanyã kʊ ma ka n wa a yɩ sɛ̃.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: N bʊɔrɔ naa yɩ nyɛfʊ naa na tʊ̃ɔ wiil yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊ amɩne ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ ãkpɛ̃ɔ.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ, a yɩn nɩ maa na pãa nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ̃mɛ a sɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: N yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ɩn mʊɔrɔ sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa, ɩka n wa a yɩ sɛ̃ tɩchɛ ka a sɔr pɔɔ wa tãn dɩna. Ɩn taa nyɛn a yɩ bamɩne a ba puoraa nɩtãa lɛ ɩn nyɛ a yɩ bamɩne na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩ puoro.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Vã ba kʊrɔ ma nɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna 'yɛrfʊ ɛ, bojũu a ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ wʊn de faara nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔ, a Juu nɩbɛ lɛ de nie, ka bala mɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, mɩ tu.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Bojũu a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na wiil nɩrɛ lɛ wʊn na tor Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, torfʊ 'lɔ na yi sɔɔfʊ pɔ, yi a pielfʊ tɩ tãn a baaraa nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, “Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara sɔɔfʊ, tor naa, wʊn tara na nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.” (Habakuk

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃n ka a wʊ suur yele yi a saaju gã chãa pɔ, ɩka wʊ nyɛn suur 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba puoro ɛ, nɩ putudẽme bala na pɔɔ a yelmãɛ nɩ a ba putuyele.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: An ɩ a ala ban na de bɔ̃ɔn a Nãaŋmɩn gãn chãa pɔ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ ir wiil ba.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Bojũu, an yi a lɛ daar jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na mɛ a wɛr, nɩbɛ na tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, ka wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ nɩ a bonsɩ ala wʊn maal, a lɛ jũu, tu yifʊ jie ba lɛ be be go ɛ.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Bojũu, ban sɔɔ bɔ̃ɔn a Nãaŋmɩn a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃, tɩ baa kʊ wʊ tɩɩr ɛ, bɩɩ dãna wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a ba tɩɛrsɩ ɩn burbur, ka a ba jɔl nyãn ala pãa lii.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Ban sɔɔ 'yɛr ɩka yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ lɛ ba ɩ, ba ɩn jɔlɔ,

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: an ɩ ba lɩɛb a tɩɩr 'lɔ ban taa kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ba kpire ɛ, na ba kʊ a bʊtɩbɛ ala ban pɛ̃n, ka a kaara a nɩbɛ kaar na kpire, nɩ lubil, nɩ dʊ̃n, nɩ dʊ̃gbɛɛ anaar jaa bʊrɔ.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Ala lɛ so ka Nãaŋmɩn ir nũu tɩchɛ ba bar be a ba vɔldɛɛr pɔ, ka ba bʊ̃nɔ̃ gãna nɩ taa, sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa nɩ a ba ãgãma.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Ba den a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ tɛɛn jire, na ba puoro tɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ a bonsɩ ala a Nãaŋmɩn na maal, tɩ bar a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a ma maala 'lɔ a pɛɛfʊ na sɛɛn bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: A anyãna jũu, Nãaŋmɩn de ban bar be a vã vɔlaa pɔ. Ka a ba pɔbɔ gba ma lɩɛb gãna nɩ a ba pɔɔ taa ba.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ a dɔɔr mɩ bar a pɔbɔ bɔfʊ, ka a vɔlaa vɛ̃ ka ba lɩɛb bʊɔrɔ a ba taa dɔɔr. Dɔɔr na lɩɛb tʊ̃nɔ̃ a vã tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyãna nɩ a ba taa dɔɔr ba nyɛn a ba tʊ̃dɛɛr toor tɔɔfʊ.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Baa nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔfʊ a wʊ sɛɛn ba ɛ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn bar ba a be a ba tɩɛr sɔ̃ɔnaa pɔ, ka ba maala a ala na ba sɛɛn maalfʊ ɛ.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Ba sɛɛn naa putuo jaa bʊrɔ, bier ɩfʊ, bɔgar dẽme, nɩ bier maalfʊ. Nyuur sɛɛn ba naa, na ba ɩ nɩkʊrbɔ, bʊɔrɔ jɛɛr, na ba bɛlɛ taa, na ba tɩɛrɛ taa sɔ̃ɔfʊ. Ba ɩn gagarsɩ,

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ taa yoe, 'laar Nãaŋmɩn, kpɛrɛ taa, mãɛ 'mɔɔfʊ nɩ yobɔyele. Ba bɔ̃ɔn sɔɩ ban ma tu maal bier, baa turo a nɩbɛ bala na dɔɔ ba nɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: Baa tara tɩɛr ɛ, baa tara yelmãɛ ɛ, baa tara nʊ̃fʊ ɛ, baa kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal ɛ.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Ban sɔɔ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn toraa nyãna binfʊ, ɩka a nɩbɛ bala na maala a anyãna sɛɛn nɩ kũu, bala mãɛ tɛɛ ba lɛ maala a anyãna ɛ, tɩchɛ ba ma sɔɔ naa a bala na tʊ̃nɔ̃ a anyãna.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Yɩn a lɛ jũu, yaa mɩ tara tu yifʊ jie ɛ, a yɩn bala na ma tɔɔrɔ a bamɩne 'yɛr. Yele jaa pɔ fʊn tɔɔr a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ 'yɛr fʊ mɩ 'yɔ̃ɔn fʊ mãɛ 'yɛr, bojũu fʊ mɩ ɩn a yele 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ na ɩ, a fʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
