english_verse: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
translation_verse: Ka a malakasɩ ata sʊɔ mɩ pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, ka a ŋmɛrbir kpɛ̃ɛ na 'lɩrɛ fɩka bũu yi a saaju lo a põnsɩ nɩ a bulsɩ jiir ata a jibʊ̃'yen pɔ.

english_verse: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
translation_verse: A ŋmɛrbir yuor din, Bʊ̃tuo. Ka a kɔ̃n jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen be tuo, ka a nɩbɛ bala na nyũ a kɔ̃tuo ala, ka yɔɔ jie kpi.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: A malakasɩ anaar sʊɔ pɛɛl naa a wʊ ɩɩl, ka ba ŋme a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen nɩ a ŋmɛraa jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen naa ŋmɛrbie ka ba jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen ɩ libe. A bɩbir jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen ba tara chãa ɛ, ka a ŋmɛraa naa ŋmɛrbie mɩ ba chaar a tãsɔɔ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen ɛ.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ n kaa naa wõ tuu na ɔɔrɔ be a saaju 'yɛrɛ nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ, “Nɛbier! Nɛbier! Nɛbier! Yele na wa naa nɩsaal jaa na be a wɛr pɔ, ala ɩ a malakasɩ ata bala na chɛ wa pɛɛl naa a ba ɩlɛ.”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: A malakasɩ anũu sʊɔ na wa pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, ka n nyɛ a ŋmɛrbir 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ yi a saaju lo a tẽe. A ŋmɛrbir nyã lɛ ba de a bɔɔ fulom pãn bir kʊ.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Wʊn wa yuo a bɔɔ fulom nyã nɛɛ, ka jʊʊr yire fɩka bũu kpɛ̃ɛ jʊʊr. A jʊʊr anyãna na yire a bɔɔ fulom nyãna pɔ liin pɔɔ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ naa tẽe wʊ jaa.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: A jʊʊr anyãna pɔ lɛ soor yi yaar a tẽe wʊ jaa ka ba kʊ ba lɔɔ fɩka lɔ̃n lɔɔ.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Ba 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka ba taa ɩrɛ wa ɔɔrɔ mɔɩ bɩɩ bʊ̃bul nɩ a tɩɛ vaar ɛ, tɩchɛ a dʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɩbɛ bala tɛɛ na ba tara a Nãaŋmɩn jãnɛ a ba gbersɩ pɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Baa kʊ ba sɔr ɩka ba kʊrɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba ge a nɩbɛ ŋmɛrsɩ anũu. Ka a ba dʊ̃fʊ ɔɔr kaara fɩka lɔ̃ɔ na dʊ̃ nɩrɛ ɔɔrɔ.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: A ber anyãna pɔ nɩbɛ na bʊɔrɔ na kũu tɩchɛ ba kʊ̃ nyɛ wʊ ɛ. Ban bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba kpi tɩchɛ a kũu kʊ̃ lɛ kʊ ba gba ɛ.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: A soor nyɛtãan wiisɩ ban pãɛ jɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a jur kaara fɩka sɛlma jupile ban su, ka a ba nin nyɛtãan nɩsaal nie.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: A ba kɔɔl kaara fɩka pɔbɔ jukɔɔl, ka a nyɩmɛ mɩ kaara fɩka jukpɛ̃ɛ nyɩmɛ.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Ba tara na nyãa pɔɔrsɩ ka a kaara fɩka kue, ka a pɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ fɩka torkosɩ a wiisɩ na tɔɔrɔ joro chiine jɛɛr jie.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Ba tara na jʊɛ dʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ lɔ̃n kaar, a jʊɛ ala tara na fɔ̃ɔ na dʊ̃n nɩbɛ ge ba ŋmɛrsɩ anũu.

english_verse: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
translation_verse: Ba tara na nãa na kaara ba, 'lɔ lɛ a malaka 'lɔ na kaara a bɔɔ fulom 'lɔ. Heburu nɩbɛ bʊɔlɔ wʊ naa, Abadɔn, ka a Greek nɩbɛ mɩ bʊɔlɔ, Apolyɔn. A par lɛ, sɔ̃sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: A nɛbier 'lɔ na de nie lɛ a nyã na tal, tɩchɛ ka a ayi sʊɔ chɛnɛ be puor waara.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Ka a malakasɩ ayʊɔb sʊɔ pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, n wõn kɔkɔr na yire a sɛlma tãɩ nyũu vɩla chʊmfʊ jie kpʊ̃kpʊɛ, be lɛ ba ma chʊm a kãnyũu na be a Nãaŋmɩn niem.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: A kɔkɔr 'yɛr kʊ a malaka ayʊɔb sʊɔ 'lɔ na tara a ɩɩl, “For a malakasɩ anaar bala ban lũ a be a Yufɩrɩtɩ põn kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ.”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: A wʊ for a malakasɩ anaar ba nyãna ban dɔ̃ɔ lũ gun a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a chuu 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ naa yuon 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩka ba kʊ a nɩsaal jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen bar.

english_verse: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
translation_verse: A wiisɩ nɩ a bala na do ɩn nyɛ a jãna pɔ, a nyãa pɔɔrsɩ mʊ̃ɔ naa fɩka bũu, ka amɩne mɩ ɩ bufulo, ka amɩne mɩ ɩ bʊ̃dʊɔr. Ka a wiisɩ jur nyɛtãan jubɛrɛ jur, ka a bũu jʊʊr nɩ jerbɩ mɩ yire a ba nɛɛ pɔ.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: A gefʊ ata ala na yi a wiisɩ nɛɛ pɔ lɛ, bũu, nɩ jʊʊr, nɩ jerbɩ, ala lɛ ba kʊn nɩbɛ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen.

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: A wiisɩ anyã fɔ̃ɔ be naa a ba nɛɛ pɔ naa jʊɛ pɔ, a ba jʊɛ kaara fɩka waba naa tara jur, ala lɛ ba de gere nɩ a nɩsaal.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: A nɩsaalba bala na chɛ, na baa kpin a gefʊ nyã ɛ, ba sɔɔ bar a tɩbɛ ala ban maal nɩ a ba nuur ɛ. Ba chɛnɛ na puoro a sɩdɛbɛ, nɩ a bʊtɩbɛ ban maal a sɛlma, nɩ a kur pɩɛl, nɩ a kur jɩɛ, nɩ a kusɩbɛ, nɩ a daar na ba wõnõ 'yɛr ɛ, ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chen ɛ.

english_verse: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_verse: Baa mɩ lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr bar a nɩkʊyelsɩ, nɩ sɔyelsɩ, nɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃yelsɩ, nɩ nãnyiyelsɩ ɛ.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: N lɛ nyɛn a malaka kpɛ̃ɔ ãsʊɔ na yi a saaju suro waara. Wʊ vaar naa julõjuur ka a sagõgoraa dɔɔl a jum. A nie kaara fɩka mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, ka a gbɛɛ mɩ kaara fɩka bũu lɩraa.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: Wʊ tara na gãn bile na yuo a wʊ nũu pɔ, na wʊ de a nũsʊ̃ɔ gbɛr 'lɔr a man pɔ tɩ a gʊbaa gbɛr dɔɔl a gogoro ju,

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: tɩ na wʊ pãa tãn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa fɩka jukpɛ̃ɛ na uor. Wʊn wa tãn a lɛ ka a kɔkɔɛ ɩ ayopõi mɩ tãn satanaa kaar.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: An wa tãn a gbɛɛ ayopõi a lɛ, ka n ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka n sɛb tɩchɛ n wõn kɔkɔr na yi a saaju 'yɛr, “A tãnfʊ ayopõi ala na 'yɛr sɔɔl bar, ta sɛb bin ɛ.”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a malaka 'lɔ ɩn nyɛ na 'lɔr a nũsʊ̃ɔ gbɛr a man pɔ tɩ dɔɔl a a gʊba gbɛr a gogoro ju, jɛɛn a nũsʊ̃ɔ a saaju.

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: Wʊ pɔn naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be be jaa kpo kpo, 'lɔ lɛ mɛ a saaju, nɩ a tẽe, nɩ a man, nɩ bomo a jaa na be a ba pɔ, “A kʊ̃ lɛ kor go ɛ!

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a bɩbir 'lɔ a malakasɩ ayopõi sʊɔ na wa pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, a Nãaŋmɩn yelsɔɔlaa na pãa maal baar nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn man kʊ a wʊ gbãgbaar 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a kɔkɔr 'lɔ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ wõ a saaju lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma go, “Chen tɩ de a gãn bile 'lɔ na yuo a malaka 'lɔ na ara a man pɔ naa gogoro ju nũu pɔ.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Ka n chen a malaka sɛ̃ tɩ sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ kʊ ma a gãn bile. Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “De 'wɔb, wʊn mire naa a fʊ pʊɔ pɔ tɩ nʊ̃mɔ̃ a fʊ nɛɛ pɔ sɩkãa kaar.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: N den a gãn bile a malaka nũu pɔ 'wɔb. Ka wʊ nʊ̃mɔ̃ a n nɛɛ pɔ a sɩkãa kaar, tɩchɛ ɩn wa vɔl baar, ka wʊ mire a pʊɔ pɔ.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: A ba lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma go, “'Yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbʊrɔ, nɩ paalsɩ, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrsɩ nɩ a nãmɩne.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ taa ɩrɛ wa mana a yõo ɛ, bojũu be lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ ɛ, na be 'mu 'mulo a tẽchɛchɛ nyãna ŋmɛrsɩ lɩjaayi nɩ ayi.

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: A dãasɩɛ dẽme ayi bala ɩ naa a kãa tɩɩr ayi nɩ a fãtɩnɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie jiir ayi, ba ara naa a Soro nyã na ɩ a wɛr wʊ jaa niem.

english_verse: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ maal ba bier, bũu ma yi naa a ba nɛɛ pɔ di a bala na ɩ a ba dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ. Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tɩɛr a ba sɔ̃ɔfʊ ma kpi.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: A nɩbɛ bala tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na nyɔɔ saa a saaju ka a saa kʊ̃ wa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban mʊɔlɔ ɛ. Ba mɩ tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na lɩɛb a kɔ̃n a jaa ka a ɩ jãɩ, na ba mɩ na tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka yelbier wa a tẽe pɔ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ban bʊɔrɔ.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Ka a ba ãgãma gã be a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ sɔkpɛ̃ɛ ju. A tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã lɛ ba lɔɔrɔ sʊkpa bʊɔlɔ nɩ Sodom bɩɩ Ijipitɩ, be lɛ ba kpa a ba Soro a daa ju.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Nɩbʊrɔ, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrɔ, nɩ balsɩ ba jaa, nɩ paalsɩ a jaa na kaara naa a ba ãgãma ban tɔr a ũufʊ bɩbie ata nɩ bʊ̃ŋmaa.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Nʊ̃ɔ na kpɛ̃n a nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ a wɛr pɔ ka ba chɩlɛ kʊrɔ taa bonsɩ, bojũu ba kʊ naa a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ayi banyãna na dɔ̃ɔ ge a wɛr nɩbɛ.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a bɩbie ata nɩ a bʊ̃ŋmaa ala na wa po, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ ban nyɛvʊr ka ba lɛ ir ɩ vʊɛ, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a bala na nyɛ ba.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: A 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ayi banyãna pãa wõn nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na yi saaju 'yɛr, “Yɩ do wa ka.” Ka ba tu a julõjuur pɔ duoro a saaju ka a ba dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ kaara ba.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ajienaa ka a tẽe mii ka a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ yie jiir pie jibʊ̃'yen lo naa kʊ nɩbɛ tur ayopõi (

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: A nɛbier ayi sʊɔ lɛ a nyã na po, tɩchɛ a ata sʊɔ mɩ turo naa.

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: A malakasɩ ayopõi sʊɔ pɛɛl naa a wʊ ɩɩl, ka a kɔkɔyɔɔ 'yɛrɛ be a saaju, “A wɛr nyã nãalʊ̃ʊ ɩ naa a sɩ Soro nɩ a wʊ Nɩ-iraa nãalʊ̃ʊ, 'lɔ lɛ na di a nãa jaa kpo kpo.”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Sɩ puor naa a Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, 'Lɔ lɛ ɩ, nɩ 'Lɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, bojũu wʊ den a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ piel kaara nɩ a wɛr wʊ jaa.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: A paalsɩ vʊ̃n fʊn nɛɛ ka fʊ suur mɩ dire. A sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka fʊn tɔɔr a kũn 'yɛr, na fʊn yab a fʊ gbãgbaar na ɩ a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ lɔ̃ɔn a fʊ nɩbɛ naa bala na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ yuor, a bil naa bɛrɛ ba jaa, na fʊn sɔ̃ɔ a bala na sɔ̃ɔ a wɛr.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn yir na be a saaju pãn pãa yuo ka ba nyɛ a nɛtɩraa daka be a yir pɔ. Ka saa nyɩɩrfʊ, gɔ̃mɔ̃, nɩ tãnfʊ, ka a tẽe wʊ jaa mii, ka a sakpʊlõbibɛrɛ loro.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Pʊɔ lɛ wʊ tara ka wʊ dɔɔfʊ ta ka wʊ kõnõ nɩ.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a nɛ'maa yele ãsʊɔ lɛ yi a saaju kaara fɩka dajɩl na ɩ jɩɛ na wʊ tara jur ayopõi nɩ ɩlɛ pie ka a nãa jupile be a jur pɔ.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: A wʊ jʊʊr pɩɩr naa a ŋmɛrbie jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen a saaju 'lɔb a tẽe. Tɩ ara gu a pɔɔ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ a dɔɔfʊ ɩka 'lɔ wa dɔɔ wʊ vɔl a bie.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: A pɔɔ dɔɔn a bie ka wʊ ɩ dabile, 'lɔ lɛ na kaara a paalsɩ a jaa nɩ la kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ na kaara a kur dãgbal kaar. Ba jãan a pɔɔ bie nyã don a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ka wʊ tɩ be a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Tɩ ka a pɔɔ mɩ jo kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ na wʊ tɩ be a jie Nãaŋmɩn na chɔɔr bar ka ban kaara wʊ maal ber tur kɔɔr ayi dɔɔl lɩjaata (

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Ka a jɛɛr lo be a saaju. Maɩkɩl nɩ a malakasɩ jɛɛr nɩ naa a dajɩl, ka a dajɩl nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ mɩ lɩɛb jɛɛrɛ nɩ a Maɩkɩl nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ba tʊ̃ɔ naa a dajɩl nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ, dii ba bar a be a saaju.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Ba lɔb a dajɩl kpɛ̃ɛ nyã bar a yõo, a kõr waa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na ɩ a dɛɛr sʊɔ, nɩ a Satan, 'lɔ lɛ bɛlʊ̃ a wɛr nɩbɛ ba jaa. Ba lɔb wʊn bar ka a wɛr pɔ, nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Be lɛ n wõ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɛr be a saaju, “Pʊ̃pãanyã, a faafʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa, a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn wiil naa a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ, nɩ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ, nɩ a wʊ Nɩ-iraa kpɛ̃ɔ. Bojũu a 'lɔ na bɛrɛ a sɩ yɛɛr nɛɛ, 'lɔ lɛ bɛrɛ ba a nɛɛ a Nãaŋmɩn niem mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ, ba daa wʊn lɔb.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Ba tʊ̃ɔ wʊ naa nɩ a Pɛraabile jãɩ nɩ a ba dãasɩɛ 'yɛrbie. Ba sɔɔn kũu na baa de a ba nyɛvʊr ka wʊ ɩ bon ɛ.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, saaju naa bala na kpɩɛrɛ a be, yɩ chɩlɛ! Tɩchɛ nɛbier ben a yɩn na be a wɛr nɩ a man pɔ, yɩn din dɔɔyɛ, bojũu a dɛɛr sʊɔ suun a yɩ sɛ̃! Ka a wʊ suur dire, bojũu a wʊ ber ba waa ɛ.”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Lɛ a dajɩl na wa nyɛ ka a ɩ tẽe lɛ wʊ lo, wʊ dire naa a pɔɔ 'lɔ na dɔɔ a dabile.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Ka ba kʊ a pɔɔ tuu kpɛ̃ɛ pibɛ ayi, ɩka wʊ ɔɔ chen a jie ban chɔɔr bar be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, be lɛ ban tɩ kaara wʊ yuom ata nɩ bʊ̃ŋmaa ka a waa taa pɔɔ wʊ ɛ.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a waa chɩr kʊ̃ɔ a nɛɛ pɔ põn kʊ̃ɔ kaar, ɩka a di a pɔɔ,

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: tɩchɛ ka a tẽe sʊ̃ʊ a pɔɔ haa a nɛɛ nyũ a kʊ̃ɔ ala a dajɩl na chɩr a nɛɛ pɔ.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Ka a dajɩl pãa nyɛ suur 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔ na wʊ chen tɩ ŋmaan a pɔɔ bibiir bala na chɛ jɛɛr. A bala na turo a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ na ba mɩ tara a Yesu 'yɛr yelmãɛ.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Ka a dajɩl ara a man gogoro nɛɛ. N nyɛn mɔdʊ̃ɔ na yire a man pɔ na wʊ tara jur ayopõi tɩ bul ɩlɛ pie ka a nãa jupile su a ɩlɛ ala pɔ. A jur ala pɔ yoe ben be na fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn yuor.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn nyɛ nyɛntãan gbɔ̃gbɔ̃ɔ tɩchɛ ka a gbɛɛ kaara fɩka pɛɛlaa gbɛɛ ka a nɛɛ mɩ kaara fɩka jukpɛ̃ɛ nɛɛ. Ka a dajɩl de a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ a wʊ nãa dakɔɔ nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ ju ãsʊɔ kaara fɩka bon ban chɛ ɩrɛ na kʊ tɩchɛ ka a chɛfʊ jie 'lɔ 'mɔ̃ pɔɔ. Nɛɛ 'maan a nɩrɛ jaa na be a wɛr pɔ tɩ na ba turo a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Ka nɩrɛ jaa pãa pɛɛrɛ a dajɩl bojũu 'lɔ lɛ de a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na ba mɩ pɛɛrɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ 'yɛrɛ nɩ tɩ sʊrɔ, “An lɛ kaara a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã kaar? Nɩ an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaan wʊ jɛɛr?”

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: A wʊ 'yɛrbie chɩrɛ nɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn na wʊ fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor nɩ a jie 'lɔ wʊn be nɩ a bala na kpɩɛrɛ a saaju.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Ba kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ jɛb a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ na wʊ tʊ̃ɔ ba na wʊ mɩ tʊ̃ɔ a nɩbʊrɔ, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrɔ, nɩ balbʊrɔ, nɩ paalsɩ.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: A bala ba jaa yuor na ba dɔ̃ɔ sɛb a Pɛraabile ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a nyɛvʊr gãn pɔ ɛ, tɩ a wɛr wa mɛ, na gbur dumo puor a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyãna.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara toor, wʊ chɛlɛ.

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa nyɔɔfʊ lɛ ban nyɔɔ wʊ, ban nyɔɔ wʊn chiin. Ala ɩ sɔkpɛ̃ɛ mɩ lɛ ban kʊn ãsʊɔ, a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ ban kʊn wʊ. A lɛ jũu a bala na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ, ba tara kãnyir nɩ yelmãɛ.

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: N pãa lɛ nyɛn a mɔdʊ̃ɔ ãsʊɔ na yire a tẽe tara ɩlɛ ayi fɩka pɛraabile tɩ 'yɛrɛ fɩka a dajɩl.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Wʊ yin de a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ kpɛ̃ɔ na wʊ wiil wʊ mãɛ a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ niem tɩ fɩr a nɩbɛ ba jaa na be ka a wɛr pɔ ka ba gbur dumo puor a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ chɛfʊ jie na 'mɔ pɔɔ.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Wʊ tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃sɩ na wʊ gba ma vɛ̃ ka bũu yi saaju suu wa a tẽe ka nɩbɛ ba jaa nyɛ.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: A wʊ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyãna jũu, ba kʊ wʊn kpɛ̃ɔ ka wʊ de a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jie, na wʊ bɛl a wɛr nɩbɛ. Ka wʊ 'yɛr ba ka ba mɛ bʊtɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ chen a sʊɔ tɩ wʊ 'mɔ pɔɔ.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Ba kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ kʊ a bʊtɩɩ 'lɔ nyɛvʊr wʊn mɛ ka wʊ nyɛtãan a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ, ka wʊ na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr ka ba kʊ a bala na tɔr a bʊtɩɩ puorfʊ.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Wʊ mɩ fɩr nɩrɛ jaa, ka bile lɛ wʊ ɩ bɩɩ kpɛ̃ɛ lɛ, taraa sʊɔ bɩɩ nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ, sumɩne bɩɩ gbãgbaa, ka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ ba jaa jãnsɩ a nũsʊ̃ɔ nũu pɔ bɩɩ a gberaa pɔ.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ daara bɩɩ da bon ka baa 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a jãnɛ na ɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ yuor bɩɩ a wʊ jãnɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a wʊ yuor ɛ.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Ka 'lɔ yɛ̃ 'yɛr lɛ, ala ɩ nɩrɛ tara na bɔ̃ɔfʊ, wʊn tʊ̃ɔ sɔr a mɔdʊ̃ɔ sɛbie an ɩ nɩsaal sɛbie lɛ. A sɛbie ɩ naa kɔɔr ayʊɔb dɔɔl lɩjaata nɩ ayʊɔb (

english_verse: who had been rescued from the earth.
translation_verse: ) bala tɛɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na faa ka a wɛr pɔ ba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were
translation_verse: Be lɛ n kaa nyɛ a Pɛraabile ka wʊ 'mataa ara nɩ nɩbɛ turo kɔbaa lɩjaayi nɩ anaar (

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: N wõn kɔkɔɛ a saaju na gɔ̃nɔ̃ fɩka kɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na joro bɩɩ saa na tãn. A gɔ̃mɔ̃ mɩ kaara fɩka kõrõjomsɩ ban ŋmiere.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Ba yiele naa yiel paalaa a nãa dakɔɔ niem nɩ a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bala niem. Nɩrɛ jaa ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ a yiel jãn ɛ, ka a nɩbɛ turo kɔbaa lɩjaayi nɩ anaar (

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: A banyãna lɛ ba sɔ̃ɔ ba mãɛ nɩ pɔbɔ ɛ, bojũu ba gun ba mãɛ ɩ chɛchɛ, na ba ma turo a Pɛraabile jie jaa wʊn chiine, bala lɛ ba da de kʊ Nãaŋmɩn fɩka bʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ ban de kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Pɛraabile.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Baa haa nɛɛ ŋmaa jir ɛ. Baa tara chɛfʊ bãlãa jaa ɛ.

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: N pãa lɛ nyɛn a malakasɩ ãsʊɔ na ɔɔrɔ be a saaju tɩ tara a 'yɛr kpo kpo bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na be ka a wɛr pɔ, nɩ paalsɩ a jaa, nɩ balbʊrsɩ a jaa, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrsɩ a jaa, nɩ nɩbʊrsɩ a jaa.

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: A malakasɩ ayi sʊɔ mɩ tun wa 'yɛrɛ, “Wʊ wɛl naa, wʊ wɛl naa, a Babilon tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã 'lɔ lɛ maal a paalsɩ a jaa a ba nyũ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele dãa ala na tɔɔ Nãaŋmɩn suur waan.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Ka a malakasɩ ata sʊɔ mɩ tu wa 'yɛrɛ nɩ kɔkɔkpɛ̃ɛ, “Ala wa ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa puor naa a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyãna naa bʊtɩɩ nyãna ban mɛ a wʊ kaar, na wʊ sɔɔ ka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a jãnɛ a gberaa pɔ bɩɩ a nũu pɔ,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: wʊ mɩ na nyũn a dãa ala na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn suur, ala lɛ ba pʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ a ŋmele pɔ na ɩ a suur. Ban ge wʊ naa nɩ a jerbɩ bũu na dire a malaka chɛchɛsɩ bala naa Pɛraabile niem.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: A ba gefʊ jʊʊr na uro ba tara ŋmaafʊ ɛ. Pɛ̃nfʊ ba be be kʊ a bala na gbur dumo puor a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a bʊtɩɩ 'lɔ ban mɛ a wʊ kaar mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ tãsɔɔ ɛ, bɩɩ ãsʊɔ jaa na sɔɔ de a jãnɛ na ɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ yuor ɛ.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, a bala na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ na ba turo a wʊ nɛɛ na ba mɩ ara nɩ yelmãɛ kʊ a Yesu ba tara kãnyir.

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: N lɛ kãan nyɛ julõjupɩla, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a julõjuur ju kaara fɩka nɩsaal bie (Danɩɛl

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ a malaka ãsʊɔ mɩ yi be a Nãaŋmɩn yir wa bʊɔlɔ 'yɛrɛ kpɛ̃ɔ jaa kʊrɔ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a julõjuur ju, “De a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ na fʊ piel tʊ̃mɔ̃ bojũu a tʊ̃mɔ̃ sɔ̃ɔ ta naa, bojũu a wɛr nyã ban na ŋma mʊ̃ɔ naa.”

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a julõjuur ju jaal a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ bar a wɛr pɔ na wʊ ŋma a wɛr wʊ jaa.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: A malaka ãsʊɔ mɩ na yi a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a saaju na wʊ mɩ tara a bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir ãsʊɔ.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Ka a malaka ãsʊɔ mɩ 'lɔ lɛ kaara a bũu ka wʊ wa yi a be a tãɩ nyũu vɩla chʊmfʊ jie bʊɔl a 'lɔ na tara a bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir kpɛ̃ɔ jaa tɩ 'yɛr, “De a fʊ bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir 'lɔ na fʊ ŋman a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃, a wɛr lɛ ɩ a tɩɛ ka wʊ wɔ̃mɔ̃ mʊ̃ɔ.”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: A malaka jaal a wʊ sɔkpɛ̃ɛ bar ka a wɛr pɔ ka wʊ ŋmara a wɔ̃mɔ̃ kpaara 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a be ban ma nɛɛrɛ ŋmɛrɛ, na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn suur.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Ba nɛɛrɛ ban a be a nɛŋmɛrfʊ jie a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ yõo, ka a jãɩ joro a ŋmɛrfʊ jie ɩrɛ na ta mɛl kɔɔr ayi ka a wii mɩ ma kpɛ ka a nɛɛ 'lɔr.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: N nyɛ naa a nɛ'maa yelwiilaa jãnɛ ãsʊɔ a saaju, malakasɩ ayopõi na tara wõmsɩ ayopõi, ala lɛ ɩ a baaraa na vɛ̃ ka Nãaŋmɩn suur lo.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: N lɛ nyɛn bon na kaara fɩka man kʊ̃ɔ jaa wel wel nyɩlɛ chãana kaar lɔ̃ɔtãan bũu ka nɩbɛ ara a wʊ gogoro par. Bala lɛ jɛb tʊ̃ɔ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a wʊ bʊtɩɩ nɩ a yuor jãnɛ. Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ban kõrõjomsɩ

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: ka ba yiele nɩ a wʊ gbãgbaa Mosesɩ nɩ a Pɛraabile yiele 'yɛrɛ, “A Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, a fʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ 'maan nɛɛ tɩ põn bar, fʊ ɩn nɩtor nɩ yelmãɛ sʊɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a nãmɩne ba jaa Nãa.

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: An lɛ kʊ̃ joro a fʊ̃ʊ Soro dãbãɛ na wʊ kʊ a fʊ yuor tɩɩr ɛ? Bojũu fʊ̃ʊ tɛɛ lɛ ɩ chɛchɛ. A paalsɩ a jaa na wa gãn kpa tẽe a fʊ niem, bojũu nɩrɛ jaa nyɛ naa a fʊ tʊ̃torsɩ.”

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Bojũu a anyã puor, n kãan nyɛ a saaju ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a jichɛchɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a dãasɩɛ jie ka wʊ yuo.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Ka a malakasɩ ayopõi yi a jichɛchɛ nyãna na ba tara a Nãaŋmɩn suur wõmsɩ ayopõi sun bʊ̃pɩɛl na nyɩlɛ ka a sɛlma bʊ̃lunsɩ bãbã a ba nyãn pɔ.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala ãsʊɔ kʊ a malakasɩ ayopõi bala sɛlma laar ayopõi na sɛɛr nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be be jaa kpo kpo suur.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Ka a jʊʊr sɛɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir yi a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ, ka nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a puorfʊ yir ka a malakasɩ ba wa baar a Nãaŋmɩn suur wõmsɩ ala baar ɛ.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: N wõn nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na yi a be a puorfʊ yir 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a malakasɩ ayopõi bala, “Yɩ chen tɩ kpaar a Nãaŋmɩn suur a laar pɔ bar a wɛr.”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Ka a malaka dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ chen tɩ kpaar a wʊ laa bar ka a tẽe ka a natɩdɛbɛ na bɩɛrɛ tɛ a nɩbɛ bala na tara a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ jãnɛ tɩ mɩ gbur dumo puor a bʊtɩɩ.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: A malaka ayi sʊɔ mɩ kpaar a wʊ laa bar a man pɔ, ka a kʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb fɩka kũu jãɩ, ka a bʊ̃vʊʊrsɩ a jaa na be a man pɔ kpi.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: A malaka ata sʊɔ mɩ kpaar a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ a põnsɩ nɩ kʊlsɩ pɔ, ka a kɔ̃n mɩ lɩɛb jãɩ.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Be lɛ n wõ a malaka 'lɔ na kaara a kɔ̃n 'yɛr, “Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Torfʊ Sʊɔ, a fʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nyã tor naa, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ, fʊ̃ʊ mɩ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be Chɛchɛ Sʊɔ a yelsɩ anyãna fʊn tɔɔr a 'yɛr jũu.

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Bojũu a putudẽme bala lɛ kʊ a fʊ nɩbɛ nɩ a fʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ a fʊ kʊ ba a jãɩ ɩka ba nyũ, a lɛ sɛɛn ba naa.”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ n wõ kɔkɔr na 'yɛr be a bɔɔr lofʊ jie, “Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, a fʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ ɩn yelmãɛ tɩ mɩ tor.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: A malakasɩ anaar sʊɔ kpaar a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ tara kpɛ̃ɔ vuul a nɩbɛ nɩ bũu.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ tʊl vuul ba naa tɩ ba chɛnɛ tʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ tara a gefʊ anyãna a jaa kpɛ̃ɔ na baa lɩɛb ba tɩɛr kʊ wʊ tɩɩr ɛ.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: A malakasɩ anũu sʊɔ kpaar a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nãa dakɔɔ ju, ka a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ wʊ jaa lɩɛb libe, ka a wõnõ a nɩbɛ ka ba ɔɔrɔ jãlbie

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: tɩ chɛnɛ tʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be a saaju a ba natɩɛ nɩ an bɩɛrɛ ba jũu. Tɩchɛ baa lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr bar a ba tʊ̃dɛbɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: A malakasɩ ayʊɔb sʊɔ mɩ kpaar naa a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yufɩrɩtɩ põn kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, ka a kʊ̃ɔ sɩr chɔɔr a sɔr bar kʊ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie nãmɩne.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: N lɛ nyɛn a sɩdɛbɛ na kaara pãtɩɛ kaar ka a yire a bon 'lɔ na kaara a dajɩl kaar nɛɛ pɔ, naa lɛ yire a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ mɩ nɛɛ pɔ, naa mɩ lɛ yire a jir ŋmarba 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɛɛr pɔ.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: A sɩɩr anyã ɩ naa a tɩɩ sɩɩr na tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃sɩ. Ba chen naa a wɛr wʊ jaa nãmɩne sɛ̃ tɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa na ŋmaan a Nãaŋmɩn Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ jɛɛr a wʊ bɩbir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ daar.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: “Nyɛ ɩn wan nãnyie kaar. Bɔɔsʊ̃n sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na ba gur ɛ, tɩ mɩ tara a wʊ bʊ̃sursɩ jɛn na wʊ kʊ̃ wa chiine nɩ sal'mʊɔr ka a ɩrɛ wʊ vĩ ɛ.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Be lɛ a sɩɩr lɔ̃ɔtaa a nãmɩne a jie 'lɔ a Heburu nɩbɛ na bʊɔlɔ Amagedɔn.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: A malakasɩ ayopõi sʊɔ kpaar naa a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ sɛsɛbɛ pɔ, ka kɔkɔr yi be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a nãa dakɔɔ par 'yɛrɛ kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, “A maal naa!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Ka a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'mar jiir ata, ka a paalsɩ tẽbɛrɛ mɩ lo lo. Nãaŋmɩn chɛnɛ na tɩɛr tɔ a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ Babilon na wʊ gbʊ̃m wʊ a dãa na sɛɛ ŋmele na ɩ a wʊ suur.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Dõdolsɩ a jaa bɔr naa ka fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ tɔ̃ɔ go ɛ,

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: A malakasɩ ayopõi na tara a laar ayopõi, ãsʊɔ wan wa 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Wa, ɩn wiil fʊn a lɛ a pɔɔ baa nyã yuor na yi na jãnɛ a mansɩ nɛɛ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: 'Lɔ lɛ a wɛr nãmɩne gãn ka a wɛr nɩbɛ mɩ buu nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Be lɛ, a malaka 'mɔɔ man a Sɩɛ pɔ chiin a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ. Be lɛ n tɩ nyɛ a pɔɔ na jãnɛ a mɔdʊ̃jɩɛ ju. Ka a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã ãgãn wʊ jaa pɔɔn yoe na fere nɩ Nãaŋmɩn na wʊ tara jur ayopõi nɩ ɩlɛ pie.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: A pɔɔ vaar naa fɔjɩ ile ile na wʊ pãɛn sɛlma nɩ kʊsɩsʊ̃n nɩ lɛrbikpãɛn. Wʊ tara naa a sɛlma ŋmele a wʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ pɔ a wʊ tʊ̃ kakaba nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele na sɛɛ.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Yuor sɛb mar a wʊ gberaa pɔ, “Fʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, a Babilon na yi yuor, pɔɔ baar ma, nɩ a wɛr tʊ̃ kakabsɩ ma.”

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: N nyɛn a pɔɔ na nyũ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ jãɩ buu, a bala na di a Yesu dãasɩɛ. Ɩn wa nyɛ wʊ a lɛ, nɛɛ 'maa ma naa.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Ka a malaka sʊʊr ma, “Ŋmɩnɛ a nɛɛ 'maa fʊ? Ɩn wiil fʊ naa a pɔɔ nɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ wʊn do na tara jur ayopõi nɩ ɩlɛ pie yelsɔɔlaa par.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ fʊn nyɛ, wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be be, pʊ̃pãanyã waa lɛ be be go ɛ. Wʊn yi naa a bɔɔ fulom pɔ na wʊ chen a sɔ̃ɔfʊ jie. A wɛr nɩbɛ bala yoe na ba sɛb a nyɛvʊr gãn pɔ ɛ, yi a wɛr maalfʊ daar ɛ, bala wa nyɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã, nɛɛ na 'maa ba naa bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be be na waa lɛ be be ɛ, tɩ na wʊn lɩɛb wa.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: “A ka ɩ naa tɩɛr na tara yɛ̃ 'yɛr. A jur ayopõi ɩ naa a tɔ̃n ayopõi a pɔɔ na jãnɛ, nɩ a nãmɩne ayopõi.

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: A ba anũu kpi naa tɩ ka bʊ̃'yen chɛnɛ, bʊ̃'yen mɩ ba wa sɛrɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ 'lɔ wa wa, wʊn kor bãlãa.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ be be na waa lɛ be be go ɛ, wʊ ɩ naa a nãmɩne aniin sʊɔ na pʊɔ ayopõi bala pɔ na chiine a sɔ̃ɔfʊ jie.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: “A ɩlɛ pie ala fʊn nyɛ ɩ naa nãmɩne pie na ba di a nãa sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ban kʊ ban kpɛ̃ɔ ka ba ɩ nãmɩne lɔ̃ɔn a mɔdʊ̃ɔ sɔ̃ɔ bãlãa.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Ba jaa tɩɛr ɩn bʊ̃'yen, ka ba de a ba kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ fɔ̃ɔ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Ban ŋmaan naa a Pɛraabile jɛɛr, tɩchɛ ka a Pɛraabile na tʊ̃ɔ ba, bojũu 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a sorbo ba jaa Soro nɩ a nãmɩne ba jaa Nãa. A bala wʊn bʊɔl na wʊ chɛl ir ka ba turo nɩ yelmãɛ, na pʊɔ wʊ naa.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ ma, “A kɔ̃n ala fʊn nyɛ be a pɔɔ baa 'lɔ na jãnɛ, ɩ naa a nɩbʊrsɩ, nɩyɔɔ, paalsɩ nɩ 'yɛrbʊrsɩ.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a ɩlɛ pie ala fʊn nyɛ ban 'laar naa a pɔɔ baa nyãna na ba tɔɔ wʊ chɩɛr tɩ chɛ wʊ sal'mʊɔr. Ban 'wɔb wʊ naa tɩ chɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ ka wʊ di.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Bojũu an ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ de 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba tɩɛr pɔ ka ba na ɩ a wʊ bɔfʊ, ka ba jaa tɩɛr ɩ bʊ̃'yen ka ba sɔɔ de a ba kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ ka wʊ di nãa ka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr wa maal baar.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: A pɔɔ fʊn nyɛ ɩ naa a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ nãa na tʊ̃ɔ a wɛr nãmɩne ba jaa.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: A anyã puor, n nyɛn a malaka ãsʊɔ na yi a saaju suro waara. Wʊ tara na kpɛ̃ɔ. A chãa na be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ chaar naa a wɛr wʊ jaa.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Na wʊ tãn kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛrɛ, “Wʊ wɛl naa! A Babilon tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ wʊ wɛl naa! Wʊ lɩɛb dɛɛr dẽme gaa jie, nɩ sɩdɛbɛ, nɩ lubil dɛbɛ nɩbɛ na ba ɔɔrɔ ɛ kpɛfʊ jie.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Bojũu an ɩ a paalsɩ ba jaa buu naa nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele, ka a nãmɩne na be ka a wɛr pɔ gãn wʊ. A yayarba na be a wɛr pɔ len tu a wʊ bʊ̃yɔɔ pɔ.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Be lɛ n lɛ wõ kɔkɔr ãsʊɔ na yi a saaju 'yɛr, “Maa nɩbɛ, yɩ wa yi a wʊ pɔ na yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa pʊɔ a wʊ dɛɛr pɔ ɛ, bɩɩ na yɩ pʊɔ a wʊ wõm pɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: Bojũu a wʊ maal dɛbɛ dɔɔl taa tɩ ta a saaju, ka Nãaŋmɩn tɩɛr tɔ a wʊ dɛɛr wʊn maal.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Yɩ kʊ wʊ nɩtãa lɛ wʊn kʊ yɩ. Yɩ yab wʊ tɩ lɛ dɔɔl wʊ. Yɩ kʊ wʊ a dãa nɩ a wʊ ŋmele nɛɛr ayi ka wʊ nyũ.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Ge wʊ ka wʊ waal, a yuor nɩ a nʊ̃ɔ wʊn bʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ a wʊ mãɛ. Wʊ ma chɛn nyãa 'yɛr, ‘N ɩn pɔɔ nãa jãnɛ, n ba ɩ pɔɔ kʊɔr ɛ. Nɩbaal kʊ̃ wa kpɛ ma jaa ɛ.’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: A lɛ jũu, bɩbir bʊ̃'yen lɛ a wõm na wa a wʊ ju, kũu, nɩ kõn, nɩ kɔ̃. Bũu lɛ ɩ a di wʊ an ɩ a Soro Nãaŋmɩn nyã na tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ɩn tʊ̃ɔfʊ sʊɔ.

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: “Ka a wɛr nãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ gãn wʊ na ba 'mataa din wʊ a nʊ̃ɔ, bala wa nyɛ a lɛ wʊn dire ka a jʊʊr uro, an ɩrɛ ban nɩbaal ka ba kõnõ kʊrɔ wʊ.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa a ger nyã jũu, ban ara na tɛɛr jaa tɩ kõnõ 'yɛrɛ, ‘Nɛbier, nɛbier, a fʊ̃ʊ tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã, pa Babilon, a fʊ̃ʊ nyã na tara a kpɛ̃ɔ, bɩbir bʊ̃'yen lɛ a sɔ̃ɔfʊ be wa a fʊ ju.’

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: “A yayarba na be a wɛr pɔ na kõnõ na tɩ waala bojũu nɩrɛ ba be be na da a bonsɩ go ɛ.

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: A sɛlma, kpãna, kʊsɩsʊ̃n nɩ a lɛrbikpãɛn, nɩ fuu pɩɛl, nɩ fuujɩ ile ile, nɩ fuu saalsɩ, nɩ a fuujɩ kpãn kpãn, nɩ a daar ala nyũu na nʊ̃mɔ̃, nɩ bomo ala jaa ban de wab chɛlɛ maal. A daar ala daa na be tuo, a kurjɩɛ, nɩ kursɔɔlɔ, nɩ a kʊsɩkpũlo bie.

english_verse: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
translation_verse: A kãnyũu vɩɛl ala, nɩ a dãn, nɩ a kãn, nɩ a jɔ̃n, nɩ chir, nɩ a niin, nɩ a piir, nɩ a wiisɩ, nɩ a wiisɩ torkosɩ, nɩ a gbãgbaar.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: “A bʊ̃vɩɛlsɩ ala na ɩrɛ fʊ a vɔlaa, a jaa chen naa. A fʊ nãbomo naa fʊ yobɔyele a jaa bɔr naa. Fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ nyãa go ɛ.

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: A yayarba na da a bomo kʊ wʊ na ba le, nɛɛ na 'maa ba naa a wʊ ge fʊ nyã pɔ, ka ba sɛr yi tɛɛr jaa tɩ kõnõ waala

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: 'yɛrɛ nɩ, ‘Nɛbier, nɛbier, a fʊ̃ʊ tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã, a fʊ̃ʊ na ma su a fɔpɩɛl, nɩ a fɔjɩ ile ile, nɩ a fɔkpãn kpãnsɩ. Na fʊ ma pãɛn a sɛlma, nɩ a kʊsɩsʊ̃n, nɩ a lɛrbikpãɛn.

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Mɔ̃tɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen lɛ a fʊ nãbomo anyãna be wɛ a jaa.’ A bala na kaara a gborbɛrɛ ala a man kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, nɩ a nɩbɛ bala na be be, nɩ a bala jaa na tʊ̃nɔ̃ dire be a man pɔ, na ara na tɛɛr.

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: Bala wa nyɛ a jʊʊr ban kõnõ na 'yɛrɛ nɩ, ‘Tẽbuor lɛ be ka na wʊ ta a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã?’

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Ban ɔɔn tãna 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a jur pɔ tɩ kõnõ tɩ waala 'yɛrɛ nɩ, ‘Nɛbier, nɛbier, a fʊ̃ʊ tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã. A jie nyã a bala jaa na tara a gborbɛrɛ yʊɔrɔ nɩ a man pɔ, ba jaa len tu a fʊ jie, tɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen a fʊ be wɛ a nɩtɔ̃?’

english_verse: The heavens should be happy with God's people and apostles and prophets. God has punished her for them.”
translation_verse: Saaju, chɩlɛ a wʊ jũu, yɩn Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɩ chɩlɛ. Nãaŋmɩn tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn maal yɩ.”

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a malaka kpɛ̃ɔ pãa 'mɔɔ kʊsɩr kpɛ̃ɛ na jãtãan nɩɛr lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ tɩ 'yɛr, “Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ ɩn lɔb fʊ bar, a fʊ̃ʊ Babilon tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã ka nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ fʊ jaa go ɛ.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Ba kʊ̃ lɛ wõ kõrõjom ŋmefʊ, nɩ yiel yielbe, nɩ daa pɛɛlbɛ, nɩ ɩlɛ pɛɛlbɛ jaa go ɛ. Tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ jaa bʊrɔ kʊ̃ lɛ be be go ɛ, ba kʊ̃ lɛ wõ jɔ̃ nãɛnbɛ dɔ̃ɔn go ɛ.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
translation_verse: Fãtɩn bũu kʊ̃ lɛ chaar be go ɛ. Ba kʊ̃ lɛ wõ pɔɔ paalaa nɩ wʊ sɩrɛ kɔkɔr go ɛ. A fʊ yayarba lɛ dɔ̃ɔ yi yuor a wɛr pɔ. A fʊ tãsɔɔ yele lɛ fʊ de bɛl a paalsɩ jaa ka ba bɔr.

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: A wʊ pɔ lɛ ba nyɛ a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ jãɩ nɩ bala ba jaa ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a wɛr pɔ.”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: A anyã puor, n wõn gɔ̃mɔ̃ fɩka nɩyɔɔ na tãna be a saaju 'yɛrɛ nɩ, “Dãnfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn! Faafʊ nɩ tɩɩr nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ ɩ naa a wʊ tʊɔr,

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Ba lɛ tãna na go, “Dãnfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn. A wʊ jʊʊr na uro duoro ba tara ŋmaafʊ ɛ.”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Be lɛ ka a kɔkɔr yi be a nãa dakɔɔ par 'yɛrɛ, “A yɩn na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn gbãgbaar na yɩ joro wʊ dãbãɛ. Ka bil bɩɩ bɛrɛ lɛ, yɩ pɛɛrɛ a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn!”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: N pãa lɛ wõn dɔ̃ɔn fɩka nɩyɔɔ na gɔ̃nɔ̃, bɩɩ fɩka kɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na joro, bɩɩ saa na tãna kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, “Dãnfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn! Bojũu a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, lɛ di a nãa.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Yɩ ɩ a sɩ chɩlɛ ka sɩ ãgãn 'maa a sɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr an ɩ a wʊ Pɛraabile pɔɔ kulfʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa, ka a pɔɔ mɩ chɔɔr a wʊ mãɛ baar.

english_verse: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
translation_verse: Wʊ sun bʊ̃pɩɛl na vɩɛlɛ tɩ nyɩlɛ.” A bʊ̃pɩɛl anyãna lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ yeltorsɩ.

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: ) Wʊn nɛɛrɛ ban ŋmɛrɛ be a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃ nɛŋmɛrfʊ jie nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ suur.

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Ka n gã kpa tẽe ɩka n puor wʊ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Ta maal a lɛ ɛ. N mɩ ɩ naa a gbãgbaa nɩtãa a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr kaar, bala lɛ tara Yesu dãasɩɛ. Puor Nãaŋmɩn! Bojũu, a Yesu dãasɩɛ lɛ ɩ a Sɩɛ 'yɛr.”

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: N nyɛn a saaju na yuo, ka a ɩ wii pɩla lɛ, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na do yuor di, yelmãɛ sʊɔ nɩ nɩtor. Yeltorsɩ lɛ wʊ ma tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr tɩ ŋmaan wʊ jɛɛr.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: A wʊ nɩbie 'lɩrɛ naa fɩka bũu, ka a nãjupil yɔɔ su a wʊ ju pɔ. Yuor sɛb mar wʊ nɩrɛ jaa na ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ka 'lɔ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: A fuu wõo wʊn su ɩn jãɩ wʊ jaa ka a yuor di, “Nãaŋmɩn 'Yɛr.”

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: A saaju sojasɩ sun bʊ̃pɩɛl don wii pɩɛl turo nɩ wʊ.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Ka a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ wʊ de chɩɛrɛ nɩ a paalsɩ ba jaa, “Wʊn kaara ba jaa nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ na kaara a kur dãgbal kaar.” (Yiel Gãn

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Yuor nyãna lɛ be a wʊ fuu wõo na suu a gbɛkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ ɩka, A NÃMƖNE BA JAA NÃA, NƖ A SORBO BA JAA SORO.

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: N nyɛn malaka na ara a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ bʊɔl a lubil a jaa na ɔɔrɔ be a saaju nɩ kɔkɔkpɛ̃ɛ, “Yɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa wa a Nãaŋmɩn lɔ̃ɔ di jie.

english_verse: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Lɛ lɛ, yɩ wa dire a nãmɩne ãgãma nɩ a fɔ̃n dẽme nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme, a wiisɩ nɩ a ba duorbo, gbãgbaar nɩ sumɩne, a bil nɩ a bɛrɛ, a nɩbɛ banyãna ba jaa ãgãma.”

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Ka n nyɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a wɛr nãmɩne nɩ a ba sojasɩ lɔ̃ɔn taa ŋmaan a 'lɔ na do a wii nɩ a wʊ sojasɩ jɛɛr.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ba nyɔɔn a mɔdʊ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔn a jir ŋmara 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ niem. A nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyãna lɛ wʊ de bɛlʊ̃ a bala na tara a mɔdʊ̃ɔ jãnɛ tɩ puor a bʊtɩɩ. Ba lɔb ba jaa ayi bʊ̃nɩbie 'yɔ̃ɔ a jerbɩ bũu na dire pɔ.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: A bala na chɛ, a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a wii ju kʊ ba naa nɩ a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir 'lɔ na yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ, ka a lubil pãa di a ba ãgãma tɩɩ.

translation_verse: Wʊ lɔb wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a bɔɔ fulom nyã pɔ, tɩ kpal na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ jãnɛ a wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ a paalsɩ bɛl go ɛ, ka a yuomo tur (

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: N nyɛn nãa dakɔɔr, ka a bala na jãnɛ, ba kʊ ban sɔr ɩka ba tɔɔr 'yɛr. N lɛ nyɛ naa a bala na dɔ̃ɔ dire a Yesu dãasɩɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr jũu ban kʊ, n nyɛn a ba sɩɩr. english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a nɩ chɛchɛsɩ banyãna na de nie ir a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊɛ. A gbɛɛ ayi kũu kʊ̃ lɛ pɔɔ ba go ɛ, tɩchɛ ban ɩn a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lorbo, ka bala nɩ a Nɩ-iraa lɔ̃ɔ di nãa a yuomo tur (

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: ka wʊ yi bɛlʊ̃ a wɛr kõkobo anaar wʊ jaa nɩbɛ, Gɔɔg nɩ Magɔgɩ na wʊ lɔ̃ɔ ba jaa ka ba ŋmaa jɛɛr. A nɩbɛ waa naa fʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba sɔr ɛ.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Ba yɛr a wɛr wʊ jaa na ba wa jilʊ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ jaa jie a tẽe 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na nʊ̃. Tɩchɛ ka a bũu yi saaju lo di ba jaa.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Ka a dɛɛr sʊɔ 'lɔ na bɛlʊ̃ ba, ba lɔb wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a jerbɩ bũu na dire pɔ, be lɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a jir ŋmara 'yɛ'yɛrɛ be. Ban ge ba naa mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ wʊ jaa, ka ba be be jaa kpo kpo.

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: N pãa lɛ nyɛn a nãa dakɔɔ pɩla, ka a 'lɔ na jãnɛ. A tẽe nɩ a saaju bɔr a wʊ niem, baa lɛ tara jie go ɛ.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: N nyɛn a kũn, bil naa bɛrɛ ka ba ara a nãa dakɔɔ niem, a ba yuor gãma bin. Ba lɛ yuon a gãn ãsʊɔ na ɩ a nyɛvʊr gãn. Ba tɔɔr naa a kũn bala 'yɛr nɩtãa a lɛ ban dɔ̃ɔ maal a ba sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a gãn pɔ bin.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: A man tɩr naa a kũn ala wʊn tara, ka a kũu nɩ a kpĩisɩ tẽe mɩ tɩr a kũn bala ban tara, ka ba tɔɔr nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ jaa 'yɛr a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ maal.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Ba lɔb a kũu nɩ a kpĩisɩ tẽe 'yɔ̃ɔ a bũu dagel pɔ. A bũu nyã na dire lɛ a kũu gbɛɛ ayi.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ jaa yuor ba wa sɛb a be a nyɛvʊr gãn pɔ ɛ, ban lɔb wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a bũu dagel nyã pɔ.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Ka n wõ kɔkɔr na yi a be a nãa dakɔɔ par 'yɛrɛ, “Pʊ̃pãanyã, Nãaŋmɩn na kpɩɛraa a nɩsaalba pɔ, na wʊ be a ba sɛ̃. Ban ɩn a wʊ nɩbɛ, ka wʊ mɩ be a ba sɛ̃ ɩ a ba Nãaŋmɩn.

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: Wʊn 'yɩɛr naa a ba nɩbikʊ̃ɔ a nie pɔ bar. Kũu kʊ̃ lɛ be be go ɛ. Waal ba lɛ be be go ɛ. Kõn mɩ ba lɛ be be go ɛ. Nɩrɛ ba lɛ bɩɛrɛ be go ɛ. Bojũu a yelkoro a jaa põn chen.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Ka a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju 'yɛr, “N maala naa a bomo a jaa bʊ̃paalaa!” Na wʊ pãa lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Sɛb a anyãna bin, baal ba be be ɛ, a ɩn yelmãɛ.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, “A maal naa. Maa lɛ a Pielfʊ nɩ a Baaraa. 'Lɔ jaa a kɔ̃nyũur na kpɛ, ɩn kʊ wʊn a nyɛvʊr kʊ̃ɔ na yi a bulo pɔ ka wʊ nyũ pɔrɔ.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na jɛb tʊ̃ɔ, ɩn kʊ wʊn a anyãna a jaa. Ka n ɩ a wʊ Nãaŋmɩn ka wʊ mɩ ɩ a n bie.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ a bala na ɩ a dãbãn dẽme, nɩ bala na ba sɔɔ de ɛ, nɩ tɩɛr dɛbɛ dẽme, nɩ nɩkʊrbɔ, nɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ, nɩ sʊɔbɔsɩ, nɩ tɩpuorbo, nɩ jir ŋmarba ba jaa, a ba jie ɩ naa be a jerbɩ bũu dagel pɔ. A nyã lɛ ɩ a kũu gbɛɛ ayi.”

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Ka a malakasɩ ayopõi na tara a wõm ayopõi 'lɔ na tara a baaraa wa 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Wa, ka n wiil fʊ a pɔɔsaraa na ɩ a Pɛraabile pɔɔ.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: A malaka 'mɔɔ man a Sɩɛ pɔ don a tɔ̃ wõo kpɛ̃ɛ ju na wʊ tɩ wiil ma a Tẽchɛchɛ na ɩ a Jerusalɛm na yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ suro waara.

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: Wʊ chaar wʊ jaa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr, a chaarfʊ kaara fɩka a kʊsɩsʊ̃n na wʊ nyɩlɛ chaar jaa wel wel a kʊsɩbɛ ala ban bʊɔlɔ a jasɩpa kaar.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Dachin wõo na kpɛ̃mɛ jilʊ̃ wʊ jaa na wʊ tara dɔ̃dɔɩ pie nɩ ayi, ka a dɔ̃dɔr ãsʊɔ jaa tara malaka na kaara. Ba sɛb a Isɩral bal pie nɩ ayi yoe mar a dɔ̃dɔɩ pɔ.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Dɔ̃dɔɩ ata ben a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie, ka ata mɩ be a kõkoor, ka ata mɩ be a kõkoor nyã, ka a ata mɩ be a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ mʊrfʊ jie.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Dachin par kparsɩ pie nɩ ayi ju lɛ a dachin dɔɔl. Ala pɔ lɛ ba sɛb a Pɛraabile nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi yoe dɔɔl.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: A malaka 'lɔ na 'yɛrɛ nɩ ma tara na sɛlma damanaa ɩka wʊ man a tẽe dɔ̃dɔɩ nɩ a dachin.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: A tẽe par kparaa jãtaa, a wõofʊ nɩ a yalfʊ wʊ jaa. Wʊ man a tẽe nyɛ ka wʊ ɩ mɛl tur dɔɔl kɔɔr anaar (

english_verse: Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about
translation_verse: Wʊ man a dachin ka a yal ɩ gbɛɛ kɔɔr ayi dɔɔl pie nɩ ayʊɔb (

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: A kʊsɩbɛ ala ban bʊɔlɔ a jasɩpa lɛ ba mɛn a dachin, ka a tẽe wʊ jaa ɩ sɛlma na wʊ chaar chãana kaar.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Kʊsɩsʊ̃n a jaa bʊrɔ lɛ ba de kpar a tẽe par kparaa. A par kpardɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn a kʊsɩsʊ̃n ala ban bʊɔlɔ a jasɩpa, ka a ayi sʊɔ mɩ di safer, ka a ata sʊɔ di agete, ka a anaar sʊɔ mɩ di ɛmerald,

english_verse: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
translation_verse: ka a anũu sʊɔ di ɔnɩksɩ, ka a ayʊɔb sʊɔ mɩ di kanɩlian, ka a ayopõi sʊɔ di keresolɩtɩ, ka a aniin sʊɔ mɩ di berl, ka a pie forbir sʊɔ di topajɩ, ka a pie sʊɔ di kɩrɩsopɩrase, ka a pie nɩ bʊ̃'yen di jasɩntɩ, ka a pie nɩ ayi sʊɔ mɩ di amɛtesete.

english_verse: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: A dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ pie nɩ ayi ala ɩ naa a kʊsɩsʊ̃n pie nɩ ayi. Kʊsɩbʊ̃'yen lɛ ba maal a jaa. A sɔkpɛ̃ɛ na be a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã pɔ sɛlma lɛ wʊ jaa ka wʊ chaar chãana kaar.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
translation_verse: N ba nyɛ puorfʊ yir be a tẽe pɔ ɛ, bojũu a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ nɩ a Pɛraabile lɛ ɩ a puorfʊ yir.

english_verse: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
translation_verse: A tẽe nyã ba bʊɔrɔ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, bɩɩ ŋmɛraa chãa ɛ, bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr lɛ ɩ a chãa, ka a Pɛraabile ɩ a wʊ fãtɩn.

english_verse: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
translation_verse: A chãa nyã pɔ lɛ a paal bʊrsɩ nɩbɛ na yʊɔrɔ, ka a wɛr nãmɩne mɩ waan a ba nãbomo a be.

english_verse: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
translation_verse: A wʊ dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ ba be na wa pɔɔ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ɛ, an ɩ tãsɔɔ ba lɛ be be go ɛ.

english_verse: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
translation_verse: A paal bʊrsɩ nɩbɛ nãbomo nɩ a ba tɩɩr, ban waan naa be.

english_verse: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_verse: Bʊ̃dɛɛr kʊ̃ kpɛ be ɛ, bɩɩ bala na tʊ̃nɔ̃ vĩ tʊ̃mɔ̃, bɩɩ nɩbɛlbɛ, tɩchɛ a bala tɛɛ yuor na sɛb a Pɛraabile nyɛvʊr gãn pɔ.

english_verse: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
translation_verse: Ka a malaka pãa wiil ma a nyɛvʊr põn kʊ̃ɔ ka wʊ ɩ jaa wel wel ka a joro yire a Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Pɛraabile nãa dakɔɔ par,

english_verse: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
translation_verse: naa joro a tẽe sɔr kpɛ̃ɛ sãlsɔɔ. Nyɛvʊr tɩɛ ara naa a põn lõbobo, ka a jaa ma wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃bʊrsɩ pie nɩ ayi yuon jaa, ka a wiil ɩka a ma wɔ̃ naa ŋmɛraa jaa gbɛbʊ̃'yen. A vaɩ lɛ ban sãan a paalsɩ a jaa nɩbɛ baal.

english_verse: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
translation_verse: Nãaŋmɩn pɔ'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ba lɛ be bon jaa pɔ go ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Pɛraabile nãa dakɔɔ na ben a tẽe pɔ, ka a wʊ gbãgbaar tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ wʊ.

english_verse: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
translation_verse: Ban nyɛn a wʊ nie, ka a wʊ yuor be a ba gber pɔ.

english_verse: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
translation_verse: Tãsɔɔ kʊ̃ lɛ be be go ɛ. Baa lɛ bʊɔrɔ fãtɩn chãa ɛ, bɩɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ chãa ɛ. Bojũu a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ na chaar ba naa a wʊ chãa, na ba di nãa bɩbir jaa.

english_verse: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
translation_verse: Ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Baal ba be a 'yɛrbie anyãna pɔ ɛ, a ɩn yelmãɛ. A Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ma kʊ a wʊ Sɩɛ a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ tʊ̃ naa a wʊ malaka ɩka wʊ wa wiil a wʊ gbãgbaa yele ala na kʊ̃ yaar tɩ ɩ ɛ.”

english_verse: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
translation_verse: “Nyɛ, n kʊ̃ yaar tɩ wa ɛ! Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na sɔɔ a 'yɛrbie anyãna a jaa na sɛb a gãn nyã pɔ.”

english_verse: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
translation_verse: Maa Jɔɔn lɛ wõ tɩ nyɛ a anyãna. Ɩn wa wõ tɩ nyɛ a lɛ baar, n gãn kpa tẽe ɩka n puor a malaka, 'lɔ lɛ wile ma a anyãna.

english_verse: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Ta maal a lɛ ɛ! N mɩ ɩ naa a gbãgbaa nɩtãa a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ bala jaa na turo a gãn nyã 'yɛr. Puor Nãaŋmɩn!”

english_verse: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Ka a malaka pãa 'yɛr kʊ ma, “Ta sɔɔl a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ 'yɛr nyãna a gãn nyãna pɔ bar ɛ, bojũu a sɔ̃ɔ gbʊr naa.

english_verse: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
translation_verse: Vɛ̃ ka a 'lɔ na ɩ nɩdɛɛr, wʊ chɛnɛ ɩrɛ a dɛɛr. Vɛ̃ ka a 'lɔ mɩ na maala yeldɛbɛ, ka wʊ chɛnɛ maala a yeldɛbɛ. Vɛ̃ ka a 'lɔ na tor, wʊ chɛnɛ ɩ a nɩtor. 'Lɔ mɩ na ɩ chɛchɛ, wʊ chɛnɛ ɩ a chɛchɛ.”

english_verse: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
translation_verse: “Nyɛ, n kʊ̃ yaar tɩ wa ɛ! N tara na a n yar na kʊ nɩrɛ jaa lɛ wʊn tʊ̃.

english_verse: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
translation_verse: Maa lɛ a Pielfʊ nɩ a Baaraa, a nidɔ̃ɔn nɩ a puor sʊɔ.

english_verse: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
translation_verse: “Pʊpɛl dẽme lɛ a bala na pɛɛ a ba fuusɩ. Ban tara na sɔr di a nyɛvʊr tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃, na ba tu a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ kpɛ a tẽe pɔ.

english_verse: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
translation_verse: Tɩchɛ baar, nɩ a sʊɔbɔsɩ, nɩ a sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ, nɩ a nɩkʊrbɔ, nɩ a tɩpuorbo, nɩ a bala jaa na nʊ̃ jir ŋmaafʊ, ban ara na a yõo.

english_verse: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
translation_verse: “Maa Yesu lɛ tʊ̃ a n malaka nɩ a dãasɩɛ nyãna a yɩ sɛ̃ kʊ a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ. Maa lɛ ɩ a David nyɩɩr nɩ a wʊ yɔ̃ɔ, nɩ a bivɩɛ ŋmɛrbir na chaar.”

english_verse: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
translation_verse: Ka a Sɩɛ nɩ a pɔɔ paalaa 'yɛr, “Wa!” Ka a 'lɔ mɩ na wõ naa 'yɛr, “Wa!” Nɩrɛ jaa kɔ̃nyũur na kpɛ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ wa, nɩrɛ jaa na bʊɔrɔ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ wa de a nyɛvʊr kʊ̃ɔ kʊfʊ nyũ pɔrɔ.

english_verse: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
translation_verse: N kpãan ãsʊɔ jaa na wõnõ a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ 'yɛr a gãn nyãna pɔ. Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wa den bon dɔɔl a 'yɛr nyã pɔ, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na dɔɔl wʊ naa a wõm ala a gãn nyã na 'yɛr a ba 'yɛr.

english_verse: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
translation_verse: Ka ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ wa ir bon bar a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ 'yɛr a gãn nyã pɔ bar, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na mɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa a nyɛvʊr tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃ nɩ a tẽchɛchɛ pɔ kpɛfʊ a gãn nyã na 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr.

english_verse: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
translation_verse: A 'lɔ na di a anyãna dãasɩɛ 'yɛr naa, “Ɔ̃ɔ, n kʊ̃ yaar tɩ wa ɛ.” A ɩ a lɛ. Wa sɩ Soro Yesu.

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_verse: A sɩ Soro Yesu wãɛfʊ wʊ be a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ sɛ̃. A ɩ a lɛ.
